articles.title: Your Backtest Left Out the Two Costs That Kill It
articles.content: The backtest showed 240% a year. Live, the same bot lost money for four months before I shut it off. Nothing in the code had changed. The market hadn't turned. The strategy was the same one that looked flawless on a chart. The gap was two numbers I'd rounded down to zero: slippage and fees. Here's what that actually means. A backtest fills your order at the exact price you asked for, instantly, every time. Live markets don't work like that. Slippage is the difference between the price you expected and the price you actually got, and it exists because the order book only has so much liquidity sitting at any given level. On major crypto pairs it averages somewhere between 0.05% and 0.30% per trade. During a news spike it can blow past 1%. Fees stack on top вҖ” a taker fill on most exchanges runs around 0.10% unless you're a high-volume account. Neither number sounds like much. That's the trap. Run a strategy that trades often and those small costs compound into something that eats your edge alive. When people finally add realistic assumptions, reported returns tend to drop 30 to 50%. Those lower numbers aren't pessimism. They're just the honest version of the same test. A study of 1,243 trading-forum posts found that 57% of backtest-to-live failures traced back to one thing: slippage that was modeled badly or not at all. I learned this the expensive way. My bot was scalping вҖ” dozens of trades a day, each grabbing a tiny move. On paper the math was gorgeous. In reality, every entry cost me a fraction of a percent I hadn't accounted for, and every exit did the same. The profit per trade was so thin that a 0.2% round-trip cost turned a winner into a scratch and a small loser into a real one. The strategy wasn't broken. My accounting was. Most traders get this wrong in a specific way: they assume the cost is small because each individual number is small. But cost per trade times number of trades is the figure that matters, and a high-frequency strategy multiplies your exposure to it. A system that trades twice a month barely notices slippage. A system that trades twenty times a day lives and dies by it. Same 0.2% cost, wildly different outcomes. The other mistake is testing on liquid conditions and trading in thin ones. Backtests average everything out. They don't know that your bot loves to fire right when volatility spikes and the order book empties вҖ” exactly when slippage is worst. So the live fills land in the fat part of the cost distribution while the backtest quietly assumed the median. You're not comparing the same thing. Here's the practical fix, and it costs you nothing but a little humility. Before you trust any backtest, add a pessimistic cost assumption and run it again. Use the taker fee for your exchange plus at least 0.1% to 0.2% of slippage per side, more if your strategy trades in fast conditions. If the edge survives that haircut, you might have something real. If it evaporates, you never had an edge вҖ” you had a rounding error that looked like one. Better to find that out in a spreadsheet than in your account. The strategies that hold up under honest cost modeling are almost always the ones that trade less and hold longer. That's not a coincidence. Fewer trades means fewer times you pay the toll, so the edge per trade only has to clear the cost once in a while instead of every hour

Щ„ШәШІШҙ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ…ШІШҜЩҮШ§ШҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЩҮ ШҜШұ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ ЩҒШұЪ©Ш§ЩҶШі ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ШҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШіШұШ№ШӘ ШіЩҲШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙ ШҙШҜЩҮ ШұШ§ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЫіЫ° ШӘШ§ ЫөЫ° ШҜШұШөШҜ Ш§ШІ ШЁШ§ШІШҜЩҮ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ Ш§ЩҶШӘШёШ§Шұ ШұШ§ ШӯШ°ЩҒ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Backtests often show impressive returns, but live trading can result in losses. Slippage and fees, especially in high-frequency trading strategies, can quickly erode reported profits, leading to a 30-50% reduction in expected returns.
insightsFa: Щ„ШәШІШҙ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ…ШІШҜЩҮШ§ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШәЫҢШұЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШёШұ ШЁШұШіЩҶШҜШҢ Ш§Щ…Ш§ ШҜШұ Щ…Ш¬Щ…ЩҲШ№ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ.
ШӘЩӮШұЫҢШЁШ§ 57% Ш§ШІ ЩҶШ§Ъ©Ш§Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШўШІЩ…ЩҲЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІЪҜШҙШӘЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢШіШ§ШІЫҢ ЩҶШ§ШҜШұШіШӘ Щ„ШәШІШҙ Ш§ШіШӘ.

ШҜШұ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш№Ш§Щ…Щ„Ш§ШӘ ШЁШ§ ЩҒШұЪ©Ш§ЩҶШі ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ШҢ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶЪ© ШЁЩҮ ШіШұШ№ШӘ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШіЩҲШҜШўЩҲШұЫҢ ШұШ§ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ.

ШўШІЩ…ЩҲЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІЪҜШҙШӘЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҜШұ ШҙШұШ§ЫҢШ· ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ Ш§Ш¬ШұШ§ ЩҶЩ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜШҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШӘШөЩҲШұШ§ШӘ ЩҶШ§ШҜШұШіШӘ Ш§ШІ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұШӘ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
ШӘШҙШ®ЫҢШө ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ЩҮШұ Щ…Ш№Ш§Щ…Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЩҮ ШҜШұ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ъ©ШұШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШұЪ© ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§Шұ Ш§ШҙШ§ШұЩҮ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ЩҲ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ЪҶЩҮ Ш·ЩҲШұ Ш§ШҙШӘШЁШ§ЩҮШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁШұШўЩҲШұШҜ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШӘШ§ЫҢШ¬ ЩҶШ§Ш§Щ…ЫҢШҜЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЩҮ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ. ШҜШұЪ© ШЁЩҮШӘШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш№Ш§Щ…Щ„ЩҮвҖҢЪҜШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұЫҢ Ш¶ШұЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Trump Media pitched $100,000 monthly fee for fast feed of presidentвҖҷs posts
articles.content: Family company has quoted the six-figure sum for high-speed access to Truth Social platform
articles.fingerprint: 000 100 fast fee feed media monthly pitched posts president trump
articles.titleFa: ШӘШұШ§Щ…Щҫ Щ…ШҜЫҢШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШіШұЫҢШ№ ШЁЩҮ ЩҫШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШҰЫҢШівҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұ ЫұЫ°Ы°,Ы°Ы°Ы° ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ Щ…Ш§ЩҮШ§ЩҶЩҮ ЩҫЫҢШҙЩҶЩҮШ§ШҜ ШҜШ§ШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШӘШұШ§Щ…Щҫ Щ…ШҜЫҢШ§ШҢ ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘ Щ…ШЁЩ„Шә ЫұЫ°Ы°,Ы°Ы°Ы° ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШіШұЫҢШ№ ШЁЩҮ ЩҫШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШҰЫҢШівҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұ ШҜШұ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… Truth Social Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫЫҢШҙЩҶЩҮШ§ШҜ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ъ©ШіШЁ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШіШұЫҢШ№вҖҢШӘШұ Ш§ШІ ЩҫШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШҰЫҢШівҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШіШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ЩҲ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұЫҢЩҶ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Trump Media has quoted a monthly fee of $100,000 for fast access to the President's posts on the Truth Social platform. This proposal is aimed at providing quicker insights into the President's communications for media companies and others.
insightsFa: ЩҫЫҢШҙЩҶЩҮШ§ШҜ ШӘШұШ§Щ…Щҫ Щ…ШҜЫҢШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШұШіШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШіШұЫҢШ№ ШЁЩҮ ЩҫШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШҰЫҢШівҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШЁЩ„Шә ЫұЫ°Ы°,Ы°Ы°Ы° ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ъ©ШіШЁ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШіШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… Truth Social ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШЁШ№ ШҜШұШўЩ…ШҜЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩ„Ш§ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШұШ§Щ…Щҫ Щ…ШҜЫҢШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШҜШұШўЩ…ШҜ Ш§ШІ Ш·ШұЫҢЩӮ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШӯШӘЩҲШ§ЫҢ ШұШіШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШұШҰЫҢШівҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұ ЩҶШӯЩҲЩҮ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ Ш§ШұШӘШЁШ§Ш·Ш§ШӘ ШҜШұ ЩҒШ¶Ш§ЫҢ ШіЫҢШ§ШіЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.

articles.title: MicrosoftвҖҷs вҖҳProject PerceptionвҖҷ Could Challenge AnthropicвҖҷs Mythos in AI Security
articles.content: Microsoft is reportedly developing Project Perception, a lower-cost AI security tool that would use multiple models to identify enterprise vulnerabilities. The post MicrosoftвҖҷs вҖҳProject PerceptionвҖҷ Could Challenge AnthropicвҖҷs Mythos in AI Security appeared first on TechRepublic .
articles.author: David Curry
articles.fingerprint: anthropic challenge could microsoft mythos perception project security
articles.titleFa: ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ В«ЩҫШұШіЩҫЪ©ШӘЫҢЩҲВ» Щ…Ш§ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲШіШ§ЩҒШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ЪҶШ§Щ„ШҙЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ В«Щ…ЫҢШӘЩҲШіВ» ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШҜШұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Щ…Ш§ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲШіШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШ§Щ… В«ЩҫШұЩҫЪ©ШӘЫҢЩҲВ» Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ЫҢЪ© Ш§ШЁШІШ§Шұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШ§ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҫШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШЁЩҲШҜ ЩҲ Ш§ШІ ЪҶЩҶШҜЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁЩҮ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁШ§ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ… Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ В«Щ…ЫҢШӘЩҲШіВ» ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Microsoft is reportedly developing Project Perception, an AI security tool with a lower cost that will utilize multiple models to identify enterprise vulnerabilities. This project could potentially challenge Anthropic's Mythos security system.
insightsFa: ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ В«ЩҫШұЩҫЪ©ШӘЫҢЩҲВ» ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЫҢЪ© Ш§ШЁШІШ§Шұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШ§ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҫШ§ЫҢЫҢЩҶ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЫҢШ§ШЁШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШЁШІШ§Шұ Ш§ШІ ЪҶЩҶШҜЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ.
ЪҶШ§Щ„Шҙ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ В«Щ…ЫҢШӘЩҲШіВ» ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ШЁ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШіШ§ЫҢШЁШұЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШЁШІШ§Шұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲШіШ§ЩҒШӘ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШіШ§ШҰЩ„ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ В«ЩҫШұЩҫЪ©ШӘЫҢЩҲВ» Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШұШ§ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜЩҮШҜ ЩҲ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ ШіШ§ЫҢШұ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢЪҜШұШ§ЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШіШҰЩ„ЩҮШҢ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШіШ§ЫҢШЁШұЫҢ ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩ…ШұШ§ЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘ.

articles.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
articles.content: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process restarts. OpenSSL shipped the HollowByte fix in June with no CVE, no advisory, and no changelog entry pointing at it. Okta's Red Team, which reported the denial-of-service bug and named it, published the
articles.author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)
articles.fingerprint: byte could flaw freeze hollowbyte memory openssl requests server tls
articles.titleFa: Ш§ШҙЪ©Ш§Щ„ HollowByte ШҜШұ OpenSSL Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШӯШ§ЩҒШёЩҮ ШіШұЩҲШұ ШұШ§ ШЁШ§ ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЫұЫұ ШЁШ§ЫҢШӘЫҢ ЫҢШ® ШЁШІЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ЫҢЪ© ШіШұЩҲШұ OpenSSL Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶШҙШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜШҢ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШұШіШ§Щ„ ЫҢЪ© ЩҫЫҢШ§Щ… ЫұЫұ ШЁШ§ЫҢШӘЫҢ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШӘШ§ ЫұЫіЫұ Ъ©ЫҢЩ„ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢШӘ ШӯШ§ЩҒШёЩҮ ШұШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҫЫҢШ§Щ…ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҮШұЪҜШІ ЩҶЩ…ЫҢвҖҢШұШіШҜШҢ Ш§Ш®ШӘШөШ§Шө ШҜЩҮШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӯШ§ЩҒШёЩҮ ШӘШ§ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҒШұШўЫҢЩҶШҜ ШҜЩҲШЁШ§ШұЩҮ ШұШ§ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІЫҢ ЩҶШҙЩҲШҜШҢ ШўШІШ§ШҜ ЩҶШ®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҙШҜ. ШӘЫҢЩ… ЩӮШұЩ…ШІ Okta Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШҙЪ©Ш§Щ„ ШұШ§ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« ШіШұЩҲЫҢШівҖҢШҜЩҮЫҢ ЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҶШ§ШіШЁ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜШҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҲ ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: An unpatched OpenSSL server can allocate up to 131 KB of memory for an 11-byte message that never arrives. This memory will remain allocated until the process restarts. Okta's Red Team reported this denial-of-service bug, highlighting its implications.
insightsFa: ЫҢЪ© ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘ ЫұЫұ ШЁШ§ЫҢШӘЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮШҜШұ ШұЩҒШӘЩҶ ЫұЫіЫұ Ъ©ЫҢЩ„ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢШӘ ШӯШ§ЩҒШёЩҮ ШҜШұ ШіШұЩҲШұ OpenSSL Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ш§Ш®ШӘЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ШіШұЩҲШұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
ШӘЫҢЩ… ЩӮШұЩ…ШІ Okta Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШҙЪ©Ш§Щ„ ШұШ§ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҶШ§Щ…ЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁШ§ЪҜ ШӘШ§ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҲ Ш§ШөЩ„Ш§Шӯ ШўЩҶ ШҜШұ ШҜШіШӘШұШі ЩҶЫҢШіШӘ.
Ш№ШҜЩ… ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ CVE ЩҲ Щ…ШіШӘЩҶШҜШ§ШӘ Ш§ШұШӘШЁШ§Ш·ЫҢ ШҜШұ Ш§ШұШӘШЁШ§Ш· ШЁШ§ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ШҢ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ЫҢЪ© ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШҜШұ OpenSSL Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШұ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘ Ш§Ш·Щ…ЫҢЩҶШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШіШұЩҲШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶШӘШұЩҶШӘЫҢ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ. ЩҒЩӮШҜШ§ЩҶ Щ…ШіШӘЩҶШҜШ§ШӘ ШұШіЩ…ЫҢ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҶЫҢШІ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҶШёШ§ШұШӘ ЩҲ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЪҜЩҲЩҶЩҮ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„Ш§ШӘ ШұШ§ ШҜШҙЩҲШ§ШұШӘШұ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: Inference cloud operator General Compute raises $400M in debt financing
articles.content: Artificial intelligence infrastructure startup General Compute Inc. today announced that it has secured $400 million in debt financing. Upper90, the investment firm that is underwriting the round, will initially provide the company with $100 million. General Compute plans to draw down additional funds as customer demand increases. Unlike most AI infrastructure providers, General Compute is […] The post Inference cloud operator General Compute raises $400M in debt financing appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
articles.author: Maria Deutscher

articles.titleFa: Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§Щҫ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ General Compute Щ…ШЁЩ„Шә ЫҙЫ°Ы° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ШЁШҜЩҮЫҢ Ш¬Ш°ШЁ Ъ©ШұШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§Щҫ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ General Compute Ш§Ш№Щ„Ш§Щ… Ъ©ШұШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШіШӘЩҮ ЫҙЫ°Ы° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ШЁШҜЩҮЫҢ Ш¬Ш°ШЁ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ШӘЩҲШіШ· ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ Upper90 Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҜШұ Ш§ШЁШӘШҜШ§ ЫұЫ°Ы° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ. General Compute ЩӮШөШҜ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШЁШ§ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШӘЩӮШ§Ш¶Ш§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶШҢ Ш§ШІ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШЁШ№ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Artificial intelligence infrastructure startup General Compute has raised $400 million in debt financing. The initial funding of $100 million is provided by investment firm Upper90, with plans to draw additional funds as customer demand increases.
insightsFa: General Compute ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЫҢЪ© Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§Щҫ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш¬Ш°ШЁ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ЫҢЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШҜШұ ЩҫШ§ШіШ® ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩӮШ§Ш¶Ш§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.
Upper90 ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§Шұ Ш§ШөЩ„ЫҢШҢ ЫұЫ°Ы° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ ШұШ§ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢШӘ General Compute Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· ШЁЩҮ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮШҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜШЁШ®Шҙ ШұШҙШҜ ЩҲ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§Щҫ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШұШҙШҜ ЩҲ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢЫҢ Ш№Щ„Ш§ЩӮЩҮ ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: MetaвҖҷs AI bots drain publisher pockets with 9 billion Q2 2026 requests at host expense while returning ZERO traffic вҖ” as ChatGPT claims 88% of AI referrals
articles.content: DataDome analysis claims agentic traffic has surged by 45% in Q2 2026 Meta AI bots have grown over 163% on the previous quarter The analysis was conducted by bot management and agent control platform DataDome If you run a website, every crawl costs bandwidth, resources, logging, and creates CDN transactions, and while search engine crawlers offered the promise of sending visitors, AI bots do not. Analysis in a report from cybersecurity firm DataDome has shown that while bots from Meta AI have increased activity, theyвҖҷre not delivering any significant returns to websites. Conversely, ChatGPT crawlers have reduced in traffic, but are sending more referrals. AI agent traffic is growing While Meta AI is usually considered to be the вҖңchatbot within FacebookвҖқ it seems that it is becoming something more вҖ“ and the emergence of the Meta-WebIndexer bot (which grew 163% on Q1) suggests that Meta may be indexing a library of websites, in much the same way Google has done for the past few decades. The growth of Meta AI as an active crawler is only part of the story, as is ChatGPTвҖҷs comparative efficiency. The OpenAI tool seems to know enough about websites, so can provide the answers it already вҖңknows.вҖқ Conversely, Meta AIвҖҷs activity indexing the web seems to explain its heavy impact in Q2 2026. But also emerging is the Model Context Protocol (MCP) signal, which connects AI agents with external tools, and differs from standard crawler traffic. вҖңQ2 showed us that the ground is shifting faster than most organizations realize. Meta now dominates AI traffic on our network, MCP traffic has emerged as a real signal, and ChatGPT is driving more referral value with fewer crawls," noted JГ©rГҙme Segura, VP of Threat Research at DataDome. The differences in the way the AI agents are interacting with websites вҖ“ some behaving like users, others scraping content вҖ“ means that organizations need to act accordingly. вҖңWhat the data makes clear is that not all agents are created equal. The organizations building policy around these distinctions are the ones gaining an edge, and that's exactly why agent trust adoption is accelerating." Unfortunately, MCPвҖҷs existence and growth into a significant, measurable quantity, means that it should also be treated as part of an organizationвҖҷs attack surface. Allocating resources Given the origins of the report, there is naturally a cybersecurity aspect to this. While ransomware, malware, and phishing are not going anywhere, autonomous software on the web needs addressing in a different way. Those вҖңorganizations building policyвҖқ that Segura mentions might, for example, give full crawl access to Google, allow ChatGPT to retrieve results, but rate-limit Meta AI based on its poor return. Meanwhile, unknown agents вҖ“ perhaps cybersecurity threats вҖ“ would require additional verification or be blocked entirely.
articles.author: Christian Cawley

articles.titleFa: ШұШҙШҜ ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ© ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ§ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ЩҮЫҢЪҶ ШЁШ§ШІЪҜШҙШӘЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШіЩҲЫҢ ЩҶШ§ШҙШұШ§ЩҶ
articles.summaryFa: ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ§ ШҜШұ ШіЩҮвҖҢЩ…Ш§ЩҮЩҮ ШҜЩҲЩ… ЫІЫ°ЫІЫ¶ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЫҢШІШ§ЩҶ ЫұЫ¶Ыі ШҜШұШөШҜ ШұШҙШҜ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШҢ Ш§Щ…Ш§ ЩҮЫҢЪҶ ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШіШ§ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ШІЩҶЪҜШұШҜШ§ЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ. ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ§ ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜШҢ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ChatGPT ШЁШ§ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ©ШҢ Ш§ШұШ¬Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШұШ§ Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘШӯЩҲЩ„ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ ШЁШ§ ШіШ§ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: A recent analysis reveals that Meta's AI bots have surged by 163% in Q2 2026, yet they return no traffic to websites. While Meta bots are becoming more active, ChatGPT crawlers are sending more referrals despite having reduced traffic. This indicates a need for organizations to adapt their strategies as interactions with websites evolve.
insightsFa: ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ§ ЫұЫ¶Ыі ШҜШұШөШҜ ШұШҙШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ© ЩҶШ§ШҙЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ§ ЩҮЫҢЪҶ Ш§ШұШ¬Ш§Ш№ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШіШ§ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҶШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ChatGPT ШЁШ§ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ©ШҢ Ш§ШұШ¬Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШұШ§ Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ.
Ш№ШұШ¶ЩҮ Щ…ШҜЩ„ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Context Protocol (MCP) Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§ШұШӘШЁШ§Ш· ШЁЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЩҲ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®Ш§ШұШ¬ЫҢ ШұШ§ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШіШ§ШІШҜ.
ШӘЩҒШ§ЩҲШӘ ШҜШұ ЩҶШӯЩҲЩҮ ШұЩҒШӘШ§Шұ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШ§ ШіШ§ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШұШ§ Ш¶ШұЩҲШұЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШіШ§ШІШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҶШ§ШҙШұШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶЪ©ЩҮ ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШіШ§ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ШІЪҜШұШҜШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ. ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶШҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШіШҰЩ„ЩҮ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜШіШ§ШІЫҢ ШұЩҲШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ ШЁШ§ ЩҲШЁвҖҢШіШ§ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ Щ…Ш·ШұШӯ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҶШұЩ…вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§ШұЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„ШӘ Ъ©Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩҒШұЩҶЫҢШ§ ШөЩҲШұШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұШҜ.

Щ…ШұЪ©ШІ ШӘШ§ШІЩҮ ШӘШЈШіЫҢШі ШҜШұ ЩҒШұЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҶШӘ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШІШҜЫҢЪ©вҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ЩҶЩӮШ·ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШіЩ„Ш§ ШҜШұ Ъ©Ш§Щ„ЫҢЩҒШұЩҶЫҢШ§ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҒШӘШӘШ§Шӯ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ ШӘШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЩҲЩ„ЩҲЪҳЫҢ ШұШЁШ§ШӘЫҢЪ© Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҜ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁШ§ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҶШІШҜЫҢЪ©вҖҢШӘШұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Щ…ЩҲЩӮШӘШ§ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШіШӘ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ ШіШЁЩӮШӘ ШЁЪҜЫҢШұШҜ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШ§Ш§ШұШІШҙвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶ ШӘШЁШҜЫҢЩ„ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ ЩҲШ¶Ш№ЫҢШӘ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢЫҢ ШӘШ§ШЁвҖҢШўЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ЩҶЩҲШіШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· ШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЩҶЫҢЩ…ЩҮвҖҢШұШіШ§ЩҶШ§ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Щ…ЩҶЩҒЫҢ ЪҜШ°Ш§ШҙШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Щ…ЩҲЩҒЩӮЫҢШӘ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩӮЩҲЫҢШӘ Щ…ЩҲЩӮШ№ЫҢШӘ ШўЩҶ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЩҲЩ„ЩҲЪҳЫҢ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: Shark’s versatile ChillPill cooling system is back to its best price
articles.content: You can clip the versatile cooling gadget, wear it, and use it as a desk fan. | Image: Shark Portable fans are one of the easiest ways to stay cool during the summer, and you don’t have to spend much to find a decent one . If you’re looking for something more versatile, though, Shark’s ChillPill Personal Fan and Cooling System combines a rechargeable fan with a dry-touch evaporative mister in a design that can be worn, clipped, or used on a tabletop. Right now, it’s on sale for $99.99 ($30 off) at Amazon , matching its all-time low. If you’re a Costco member, you can get it for the same price with a free clip, crossbody strap, and InstaChill Cooling Plate which you can press against your neck or wrists for quick relief. Shark ChillPill Personal Fan and Cooling System Where to Buy: $129.99 $99.99 at Amazon $129.99 $99.99 at Costco Unlike a standard handheld fan, the two-in-one device is designed so that you can wear it or twist it into tabletop mode, making it useful whether you’re commuting, waiting in line at a theme park, working out, or just trying to stay cool at your desk. You can even clip it to a purse or stroller, so it’s always within easy reach while you’re on the go. It also offers 10 fan speeds and matching noise levels, allowing you to choose anything from a quiet breeze for indoor use to stronger cooling outdoors. When a fan alone isn’t enough, you can switch on the dry-touch evaporative mist, which cools your skin without leaving your clothes soaked. You can also choose between continuous and interval misting depending on how long you want the water to last. Plus, Shark says the ChillPill can run for up to 11 hours on its lowest setting, which should be enough to get through a full day at a festival or while traveling. When it’s time to recharge, the device uses USB-C, so you can likely use the same cable you’re already carrying for your phone, tablet, or laptop.

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ Ы№Ы№.Ы№Ы№ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ ШўЩ…Ш§ШІЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ Ш§ШіШӘШҢ ЩҲ ШҜШұ ШөЩҲШұШӘ Ш№Ш¶ЩҲЫҢШӘ ШҜШұ CostcoШҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢШҜ ШўЩҶ ШұШ§ ШЁШ§ Ш§ЩӮЩ„Ш§Щ… Ш§Ш¶Ш§ЩҒЫҢ ШұШ§ЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҶ Ш®ШұЫҢШҜШ§ШұЫҢ Ъ©ЩҶЫҢШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Shark's new ChillPill is a versatile personal fan and cooling system that can be used as a tabletop fan or worn as a portable device. It's currently available for $99.99 on Amazon, and Costco members can purchase it with additional free items.
insightsFa: ChillPill ЫҢЪ© ЩҒЩҶ ЩҲ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ… Ш®ЩҶЪ©вҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЩҮ ШҙШ®ШөЫҢ ШЁШ§ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӯЩ…Щ„ Ш§ШіШӘ.

Щ…ШҜШӘ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ Ъ©Ш§ШұЪ©ШұШҜ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮ ШҜШұ Ъ©Щ…ШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШӘЩҶШёЫҢЩ… ШЁЩҮ ЫұЫұ ШіШ§Ш№ШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұШіШҜ.

articles.title: Apple overtakes Nvidia to reclaim the title of worldвҖҷs most valuable company at $4.88 trillion
articles.content: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the worldвҖҷs most valuable company for the first time since April 2025. Apple closed at roughly $4.88 trillion as its shares held steady, while Nvidia fell 3.5% to approximately $4.86 trillion. Nvidia had held the top spot for nearly a year after becoming the first company to surpass […] This story continues at The Next Web
articles.author: Ana Maria Constantin
articles.fingerprint: apple company most nvidia overtakes reclaim title trillion valuable world
articles.titleFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШЁШ§Шұ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ ЩҫЫҢШҙЫҢ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘ ЩҲ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШұШІЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ Ш§ШІ ШўЩҶ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШұЩҲШІ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ ЩҫЫҢШҙЫҢ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘ ЩҲ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҶШ®ШіШӘЫҢЩҶ ШЁШ§Шұ Ш§ШІ ШўЩҲШұЫҢЩ„ ЫІЫ°ЫІЫө ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШұШІШҙ Ыҙ.ЫёЫё ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШұШІЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҶШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШіЩҮШ§Щ… Ш§ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ ШЁШ§ Ш§ЩҒШӘ Ыі.Ыө ШҜШұШөШҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ыҙ.ЫёЫ¶ ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШұШіЫҢШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: On Friday, Apple overtook Nvidia to become the worldвҖҷs most valuable company for the first time since April 2025, closing at approximately $4.88 trillion. Meanwhile, Nvidia's shares fell by 3.5% to around $4.86 trillion.
insightsFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҫШі Ш§ШІ ЫұЫ¶ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ ШҜЩҲШЁШ§ШұЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШұШІЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШіШӘ ШўЩҲШұШҜ.
Ш§ШұШІШҙ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШЁЩҮ Ыҙ.ЫёЫё ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШұШіЫҢШҜ.
Ш§ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢШ§ ШЁШ§ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ Ш§ШұШІШҙ ШіЩҮШ§Щ…ШҢ Ш¬Ш§ЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ Ш§ШІ ШҜШіШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜ.
ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁЫҢЩҶ ШҜЩҲ ШәЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШҜЫҢЩҶШ§Щ…ЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§Ш«ШұЪҜШ°Ш§Шұ ШЁШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШіЩҮШ§Щ… ЩҲ ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Ш§ЩӮШӘШөШ§ШҜЫҢ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘЫҢ ШҜШұ ШөЩҶШ№ШӘ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ ЩҲ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ШўЩҶ ШЁШұ Ш§ЩӮШӘШөШ§ШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш§ЩҶШӘШёШ§ШұЩҮШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜЩҲ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШұШ§ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ ШҜЩҮШҜ.

articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШіЫҢШ§ШіШӘ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Ш§ШҙШӘШұШ§Ъ©вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ ЩҲ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШҙШӘШұШ§Ъ©ЫҢ ШұШ§ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜЩҮШҜ.

articles.title: AAPL capitalization squeaks past NVDA, Apple becomes world's most valuable company
articles.content: Apple stock ended trading on Friday as the world's most valuable publicly traded company, overtaking Nvidia for the first time since April 2025 after a sustained recovery for the iPhone maker met a sharp selloff in chip stocks. Apple once again crowned Most Valuable Company Apple shares closed at $333.74, leaving the company with a market capitalization of approximately $4.88 CAP trillion. Nvidia ended down about 3.5% on the day, with a value of about $4.86 trillion. The distinction is largely symbolic, and a lead this narrow could disappear during the next trading session, if not in after-hours trading over the weekend. Still, Apple's return to the top caps a striking reversal from the tariff, China, and artificial intelligence concerns that weighed on its shares the last time it held the position. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums
articles.author: Andrew Orr <andreworr@appleinsider.com>

articles.summaryEn: On Friday, Apple's stock ended trading as the world's most valuable publicly traded company, surpassing Nvidia for the first time since April 2025. With shares closing at $333.74, Apple's market capitalization stood at approximately $4.88 trillion, while Nvidia saw a 3.5% decline, resulting in a valuation of about $4.86 trillion.

articles.title: IPhone Hacking Firm Sues Ex-Worker Over Alleged Theft of Secrets
articles.content: Magnet Forensics Inc., a Canadian cybersecurity firm, accused a former contractor of sharing trade secrets about a previously unknown flaw used to hack iPhones with a rival firm.
articles.author: Patrick Howell O'Neill

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЫҢЪ© ШұЩӮЫҢШЁ Щ…ЩҶШӘЩӮЩ„ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: The Samsung S95H is one of the best OLED TVs I've ever tested, even if it does feature a controversial design
articles.content: Samsung S95H review: Two minute review Sitting at the top of SamsungвҖҷs 2026 TV lineup, the Samsung S95H is a flagship OLED TV that delivers excellent performance, particularly when it comes to picture, and a full set of features both for smart TV and for gaming. This could easily be one of 2026вҖҷs best TVs . The S95H has a full list of features. Its NQ4 AI Gen 3 Processor comes with a stack of AI tools for picture, such as AI Motion Enhancer Pro and AI 4K Upscaling Pro, AI tools for sound such as Adaptive Sound Pro, and other AI tools for content recommendation. All of these fall under the Samsung Vision AI banner. Picture quality on the S95H is superb. It delivers the highest brightness to date from a flagship OLED TV, along with vibrant, accurate colors, crisp textures and powerful contrast. Its anti-reflection screen is effective at eliminating reflections and while its Filmmaker Mode is a little dim in bright conditions, its Movie mode is a great brighter alternative. Motion handling is mostly smooth and natural for both movies and sport, and while there are some hints of black crush in certain movies I tested, the S95HвҖҷs picture is still up there with the best OLED TVs . The Samsung S95H delivers solid overall built-in sound. Its accuracy and precision are its strengths, with an excellent connection between the action on screen and the sound. The bass is punchy, thereвҖҷs some nice impact and the soundstage can be wide. Still, this level of picture quality deserves a fuller sound, so IвҖҷd recommend one of the best soundbars or speakers. Design is arguably the S95HвҖҷs most controversial topic this year. A new silver frame around the TV, called the FloatLayer Design, has been added and I personally found the frame to be distracting. While meant for wall-mounting, the extra width may cause space issues for some people and the handsome central pedestal stand of its predecessor has been replaced by two plastic feet. The One Connect Box has been removed, with ports returning to the TV itself вҖ” but you can connect Samsung Wireless One Connect Box to add more HDMI ports. The S95H is a phenomenal gaming TV. Carrying four HDMI 2.1 ports that support 4K 165Hz, full VRR, HDR10+ gaming and ALLM, and boasting an ultra-low 9.5ms input lag at 60Hz, performance is smooth and the picture always looks great. Easily one of 2026вҖҷs best gaming TVs . One UI Tizen is the S95HвҖҷs smart TV platform. It supports all major streaming apps, delivers pretty smooth performance and is easy to navigate. While there are some quirks that I found frustrating (namely auto-playing Samsung TV Plus), itвҖҷs still a good smart TV platform. Its Vision AI companion can be useful for those who enjoy AI, and while it doesnвҖҷt quite measure up to LGвҖҷs webOS, itвҖҷs still solid. The 65-inch S95H I tested costs $3,399 / AU$5,299, putting it at the premium end of the TV market. While this is a high price, the S95H delivers features and performance to justify the price and itвҖҷs competitively priced with rivals, with the LG G6 65-inch costing $3,299 / AU$4,999. I expect prices for the S95H will drop shortly, with some deals already active in Australia at the time of writing. Samsung S95H review: Prices & release dates (Image credit: Future) Release date: June 2026 55-inch price: $2,299 / AU$3,999 65-inch price: $3,299 / AU$5,299 77-inch price: $4,299 / AU$7,999 83-inch price: $6,299 / AU$9,999 The Samsung S95H is the flagship OLED in SamsungвҖҷs 2026 TV lineup. It sits above the mid-range Samsung S90H and budget Samsung S85H. The 65-inch model I tested launched in June 2026 at $3,299 / AU$5,299. In the few weeks since its release, prices have remained the same, though we have seen some price reductions in Australia, particularly in larger-sized models, with up to AU$1,500 off the 83-inch model. The S95HвҖҷs launch prices are cheaper than both its predecessor, the S95F, and the rival G6. However, the G6 has been out for longer, so its prices have dropped over time. Samsung S95H review:вҖҰ
articles.author: James Davidson

articles.title: Inc Ransomware Exploits SonicWall SMA Zero-Days
articles.content: When chained together, the two vulnerabilities allow threat actors to gain root-level capabilities on SonicWall's mobile access appliances.
articles.author: Nate Nelson
articles.fingerprint: days exploits inc ransomware sma sonicwall zero
articles.titleFa: ШіЩҲШЎШ§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ШЁШ§Ш¬вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ Ш§ШІ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШөЩҒШұ ШұЩҲШІ SonicWall SMA
articles.summaryFa: ШҜЩҲ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШҜШұ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ SonicWallШҢ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩ… Щ…ШұШӘШЁШ· ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜШҢ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬Щ…Ш§ЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШұШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШіШ·Шӯ ШұЫҢШҙЩҮ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ЩҫЫҢШҜШ§ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ ШұШ§ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШўЩҲШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: The two vulnerabilities in SonicWall's mobile access appliances, when chained together, allow attackers to gain root-level access. This raises new security concerns for these devices.
insightsFa: ШҜЩҲ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜШұ SonicWall SMA ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬Щ…Ш§ЩҶ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШІЩҮ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШұЫҢШҙЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ.
ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ЫҢШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШіШұШ№ШӘ ШЁШұШ·ШұЩҒ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.
Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ SonicWall ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұШҜ.
Ш§ШӯШӘЫҢШ§Ш· ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ШЁШ§Ш¬вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ Ш¶ШұЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШӯШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШҜШӘ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ШЁ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШәЫҢШұЩ…Ш¬Ш§ШІ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШӯШіШ§Ші ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: 500 Blog Posts To Learn About Cloud Computing
articles.content: Let's learn about Cloud Computing via these 500 free blog posts. They are ordered by HackerNoon reader engagement data. Visit the Learn Repo or LearnRepo.com to find the most read blog posts about any technology. It's raining cloud stories. 1. What is IPFS? - A Beginner's Guide IPFS consists of several innovations in communication protocols and distributed systems that have been combined to produce a file system like no other. 2. Introduction to AWS With Terraform Almost one month ago, I released Practical AWS, a training concerned With the actual use of AWS rather than with theory & ideas. 3. The Linux Writing Contest by Linode and HackerNoon This one is for all the Linux lovers - Linode & HackerNoon are excited to host a Linux Writing Contest! 4. Why (and when) you should use Kubernetes Kubernetes is a powerful container management tool that automates the deployment and management of containers. Kubernetes (k8вҖҷs) is the next big wave in cloud computing and itвҖҷs easy to see why as businesses migrate their infrastructure and architecture to reflect a cloud-native, data-driven era. 5. Is Cloud Gaming Doomed Because of Physics? Cloud gaming is a hot topic today but gamers speak negatively about input latency. I discuss the journey of input latency from cloud servers to users' monitors. 6. Serverless Software Development: Everything You Need to Know What is Serverless Software Development, its benefits, different types of services available, how to build Serverless applications, and some best practices. 7. Building Robust Cloud Infrastructure with Python and Terraform In this practical guide, we will explore hands-on examples of leveraging Python and Terraform to tackle real-world cloud infrastructure challenges. 8. Will Data Centers Ruin Your Neighborhood? People are protesting data center construction near neighborhoods вҖ” are their fears well-founded or overblown? 9. 5 Steps for Dockerizing NestJs With Prisma Dockerize NestJS + Prisma in 5 steps 10. Data Migration from Dell ECS to MinIO Dell ECS clusters allow you to migrate your data to any S3 compatible store. Dell ECS calls this feature вҖңData MovementвҖқ, also called copy-to-cloud. 11. An In-Depth Guide to AWS Lambda Error Handling In this article, we'll be discussing everything you need to know about the basics of AWS Lambda error handling. 12. Managing Stateful Applications in Containerized Environments Learn essential tips for handling stateful applications in container setups. Discover efficient strategies for seamless management in containerized environments 13. Is There An Affordable Alternative To Step Functions? AWS Step Functions is a great service for orchestrating multi-step workflows with complex logic. ItвҖҷs fast to implement, relatively easy to use and just works. The problem is its price. 14. How to Set Up a Free Oracle Cloud VM for Web Development (2025 Guide) Oracle Cloud offers a free VM with 4 vCPUs, 24GB RAM, and 200GB storage (as of 2025). 15. How to Connect Distributed Clients to a Private InfluxDB Database Ockam enables developers to securely connect clients to the InfluxDB database with exposing it to the public internet. 16. How to Pass the Certified Kubernetes Administrator Exam in 7 Days A couple of weeks ago I passed the Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) exam with 7 days of prep. HereвҖҷs how I did it. 17. How to Use AWS S3 with Python 8 Must-Know Tricks to Use S3 More Effectively in Python: In this article, weвҖҷll look at various ways to leverage the power of S3 in Python. 18. 6 Critical Challenges of Productionizing Vector Search Prepare for complexities of deploying vector search in production with insights on indexing, metadata filtering, query language, and vector lifecycle management 19. Kubernetes Secrets and ConfigMaps In this article we will discuss about the applications of Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets. 20. How to Deploy AWS Lambda with Docker Containers You can now build your serverless workloads in a Docker container with AWSвҖҰ

Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№Ш§ШӘ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ AWSШҢ Kubernetes ЩҲ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҶШұЩ…вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ШіШұЩҲШұ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШЁШ№ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШұШ§ШҜЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ЫҢШ§ШҜЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШұШ§ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҙ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ Ш№Щ„Ш§ЩӮЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҶШҜЩҶШҜ ШЁШіЫҢШ§Шұ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШҜЫҢвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
ШЁШұШ®ЫҢ ЩҫШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„Ш§ШӘ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Щ…ШұШ§Ъ©ШІ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ ШЁШұ Щ…ШӯЩ„ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІЩҶШҜ.
ШұШ§ЫҢШ§ЩҶШҙ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШҙЪ©Щ„вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Linus Torvalds To Critics of AI Coding On Linux: 'Fork It. Or Just Walk Away.'
articles.content: Linus Torvalds says the Linux kernel will not ban AI-assisted coding tools, and if anti-AI absolutists have a problem with that, they can "fork it" or "walk away." An anonymous reader quotes a report from Ars Technica: Writing in a lengthy post on the Linux kernel mailing list this week, Torvalds said that "Linux is not one of those anti-AI projects, and if somebody has issues with that, they can do the open-source thing and fork it. Or just walk away." The statement came amid a lengthy thread arguing about the use of Sashiko, an "agentic Linux kernel code review system" that its creators claim can, in tests, independently find 53.6 percent of the bugs that would end up being fixed by human coders in later commits. But the tool can also waste maintainers' time by sending "false positive" reports of bugs that don't exist, at a rate Sashiko's maintainers estimate is "well within [the] 20% range." In discussing whether maintainers should be subjected to a flood of these kinds of automated, AI-powered bug report emails (true or false), one poster cited the Software Freedom Conservancy's recent statement that the open source community "should support, not just tolerate, those who outright reject LLM-gen-AI systems" and that "every FOSS contributor deserves self-determination regarding LLM-gen-AI." In the face of that statement, Torvalds said that he rejects those who demand that their open source projects not accept any LLM-generated code or revisions. "We're not forcing anybody to use [LLM tools], but I will very loudly ignore people who try to argue against other people from using it," Torvalds said. Torvalds said his position on this is a pragmatic one that's "based on technical merit. Not fear of new tools." And when it comes to utility, Torvalds said that "AI is a tool, just like other tools we use. And it's clearly a useful one. It may not have been that 'clearly' even just a year ago, but it's no longer in question today. Anybody who doubts that clearly hasn't actually used it." [...] While Torvalds acknowledged that "AI isn't perfect," he urged detractors to compare the output of these tools to the performance of human code maintainers. "Anybody who points to the problems at AI had better be looking in the mirror and pointing at themselves at the same time," Torvalds wrote. "Because it's not like natural intelligence is always all that great either." Read more of this story at Slashdot.

Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ ШЁШ§ Щ…ШӘШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩ„ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩ…Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁЫҢЩҶ ШҜЩҲ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Щ…ШұШӘШЁШ· ШЁШ§ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩӮШұШ§ШұШҜШ§ШҜ Ш§Ш®ЫҢШұ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіЩҫЫҢШі Ш§ЫҢЪ©Ші ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩӮШҜШұШӘ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁШ§ШӘЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ЩҒЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ШЁШіШІШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁШ§ШӘЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШұЩҲЩҶШҜЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШіШӘ ШўЩҲШұШҜЩҶ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШЁШ№ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁШ§ШӘЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩҫЫҢШҙШұЩҒШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЪҶШҙЩ…вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ ШЁЫҢЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ…ШҜ.

articles.title: AWS Billing Glitch Hits Customers With Billion-Dollar Fees
articles.content: An error with the cloud computing giantвҖҷs billing operation caused some customersвҖҷ monthly bills to rise from a few cents to billions of dollars.
articles.author: Caroline Haskins
articles.fingerprint: aws billing billion customers dollar fees glitch hits
articles.titleFa: Ш§ШҙЪ©Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ AWS Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ ШЁШ§ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮ Ъ©ШұШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ЫҢЪ© Ш§ШҙЪ©Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ… ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁ AWS ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШәЫҢШұШ№Ш§ШҜЫҢ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁ ШЁШұШ®ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШІ ЪҶЩҶШҜ ШіЩҶШӘ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜЩҮШ§ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҙШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ ШҜШұ Ш№Щ…Щ„ЫҢШ§ШӘ ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШЁШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш§ШӘЩҒШ§ЩӮ Ш§ЩҒШӘШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: A glitch in AWS's billing system led to some customers experiencing an astronomical increase in their monthly charges, with bills rising from just a few cents to billions of dollars. This error has raised concerns about the reliability of billing operations in cloud services.
insightsFa: Ш§ШҙЪ©Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШіШҰЩ„ЩҮ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘ Ш§Ш·Щ…ЫҢЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШўЩҲШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш№ЩҲШ§ЩӮШЁ Ш§ЩӮШӘШөШ§ШҜЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШұ ШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ.
ШӯШ¬Щ… ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШәЫҢШұЩ…ЩҶШӘШёШұЩҮ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁШұ ШӘШөЩ…ЫҢЩ…вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұЫҢ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ.
ШӯЩ„ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁ ЩҲ Ш¬ШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Ш®ШіШ§ШұШ§ШӘ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ AWS ЪҶШ§Щ„Шҙ ШЁШұШ§ЩҶЪҜЫҢШІ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШЁЩҲШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§ШҙШӘШЁШ§ЩҮШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш№ЩҲШ§ЩӮШЁ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ ЩҲ ШЁШұ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ. Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШҙШҜЩҮ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁШұ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ШіШЁвҖҢЩҲЪ©Ш§ШұЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ Щ…ЩҶЩҒЫҢ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ.

articles.title: Google-backed satellites for wildfire detection launch as smoke chokes US, Canada
articles.content: As smoke from hundreds of burning wildfires spread across Canada and the United States, the first three operational satellites in the Google-backed FireSat program successfully launched into orbit. The satellites will begin providing wildfire detection capable of spotting even small fires in the United States, Australia, and Europe before the end of the year. The launch of the microsatellites aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on July 7, 2026 marks a transition to вҖңinitial operational capabilityвҖқ for the FireSat constellation managed by the nonprofit Earth Fire Alliance . After a three-month testing period, the three satellites will begin actively providing data to fire agencies while covering every fire-prone region on Earth at least twice per day. FireSat represents the first satellite constellation purpose-built for detecting wildfires, including spotting smaller fires that other satellites may miss. The satellites were designed by California-based satellite manufacturer Muon Space and have received over $15 million from Google to support initial deployment. Other notable financial supporters include the Bezos Earth Fund that committed $26 million. Read full article Comments
articles.author: Jeremy Hsu
articles.fingerprint: backed canada chokes detection google launch satellites smoke wildfire
articles.titleFa: Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШӯШӘ ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЪҜЩҲЪҜЩ„ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢШіЩҲШІЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШ¶Ш§ ЩҫШұШӘШ§ШЁ ШҙШҜЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШЁШ§ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ШҜЩҲШҜ ЩҶШ§ШҙЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШөШҜЩҮШ§ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢШіЩҲШІЫҢ ШҜШұ Ъ©Ш§ЩҶШ§ШҜШ§ ЩҲ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮШҢ ШіЩҮ Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮ Ш№Щ…Щ„ЫҢШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҶШ®ШіШӘ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮ FireSat Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШ§ ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЪҜЩҲЪҜЩ„ ШіШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШҢ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШҜШ§Шұ ЩҫШұШӘШ§ШЁ ШҙШҜЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩӮШ§ШҜШұ ШЁЩҮ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢШіЩҲШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ШҢ ШӯШӘЫҢ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶЪ©ШҢ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮШҢ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§Щ„ЫҢШ§ ЩҲ Ш§ШұЩҲЩҫШ§ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҲШҜ ЩҲ Ш§ШІ ЩҫШ§ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШіШ§Щ„ Ш¬Ш§ШұЫҢ ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢШӘ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШўШәШ§ШІ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮЩҶШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: As smoke from hundreds of wildfires spreads across Canada and the US, the first three operational satellites in the Google-backed FireSat program have successfully launched into orbit. These satellites will be capable of detecting wildfires, including smaller ones, in the US, Australia, and Europe by the end of the year.
insightsFa: ШіЩҮ Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮ ШҜШұ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮ FireSat ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶвҖҢШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ШәЫҢШұШ§ЩҶШӘЩҒШ§Ш№ЫҢ Earth Fire Alliance Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜЩӮЫҢЩӮ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҲЩӮШ№ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢШіЩҲШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ ШҙШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
ШӯЩ…Ш§ЫҢШӘ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ ЫұЫө Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ Ш§ШІ ШіЩҲЫҢ ЪҜЩҲЪҜЩ„ ЩҲ ЫІЫ¶ Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ Ш§ШІ Ш·ШұЩҒ Bezos Earth Fund Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш¬Щ…ЩҲШ№ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш·ЩҲШұ Ш®Ш§Шө ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢШіЩҲШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Щ…Ш§ЩҮЩҲШ§ШұЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЩҫЩҲШҙШҙвҖҢШҜЩҮЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҶШ§Ш·ЩӮ Щ…ШіШӘШ№ШҜ ШўШӘШҙвҖҢШіЩҲШІЫҢ ШұШ§ ШӯШҜШ§ЩӮЩ„ ШҜЩҲ ШЁШ§Шұ ШҜШұ ШұЩҲШІ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: Apple and Google ordered to purge вҖҳnudifyвҖҷ apps from App Stores
articles.content: In letters sent to Apple and Google, San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu said that both companies have long been aware that they are hosting apps in violation of state law.
articles.author: Lucas Ropek

articles.titleFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲЪҜЩ„ Щ…Щ„ШІЩ… ШЁЩҮ ШӯШ°ЩҒ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ "ЩҶЩҲШҜЩҒШ§ЫҢ" Ш§ШІ ЩҒШұЩҲШҙЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶ ШҙШҜЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШҜШ§ШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҮШұ ШіШ§ЩҶвҖҢЩҒШұШ§ЩҶШіЫҢШіЪ©ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲЪҜЩ„ ЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШұШіШ§Щ„ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Ш§Ш№Щ„Ш§Щ… Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜЩҲ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШҜШӘ Ш·ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩӮЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„ШӘЫҢ ШұШ§ ЩҶЩӮШ¶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜШҢ ШўЪҜШ§ЩҮ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ ЩҮШҜЩҒ ЩҶЩҲШҜЩҒШ§ЫҢ ШӘШөШ§ЩҲЫҢШұ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш№ШұШ¶ЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШӯШ§Ш¶Шұ ШӘШӯШӘ ЩҒШҙШ§Шұ ЩӮШ§ЩҶЩҲЩҶЫҢ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: San Francisco City Attorney David Chiu has sent letters to Apple and Google, stating that both companies have long been aware of hosting apps that violate state law. These apps are designed to nudify images, and they are now under legal pressure to be removed.
insightsFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҶЩҲШҜЩҒШ§ЫҢ ШӘШөЩҲЫҢШұ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ЩҶЩӮШ¶ ЩӮЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„ШӘЫҢ ЩҮШҜЩҒ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҲ ЪҜЩҲЪҜЩ„ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Ш№ШҜЩ… Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… ШіШұЫҢШ№ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӯШ°ЩҒ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШӘШӯШӘ ЩҒШҙШ§Шұ ЩӮШ§ЩҶЩҲЩҶЫҢ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ.
ШҜШ§ШҜШіШӘШ§ЩҶ ШіШ§ЩҶвҖҢЩҒШұШ§ЩҶШіЫҢШіЪ©ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ ШөШұШ§ШӯШӘ Ш§ШІ ЩҶЩӮШ¶ ЩӮЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЩҶ ШӘЩҲШіШ· Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ ШіШ®ЩҶ ЪҜЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶШҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШЁШ№Ш§ШӘ ЩӮШ§ЩҶЩҲЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜЩҲ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩ…ШұШ§ЩҮ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.

articles.title: Galaxy Z Fold 8 shows up on video as BTS joins SamsungвҖҷs hype train
articles.content: Credit: Ice Universe TL;DR A member of the popular boy band group BTS recently shared a video teasing one of SamsungвҖҷs upcoming foldables. The foldable in question appears to be the Galaxy Z Fold 8. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 just showed up in a place you wouldnвҖҷt expect WeвҖҷre only days away from SamsungвҖҷs much-anticipated Galaxy Unpacked event, where it will introduce the Galaxy Z Fold 8 series and Flip 8. As you would expect, advertising for the upcoming foldables has picked up. Recently, Samsung and Spider-Man teamed up for a teaser. Now, weвҖҷre getting another taste from one of the most popular bands in the world. If youвҖҷre a fan of BTS, then you probably know that the South Korean boy band is currently on tour around the world. Ahead of a show taking place in Paris, one of the members took some time out of their busy schedule to tease one of SamsungвҖҷs upcoming foldables. The band member in question is J-Hope , and the foldable he is holding appears to be the Galaxy Z Fold 8.
articles.author: Ryan McNeal

articles.title: Apple Raises iPhone Prices in Japan
articles.content: After raising prices on all Macs and iPads worldwide last month, Apple has now increased the price of iPhone models in Japan. As spotted by the Spanish blog iPhoneros , the iPhone 16, iPhone 17e, iPhone 17, iPhone Air, iPhone 17 Pro, and iPhone 17 Pro Max all received price increases on Apple's online store in Japan today. Prices increased by up to 11%. Model Previous Price New Price Increase iPhone 17 Pro Max ВҘ194,800 ВҘ214,800 10.3% iPhone 17 Pro ВҘ179,800 ВҘ194,800 8.3% iPhone Air ВҘ159,800 ВҘ177,800 11.3% iPhone 17 ВҘ129,800 ВҘ142,800 10% iPhone 17e ВҘ99,800 ВҘ107,800 8% iPhone 16 ВҘ114,800 ВҘ124,800 8.7% Apple attributed the Mac and iPad price increases last month to the ongoing memory chip shortage, which has driven up chip prices. In this case, however, the increases are likely due to the Japanese yen's depreciation against the U.S. dollar over the past year. Apple did not raise iPhone prices today in the U.S. or any other countries that we checked. Apple also raised the prices of iCloud+ and Apple Music plans in many countries today, including Japan, so price increases are a theme this Friday. articles.title: Apple Passed Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company Again
articles.content: Apple momentarily became the world's most valuable publicly traded company again today after its market valuation surpassed that of Nvidia, according to CNBC . However, the two companies have since traded places during today's session. The price of Apple's stock has increased by around 20% since it closed at $275.15 on June 25, pushing the company's valuation to nearly $5 trillion. AAPL is now trading above the $332 mark on the intraday market. Saudi Aramco technically remains the world's most valuable company, but it is often overlooked in this context because it is largely state-controlled. Tags: AAPL , Nvidia This article, " Apple Passed Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company Again " first appeared on MacRumors.com Discuss this article in our forums

articles.summaryEn: Today, Apple momentarily regained its title as the world's most valuable publicly traded company after its market valuation surpassed Nvidia. The price of Apple's stock has risen by approximately 20% since closing at $275.15 on June 25, and is now trading above $332.

articles.title: MacRumors Giveaway: Win a MacBook Neo Plus Color-Matched Satechi Accessories
articles.content: For this week's giveaway, we've teamed up with Satechi to offer MacRumors readers a chance to win a MacBook Neo and a set of color-matched MacBook Neo accessories to go along with it. A second-place winner will also get a set of Satechi's new accessories. The MacBook Neo collection includes the $45 OntheGo 5-inвҖ“1 Multiport Adapter , the $45 USB-C Snap Hub , and the $30 Slim EX Wireless Mouse . All of the accessories come in Citrus, Blush, Indigo, and Silver to match the ‌MacBook Neo‌ color options. Satechi's OntheGo 5-inвҖ“1 Multiport Adapter plugs into the USB-C port on the ‌MacBook Neo‌ and adds five additional ports. It has two USB-C ports, a 5Gb/s USB-A port, an HDMI port that supports up to a 4K 60Hz external display, and an SD card slot. One of the USB-C ports offers 60W passthrough charging and the other supports 5Gb/s data transfer speeds. An included magnet lets it attach to a MagSafe iPhone or to the lid of the MacBook with a 3M adhesive magnet mount ring. The matching built-in USB-C cable is braided for durability, and it rolls up into the adapter, making it a compact travel option. It measures 2.57 inches in diameter and it's 0.87 inches thick. Satechi's USB-C Snap Hub also adds ports, but it has a different design. It plugs into both of the ‌MacBook Neo‌ USB-C ports, expanding them into six connections. There are two USB-C ports, an HDMI port that supports a 4K 60Hz display, a 5Gb/s USB-A port, an SD card slot, and a microSD card slot. One USB-C port supports 45W passthrough charging, and the other offers 5Gb/s data transfer speeds. The hub fits flush to the side of the ‌MacBook Neo‌, and it has a dual-material design. The top is an anodized aluminum that matches the ‌MacBook Neo‌'s body, while the base is a soft-touch ABS plastic meant to match the keyboard finish. Satechi's Slim EX Wireless Mouse completes the collection, and it's made from color-matched aluminum. The mouse supports two Bluetooth channels and a 2.4GHz wireless connection, and it is able to work across macOS, iPadOS, Windows, and Android. It features quiet click switches and a precision-machined scroll wheel, plus there is a USB-C rechargeable, user-replaceable battery for longevity. We have a ‌MacBook Neo‌ and a Satechi accessory set to go along with it for one lucky MacRumors reader, and a second winner will get the ‌MacBook Neo‌ accessory kit. To enter to win, use the widget below and enter an email address. Email addresses will be used solely for contact purposes to reach the winner(s) and send the prize(s). You can earn additional entries by subscribing to our weekly newsletter , subscribing to our YouTube channel , following us on Twitter , following us on Instagram , following us on Threads , or visiting the MacRumors Facebook page . Due to the complexities of international laws regarding giveaways, only U.S. residents who are 18 years or older, UK residents who are 18 years or older, and Canadian residents who have reached the age of majority in their province or territory are eligible to enter . All federal, state, provincial, and/or local taxes, fees, and surcharges are the sole responsibility of the prize winner. To offer feedback or get more information on the giveaway restrictions, please refer to our Site Feedback section , as that is where discussion of the rules will be redirected. Satechi Giveaway The contest will run from today (July 17) at 9:00 a.m. Pacific Time through 9:00 a.m. Pacific Time on July 24. The winner will be chosen randomly on or shortly after July 24 and will be contacted by email. The winner will have 48 hours to respond and provide a shipping address before a new winner is chosen. articles.title: Apple Raises iCloud+ Prices in 8 Countries
articles.content: Apple has increased the price of iCloud + in Nigeria, TГјrkiye, Vietnam, Japan, Egypt, New Zealand, the Philippines, and Indonesia, according to an updated version of its iCloud support document . Price increases range from 11 percent to 55 percent depending on the plan and the country. Nigeria saw the biggest hike, and a 50GB plan is now вӮҰ1,300, up from вӮҰ900. TГјrkiye saw the next largest increase, with a 50GB plan priced at 49.99 TL, up from 39.99 TL. Apple has likely adjusted pricing due to currency fluctuations. The Japanese Yen has weakened over the past year, for example, and the dollar is up almost 10 percent against the yen. The Turkish Lira has also depreciated, increasing the value of the U.S. dollar. A full list of price changes can be found on Apple's iCloud website . U.S. pricing has not changed, nor has pricing in any other country not listed above. All Apple Accounts receive 5GB of free ‌iCloud‌ storage. Apple's ‌iCloud‌+ plans give users extra storage, as well as perks like Hide My Email, HomeKit Secure Video , and ‌iCloud‌ Private Relay. articles.title: Apple Music Now Costs $11.99 as Apple Increases Subscription Prices
articles.content: Apple today increased the price of Apple Music and some Apple One subscription bundles. An individual ‌Apple Music‌ subscription is now $1 more expensive in the United States, going from $10.99 to $11.99 per month. Pricing on the ‌Apple Music‌ Family plan increased by $3, increasing from $16.99 to $19.99. Student pricing is up $1, with the plan now priced at $6.99. The price of the individual ‌Apple One‌ plan hasn't changed, but the Family and Premier plans have gone up $2. The Family plan is now $27.95 per month and the Premier plan is $39.95 per month. Apple has also raised prices in Brazil . In a statement to Music Business Worldwide , Apple said it was raising prices due to the "result of rising licensing costs." ‌Apple Music‌ prices were last increased in October 2022. articles.title: iPhone 18 Pro Launching in Two Months With These 12 New Features
articles.content: It is now mid-July, and that means the iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max are now just two months away. The devices are expected to look similar to the iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Pro Max, but there will still be many year-over-year changes, with rumored features including a smaller Dynamic Island, 5G via satellite, and more. Apple is expected to unveil the iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max, and a foldable " iPhone Ultra " in the first half of September, and the devices should be released in the second half of the month. The regular iPhone 18, a lower-end iPhone 18e, and a second-generation iPhone Air will reportedly be announced around March 2027 . Below, we have recapped 12 features rumored for the iPhone 18 Pro models, as of July: Smaller Dynamic Island: It has been rumored that Face ID's flood illuminator will be moved under the screen on the iPhone 18 Pro models, paving the way for a smaller Dynamic Island on the devices. LTPO+ Displays: The next Pro models are expected to have the same overall design as the iPhone 17 Pro models , including 6.3-inch and 6.9-inch display sizes and a "plateau" housing three rear cameras. However, the displays will reportedly use so-called LTPO+ display technology , which should contribute to longer battery life. Variable Aperture: The main 48-megapixel Fusion camera on both iPhone 18 Pro models is rumored to have a variable aperture , which would allow users to control the amount of light that passes through the camera's lens and reaches the sensor. This would provide greater control over depth of field. However, given that iPhones have smaller image sensors due to smartphone size constraints, it is unclear exactly how meaningful this improvement would be. A20 Pro Chip: Apple's next-generation A20 Pro chip is expected to use TSMC's first-generation 2nm process, whereas the A19 Pro chip is 3nm. With a 2nm architecture and a new packaging design , the A20 Pro chip should deliver solid year-over-year performance and power efficiency gains. C2 Modem: Apple's custom C1 cellular modem for 5G and LTE debuted in the iPhone 16e last year, and that was followed by a C1X chip in the iPhone Air. Apple says the C1X modem is up to twice as fast as the C1 modem, and the most power-efficient modem in an iPhone ever. The improvements should continue with Apple's third-generation C2 modem in the iPhone 18 Pro models. 5G via Satellite: With the C2 modem, the iPhone 18 Pro models will reportedly support 5G via satellite for web browsing without Wi-Fi or cellular connectivity. N2 Chip: Most of the iPhone 17 models and the iPhone Air are equipped with an Apple-designed N1 chip that enables Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 6, and Thread. Apple says the N1 chip also improves the overall performance and reliability of features like Personal Hotspot and AirDrop. iPhone 18 Pro models are expected to have Apple's next-generation N2 chip, but it is not yet known what improvements would come with this upgrade. Dark Cherry: The special color for the iPhone 18 Pro models will reportedly be Dark Cherry , alongside Light Blue, Dark Gray, and Silver. The existing Cosmic Orange and Deep Blue colors are expected to be discontinued. Simplified Camera Control: Apple is expected to simplify the Camera Control button on the iPhone 18 Pro models, by removing touch sensitivity and haptic feedback. The redesigned button will only have pressure sensitivity. Redesigned Rear Ceramic Shield: The rear Ceramic Shield area for MagSafe is rumored to feature a more frosted and seamless appearance on the iPhone 18 Pro models compared to the current two-tone design. Larger Battery (iPhone 18 Pro Max): According to alleged Chinese regulatory database filings , the iPhone 18 Pro Max will have nearly a 10% larger battery compared to the iPhone 17 Pro Max. Thicker Design (iPhone 18 Pro Max): It has been rumored that the iPhone 18 Pro Max will be thicker than both the iPhone 17 Pro Max and the iPhone 18 Pro. Bookmark our iPhone 18 and iPhone 18 Pro roundups toвҖҰ

articles.title: iPhone Loyalty Rate Climbs to 87% as Switching to Android Slows
articles.content: iPhone owners are sticking with the platform at a higher rate than a year ago, with new research firm CIRP putting iPhone loyalty at 87% for the first quarter of 2026. CIRP's latest quarterly survey found that just 12% of new iPhone buyers in the March quarter had come over from an Android phone, with the remaining 1% arriving from a feature phone, a different smartphone platform, or buying a smartphone for the first time. That puts loyalty three percentage points above the same quarter a year earlier, when 14% of buyers switched from Android and the overall loyalty figure sat at 84%. The firm has been tracking this movement for years by asking iPhone buyers what device they owned right before their purchase, among other questions about how they shop. Across the periods it measured recently, the share of buyers coming from Android has held in a fairly narrow 11% to 15% band, a much smaller pool of switchers than in the iPhone's early years, when Apple was still expanding beyond AT&T to other U.S. carriers and picking up large numbers of new users in the process. The trend held steady in early 2024 too, when 13% of buyers arrived from Android and loyalty came in at 85%. CIRP's numbers suggest the bulk of smartphone owners settled on a platform long ago, and only a small slice change sides each year. This is a pattern other recent surveys have pointed to as well, even if their methodology and figures differ from CIRP's. Apple will roll out Siri AI, its overhauled AI assistant, in iOS 27 later this year, which could help retain existing iPhone owners. Attracting Android converts is a separate challenge, though Apple's upcoming foldable iPhone will introduce a new form factor that may appeal to some existing Android foldable owners. ЩҒЩӮШ· ЫұЫІЩӘ Ш§ШІ Ш®ШұЫҢШҜШ§ШұШ§ЩҶ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШўЫҢЩҒЩҲЩҶ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЩҶШҜШұЩҲЫҢШҜ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
ЩҶШұШ® ЩҲЩҒШ§ШҜШ§ШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Щ…ЩӮШ§ЫҢШіЩҮ ШЁШ§ ШіШ§Щ„ ЪҜШ°ШҙШӘЩҮ ШіЩҮ ШҜШұШөШҜ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШҙЩ…Ш§Шұ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ ШЁЫҢЩҶ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЫҢШІШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЩҶШӘШҙШ§Шұ Siri AI ШҜШұ iOS 27 Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶЪҜЩҮШҜШ§ШұЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШўЫҢЩҒЩҲЩҶ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш«ШЁШ§ШӘ ЩҲ Щ…ШӯШЁЩҲШЁЫҢШӘ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ЫҢ ШўЫҢЩҒЩҲЩҶ ШҜШұ ШЁЫҢЩҶ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҶШҜШұЩҲЫҢШҜ ШЁЩҮ Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ЫҢ ЩҲЩҒШ§ШҜШ§ШұЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШұЩҶШҜ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: The Zilog Z80 has turned 50
articles.content: Article URL: https://goliath32.com/blog/z80.html Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48951461 Points: 121 # Comments: 31
articles.author: st_goliath
articles.fingerprint: turned z80 zilog
articles.titleFa: ШІШ§ЫҢЩ„ЪҜ Z80 ШЁЩҮ ЩҶЫҢЩ…вҖҢЩӮШұЩҶЪҜЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШіЫҢШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШІШ§ЫҢЩ„ЪҜ Z80ШҢ ЫҢЪ© Щ…ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲЩҫШұЩҲШіШіЩҲШұ Щ…ШҙЩҮЩҲШұ ЩҲ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұЪҜШ°Ш§Шұ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҜШұ ШҜЩҮЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ 1970 ЩҲ 1980 ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘШҢ ШЁЩҮ 50 ШіШ§Щ„ЪҜЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШіЫҢШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲЩҫШұЩҲШіШіЩҲШұ ШҜШұ ШӘШӯЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ъ©Ш§Щ…ЩҫЫҢЩҲШӘШұ ЩҲ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Ъ©ШӘШұЩҲЩҶЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ЩҶЩӮШҙ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҒШ§ Ъ©ШұШҜ ЩҲ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШҜШұ ШЁШұШ®ЫҢ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұЩҲШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: The Zilog Z80, a famous and influential microprocessor developed in the 1970s and 1980s, has turned 50 years old. This microprocessor played a significant role in the evolution of computer technology and electronic devices and is still used in some systems today.
insightsFa: ШІШ§ЫҢЩ„ЪҜ Z80 ШҜШұ ШіШ§Щ„ 1978 Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ ШҙШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲЩҫШұЩҲШіШіЩҲШұ ШҜШұ Ъ©Ш§Щ…ЩҫЫҢЩҲШӘШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®Ш§ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ЩҲ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢЩҲЫҢЫҢ Щ…ШӯШЁЩҲШЁЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘ.
ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШіШ§ШҜЪҜЫҢ ЩҲ Ш§ЩҶШ№Ш·Ш§ЩҒвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢШҢ Z80 ШӘШ§ Ш§Щ…ШұЩҲШІ ШҜШұ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ№ШҜШҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§Шұ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІЩҶШҜЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЩҫШ§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁШіЫҢШ§ШұЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЩҲЩ„ЩҲЪҳЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҜШұЩҶ ШҜШұ ЩҶШёШұ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш¶ШұШЁвҖҢШ§Щ„Ш§Ш¬Щ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ 50 ШіШ§Щ„ЪҜЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲЩҫШұЩҲШіШіЩҲШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШӘШ§ШұЫҢШ®ЪҶЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ ШұШ§ ЩҶЩ…Ш§ЫҢШҙ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш®ШЁШұ ШҜШұШЁШ§ШұЩҮ ШІШ§ЫҢЩ„ЪҜ Z80 ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘШ§ШұЫҢШ®ЪҶЩҮ ЩҲ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Ш№Щ…ЫҢЩӮ Щ…ЫҢЪ©ШұЩҲЩҫШұЩҲШіШіЩҲШұЩҮШ§ ШЁШұ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҫШ§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§Шұ ЩҶШіЩ„ЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§Щ…ЩҫЫҢЩҲШӘШұЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ„Ъ©ШӘШұЩҲЩҶЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮ ЩҲ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШҜШұ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЩҲЩ„ЩҲЪҳЫҢЪ© Щ…ШҜШұЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұШҜ.

articles.title: GPT-5.6 is deleting user files when given full access, and OpenAI says it shouldn't but did
articles.content: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 has accidentally wiped users' entire home directories in several cases, mostly in the unprotected "Full Access Mode." The model overwrites a temporary directory variable and carries out destructive actions on its own instead of asking for confirmation. OpenAI has announced extra safeguards and a detailed post-mortem. The article GPT-5.6 is deleting user files when given full access, and OpenAI says it shouldn't but did appeared first on The Decoder .
articles.author: Matthias Bastian
articles.fingerprint: access deleting did files full given gpt openai says shouldn user when
articles.titleFa: GPT-5.6 Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ШӘ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ Ъ©Ш§Щ…Щ„ШҢ ЩҶШ§Ш®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘЩҮ Щ…Ш¬ШЁЩҲШұ ШЁЩҮ ШӯШ°ЩҒ ЩҒШ§ЫҢЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ Ъ©ШұШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ GPT-5.6 Ш§ШІ OpenAI ШҜШұ ЪҶЩҶШҜЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШ·ЩҲШұ ЩҶШ§Ш®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘЩҮ ШҜШ§ЫҢШұЪ©ШӘЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®Ш§ЩҶЪҜЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ ШӯШ°ЩҒ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШҢ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩҲЫҢЪҳЩҮ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ШӘ В«ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ Ъ©Ш§Щ…Щ„В» ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ШӯЩҒШ§ШёШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШ¬Ш§ЫҢ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШҢ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШ·ЩҲШұ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§Шұ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… ШЁЩҮ ШӯШ°ЩҒ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ OpenAI ШӘШҜШ§ШЁЫҢШұ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш¬Щ„ЩҲЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ Ш§Ш№Щ„Ш§Щ… Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 has unintentionally deleted users' home directories in several instances, mainly in the unprotected 'Full Access Mode.' Instead of asking for user confirmation, the model automatically executes destructive actions and OpenAI has announced additional safeguards to address this issue.
insightsFa: GPT-5.6 ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ШӘ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ Ъ©Ш§Щ…Щ„ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ШІ ШЁЫҢЩҶ ШұЩҒШӘЩҶ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШ·ЩҲШұ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§Шұ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… ШЁЩҮ ШӯШ°ЩҒ ЩҒШ§ЫҢЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.
OpenAI ШЁЩҮ ШӘШҜШ§ШЁЫҢШұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш¬Щ„ЩҲЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШҙШ§ШұЩҮ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Щ…ЩҶЩҒЫҢ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЫҢШ§ШЁШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш¬ШҜЫҢвҖҢШӘШұ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ ШұЫҢШіЪ© ШҜШұ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Щ…ШіШӘЩӮЫҢЩ… ШўЩҶ ШЁШұ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ Ш§Щ„ШІШ§Щ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШұШ§ ШҜШұ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҶЩ…Ш§ЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
articles.content: TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra . The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge . YouTube has been working on a similar tool and recently made it available to all adult users . Creators who are part of TikTok's test and want to use the tool will first have to verify their identity with a company called Jumio . You'll have to do a real-time selfie scan and an ID check, but Kizer says that "TikTok does not retain ID documents, and facial informati вҖҰ Read the full story at The Verge.
articles.author: Jay Peters
articles.fingerprint: detection likeness testing tiktok tool
articles.titleFa: ШӘШіШӘ Ш§ШЁШІШ§Шұ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҙШЁШ§ЩҮШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӘЫҢЩғвҖҢШӘЩҲЩғ
articles.summaryFa: ШӘЫҢЩғвҖҢШӘЩҲЩғ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҙШЁШ§ЩҮШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЩҫШұШҜШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШіШ§ШІЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ Щ…ШӯШӘЩҲШ§ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШұШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ШӘШ§ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШЁШ§ЩҮШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙ ШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШЁШІШ§Шұ ШҜШұ Ш§ШЁШӘШҜШ§ ШЁШ§ ШӘШ№ШҜШ§ШҜЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШіШ§ШІЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ ШўЩ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ЫҢЫҢ ШўШІЩ…Ш§ЫҢШҙ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ ЩҲ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ ЩҮЩҲЫҢШӘ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ЫҢЪ© ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш«Ш§Щ„Ш« ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: TikTok is testing a new tool that scans for AI likenesses and allows creators to report them to the company. Initially, the tool is being tested with a select group of U.S. creators and requires identity verification through a third-party service.

ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ ЩҮЩҲЫҢШӘ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұ Ш§ШІ Ш·ШұЫҢЩӮ ЪҶЪ© Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіЩҮ ЩҲ ШіЩ„ЩҒЫҢ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.

articles.title: The WeekвҖҷs 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: No Summer Doldrums As Dollars Still Flow To AI
articles.content: Want to keep track of the largest startup funding deals in 2026 with our curated list of $100 million-plus venture deals to U.S.-based companies? Check out The Crunchbase Megadeals Board . This is a weekly feature that runs down the weekвҖҷs top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last weekвҖҷs biggest funding deal roundup here . It was not a holiday week on the funding front, as a raft of largely AI-focused companies closed big rounds. The largest of these was a $1.5 billion financing to enterprise AI startup Fireworks AI, and a Series D for meal and delivery provider Wonder . The week also included some big financings for enterprise tech, food delivery, drones and construction automation. 1. Fireworks AI , $1.5B, enterprise AI tools: Fireworks AI, a developer of tools for enterprises to turn вҖңgeneral-purpose models into specialized intelligence trained on their own data,вҖқ raised $1.505 billion in Series D funding. Atreides Management , Index Ventures and TCV led the financing, which set a $17.5 billion valuation for the San Mateo, California-based company. 2. Wonder , $650M, meals and delivery: Wonder, an operator of kitchens and meal delivery services, closed on $650 million in Series D funding at a $9 billion pre-money valuation. Capital will go in part toward expanding operations for the New York-based company, which currently has 140 locations. 3. Chai Discovery , $400M, life sciences AI: AI drug discovery startup Chai Discovery secured $400 million in Series C funding at a $3.8 billion valuation. Index Ventures led the financing, investing alongside Sequoia Capital , Dimension , Kleiner Perkins and others. 4. Walden Robotics , $300M, robots: Cambridge, Massachusetts-based Walden Robotics, a startup building general-purpose robots for work in manufacturing and logistics, launched out of stealth with $300 million in funding. Toyota and Deviation Capital led the round, which values the company at $1.1 billion. 5. Brinc , $125M, drones: Seattle-based Brinc, a developer of drones for use in public safety and emergency operations, raised $125 million in fresh funding. Motorola Solutions led the financing, with participation from Index Ventures , and Figma founder and CEO Dylan Field . 6. (tied) TerraFirma , $100M, construction automation: Austin-based TerraFirma, a developer of AI-enabled software and autonomous robotics technology for the construction industry, landed $100 million in new funding, bringing total investment to date to $115 million. 6. (tied) Spectro Cloud , $100M, enterprise AI: Spectro Cloud, a provider of AI infrastructure management software, said it raised more than $100 million in a Series D round led by Goldman Sachs Alternatives . The financing brings total capital raised by San Jose-based Spectro Cloud to $260 million. 8. Singularity , $80M, defense tech: Singularity, a startup focused on developing air defense technology, emerged from stealth with $80 million in Series A funding. Khosla Ventures and Felicis 1 led the financing, which set a $400 million valuation for the Los Angeles-based company. 9. (tied) Flex , $70M, fintech: San-Francisco-based fintech startup Flex, a private banking platform for high-net-worth business owners, raised $70 million in a Series B1 financing led by Halo Fund . The round follows the company’s $60 million Series B in December. 9. (tied) State Affairs , $70M, AI and policy: State Affairs, an AI platform for policy and regulation, secured $70 million in Series A funding led by Khosla Ventures and Founders Fund . Methodology We tracked the largest announced rounds in the Crunchbase database that were raised by U.S.-based companies for the period of July 11-17. Although most announced rounds are represented in the database, there could be a small time lag as some rounds are reported late in the week. Illustration: Dom Guzman Felicis is an investor in Crunchbase. They have no say in our editorial process. For more, head here . ↩
