articles.title: What Zelenskyy risks by firing UkraineвҖҷs tech-minded defense minister
articles.content: On July 15, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, the official most closely identified with UkraineвҖҷs much-lauded effort to turn drones, software, and commercial technology into military advantage. The move could have implications not just for Kyiv, but also for Washington. Ukraine has become the clearest test of how a smaller force can use inexpensive systems and rapid development to counter a larger conventional army. Those are meaningful lessons to a Pentagon preparing for wars in which U.S. forces may need to disperse and function while under long-range attack. American military leaders and defense companies alike are studying UkraineвҖҷs rapid feedback loops between frontline units, developers, and manufacturers, which means ZelenskyyвҖҷs decision may affect how quickly those lessons continue to emerge . Fedorov, 35, entered the Defense Ministry in January after more than six years as UkraineвҖҷs minister of digital transformation, where he oversaw Diia, the countryвҖҷs system for moving government documents and services online. In his new role, he brought the tech-executive ethos to a military bureaucracy. His ministry expanded digital platforms for service members, reservists, and conscripts; created systems that enabled units to order equipment directly from Ukrainian manufacturers; and rewarded drone teams for verified battlefield results with credits toward additional technology. It also pushed manufacturers toward common controls for fiber-optic drones, making different models easier for troops to use, and required more suppliers to compete for government contracts. Fedorov said one medium-range strike drone tender lowered prices by around 20%. Some of his most visible successes involved the technology Ukraine uses to offset RussiaвҖҷs advantages in troop numbers and industrial capacity. Fedorov helped persuade Elon Musk to restrict unauthorized Russian access to SpaceX’s satellite internet network Starlink, disrupting Russian drone operations. Fedorov’s six-month tenure also coincided with an expansion of Ukrainian long-range strikes against refineries, logistics sites, and military targets in Russia and occupied Crimea. Those campaigns can’t be credited to one official, though Fedorov helped build the procurement and technology system behind them. The reason for his removal remains only partly explained . Zelenskyy said a rift between Fedorov and Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi, UkraineвҖҷs commander in chiefвҖ”over military reforms and the division of authority between the Defense Ministry and the armed forces leadershipвҖ”had forced him to pick a side. Fedorov, for his part, claimed in a press conference that he had urged Zelenskyy to replace Syrskyi and the chief of the general staff. Fedorov later agreed to work with the military leadership, he said, but found that his efforts to restructure units and create specialized centers were being blocked. The Kyiv Independent also reported that some lawmakers believed Fedorov had moved too slowly to reform the system for recruiting civilians into the armed forces. (The Defense Ministry did not respond to Fast Company вҖҷs request for comment.) Zelenskyy faced a legitimate management problem . A civilian defense minister and the military commander can’t pursue incompatible strategies for long. Fedorov had held the job for only six months, lacked military service experience, and at times presented technology as a more complete answer than it could realistically provide. Ukraine still needs trained infantry, a coherent command structure, and a functioning mobilization system. The decision could have ramifications that ripple through the war effort. By retaining Syrskyi and removing Fedorov, Zelenskyy preserved continuity in the chain of command. But he also, in effect, weakened the office with the clearest mandate to challenge procurement practices, personnel systems, and a command culture that many Ukrainian soldiers considerвҖҰ
articles.author: Max Ufberg

articles.title: Apple Music gets first U.S. price hike in four years
articles.content: Apple Music is raising U.S. subscription prices for the first time since 2022, increasing every paid tier and hitting family subscribers with the largest jump. Apple Music raises prices The Apple Music Individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, up from $10.99. The Family plan rose from $16.99 to $19.99 per month and continues to support as many as six people through Family Sharing. Verified college students will pay $6.99 per month, up from $5.99. The Student plan includes the full Apple Music service and access to Apple TV at no additional cost. The increases add $12 per year to the Individual and Student plans. A Family subscription will cost $36 more annually, bringing the total to $239.88 per year. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums
articles.author: Andrew Orr <andreworr@appleinsider.com>
articles.fingerprint: apple first four gets hike music price years

ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ Ш·ШұШӯ ЩҒШұШҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ 11.99 ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ЩҲ Ш·ШұШӯ Ш®Ш§ЩҶЩҲШ§ШҜЪҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ 19.99 ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ ШұШіЫҢШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. The pair also talks about ChatGPT vs Siri AI, OpenAI vs Apple, the end of carrier-financed unlocked iPhones, and nine upcoming new emoji. They also compare ChatGPT and Siri AI, and discuss the end of carrier-financed unlocked iPhones and new emoji possibilities.
insightsFa: ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ iOS 27
Щ…ЩӮШ§ЫҢШіЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ChatGPT ЩҲ Siri
ЩҫШ§ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШўЫҢЩҒЩҲЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШўШІШ§ШҜ ШҙШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ш§ЩҫШұШ§ШӘЩҲШұЩҮШ§
ЩҶЪ©Ш§ШӘ ЩҶШөШЁ iOS 27 ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮ Ш§ШөЩ„ЫҢ
Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩ…ЩҲШ¬ЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ШЁШӘШ§ЫҢ Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ…ЫҢ iOS ШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШұШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ШӘШ§ ЩҫЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЩҶШӘШҙШ§Шұ ШұШіЩ…ЫҢШҢ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШұШ§ ШўШІЩ…Ш§ЫҢШҙ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЪҜЩҲШҙЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҒЩҲЩҶ ШұШ§ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: An AWS billing bug sent users estimated charges of up to $2.5 trillion
articles.content: AWS users woke up Friday to find estimated billing emails showing charges ranging from hundreds of millions to $2.5 trillion. A unit pricing error in the AWS Billing ConsoleвҖҷs estimated billing computation subsystem was the cause, Amazon confirmed. One Reddit user whose charges totalled $0.19 last month received an estimated bill of nearly $2.5 billion. […] This story continues at The Next Web
articles.author: Cristian Dina
articles.fingerprint: aws billing bug charges estimated sent trillion users
articles.titleFa: ШЁug ЩҒШ§Ъ©ШӘЩҲШұШҜЩҮЫҢ AWSШӣ ШЁШұШўЩҲШұШҜ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШӘШ§ ЫІ.Ыө ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ
articles.summaryFa: Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ AWS ШөШЁШӯ ШұЩҲШІ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЩҮ ШЁШ§ Ш§ЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· ШЁЩҮ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҶШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШұШўЩҲШұШҜ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ШІ ШөШҜ Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШӘШ§ ЫІ.Ыө ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ Щ…ШӘШәЫҢШұ ШЁЩҲШҜ. ШўЩ…Ш§ШІЩҲЩҶ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШіШҰЩ„ЩҮ ЩҶШ§ШҙЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЫҢЪ© Ш®Ш·Ш§ЫҢ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ… Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁЩҮ ШЁШұШўЩҲШұШҜ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁ Ъ©ЩҶШіЩҲЩ„ AWS ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш«Ш§Щ„ШҢ ЫҢЪ©ЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ ЪҜШ°ШҙШӘЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШ· Ы°.ЫұЫ№ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ЩҫШұШҜШ§Ш®ШӘ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ ШЁЩҲШҜШҢ ШЁШұШўЩҲШұШҜ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁЫҢ ЩҶШІШҜЫҢЪ© ШЁЩҮ ЫІ.Ыө Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ ШЁЩҲШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: AWS users woke up on Friday to find billing emails estimating charges ranging from hundreds of millions to $2.5 trillion. Amazon confirmed that this issue was caused by a pricing error in the AWS Billing Console's estimated billing computation subsystem. For instance, a user whose actual charges were just $0.19 last month received an estimated bill of nearly $2.5 billion.
insightsFa: Ш®Ш·Ш§ЫҢ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Ъ©ЩҶШіЩҲЩ„ AWS ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« ШЁШұЩҲШІ Ш§ШұЩӮШ§Щ… ШәЫҢШұЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁ ШҙШҜ.
ШЁШұШ®ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЪҶЩҶШҜЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұЫҢ ЩҲ ШӯШӘЫҢ ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶЫҢ ШҜШұЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶШҜ.
ШўЩ…Ш§ШІЩҲЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ШіШұШ№ШӘ Ш№Щ„ШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ ШұШ§ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ ЩҲ ШӘЩҲШ¶ЫҢШӯ ШҜШ§ШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш№ШӘШЁШ§Шұ AWS ШўШіЫҢШЁ ШЁШұШіШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ ЩҲ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШӯШӘЫҢШ§Ш· ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Ш®Ш·Ш§ЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҒЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұ ШөЩҲШұШӘвҖҢШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш№ШӘШЁШ§Шұ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ШўШіЫҢШЁ ШЁШІЩҶШҜ. ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« ШіЩҲШЎШӘЩҒШ§ЩҮЩ… ЩҲ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҜШұ Щ…ЫҢШ§ЩҶ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: Emesent secures $17 million to accelerate autonomous intelligence platform
articles.content: Emesent, an autonomous mapping and robotics company, has announced $17 million in total new funding to accelerate development of its Cortex AI autonomy platform, expand its Aura cloud software offering, and deepen its operations across the mining, defense, and architecture, engineering and construction (AEC) sectors. The raise comprises a $7 million venture debt facility from […]
articles.author: Sam Francis
articles.fingerprint: accelerate autonomous emesent intelligence million platform secures
articles.titleFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Emesent ЫұЫ· Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘШіШұЫҢШ№ ШҜШұ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… ЩҮЩҲШҙ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұШ§ЩҶ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ EmesentШҢ ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҶЩӮШҙЩҮвҖҢШЁШұШҜШ§ШұЫҢ ЩҲ ШұШЁШ§ШӘЫҢЪ© Ш®ЩҲШҜШұШ§ЩҶШҢ Ш§ШІ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ЫұЫ· Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ Ш®ШЁШұ ШҜШ§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… ЩҮЩҲШҙ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұШ§ЩҶ Cortex AI Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ЩҶШұЩ…вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ Aura ЩҲ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ Ш№Щ…Щ„ЫҢШ§ШӘ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁШ®ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш№ШҜЩҶШҢ ШҜЩҒШ§Ш№ ЩҲ Щ…ЩҮЩҶШҜШіЫҢ ЩҲ ШіШ§Ш®ШӘЩ…Ш§ЩҶ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Emesent, an autonomous mapping and robotics company, has announced a total of $17 million in new funding to accelerate the development of its Cortex AI autonomy platform. The funding will also expand its Aura cloud software offering and deepen its operations in the mining, defense, and architecture, engineering, and construction sectors.
insightsFa: ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ЫұЫ· Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘШіШұЫҢШ№ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… ЩҮЩҲШҙ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұШ§ЩҶ
ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ Ш№Щ…Щ„ЫҢШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁШ®ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш№ШҜЩҶ ЩҲ ШҜЩҒШ§Ш№
ШӘЩӮЩҲЫҢШӘ ЩҶШұЩ…вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ Aura ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ШЁЩҮШӘШұ
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ШӘШЈЩ…ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШіШұЫҢШ№ ЩҶЩҲШўЩҲШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШұШЁШ§ШӘЫҢЪ© Ш®ЩҲШҜШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ Ъ©Ш§ШұШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ ШөЩҶШ§ЫҢШ№ Щ…Ш®ШӘЩ„ЩҒ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: Critical Zoom Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Windows Accounts
articles.content: Zoom patched a critical Windows flaw that could enable remote account takeover, along with three high-severity privilege-escalation vulnerabilities. The post Critical Zoom Flaw Could Let Attackers Take Over Windows Accounts appeared first on TechRepublic .
articles.author: Ken Underhill
articles.fingerprint: accounts attackers could critical flaw let over take windows zoom
articles.titleFa: ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩӮШө ШЁШӯШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШІЩҲЩ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬Щ…Ш§ЩҶ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШІЩҮ ШҜЩҮШҜ ШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲЫҢЩҶШҜЩҲШІ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШөШұЩҒ ШҜШұШўЩҲШұЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШІЩҲЩ… ЫҢЪ© ЩҶЩӮШө ШЁШӯШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШұШ§ ШҜШұ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ ЩҲЫҢЩҶШҜЩҲШІ ШұЩҒШ№ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШұШ§ЩҮ ШҜЩҲШұ ШЁЩҮ ШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұЫҢ ШұШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬Щ…Ш§ЩҶ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҮЩ… Ъ©ЩҶШҜ. ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶШҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ ШұЩҒШ№ ШіЩҮ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ ШЁШ§ ШҙШҜШӘ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШіШ·Шӯ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Zoom has patched a critical Windows flaw that could allow remote account takeover. This update also addresses three additional high-severity privilege escalation vulnerabilities.
insightsFa: ЩҶЩӮШө ШЁШӯШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШІЩҲЩ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬Щ…Ш§ЩҶ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШІЩҮ Ъ©ЩҶШӘШұЩ„ Ш§ШІ ШұШ§ЩҮ ШҜЩҲШұ ШӯШіШ§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲЫҢЩҶШҜЩҲШІ ШұШ§ ШЁШҜЩҮШҜ.
ШІЩҲЩ… Ш№Щ„Ш§ЩҲЩҮ ШЁШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҶЩӮШөШҢ ШіЩҮ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ ШұШ§ ЩҶЫҢШІ ШұЩҒШ№ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШІ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ШІЩҲЩ… ШЁЩҮ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.

articles.title: Apple Music is getting a price hike
articles.content: Apple Music is more expensive now. In the US, an individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, a $1 bump up from the previous $10.99 price. A family plan now costs $19.99 per month, up from $16.99, and a student plan costs $6.99 per month, up from $5.99. Apple, in a statement to Music Business Worldwide , says it is increasing prices "as a result of rising licensing costs." Music Business Worldwide also reports that prices gone up in the UK and Europe, and the publication "understands" that hikes are hitting other countries as well. Apple didn't immediately reply to a request for comment from The Verge . Apple Music last got a price increase i вҖҰ Read the full story at The Verge.
articles.author: Jay Peters
articles.fingerprint: apple getting hike music price

articles.title: Apple raises prices for Apple Music and Apple One subscriptions
articles.content: Apple has raised the price of Apple Music today in the United States and other countries. Starting today, Apple MusicвҖҷs Individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, up from $10.99. Apple has also increased the price of select Apple One bundles. In a statement to 9to5Mac , Apple said: вҖңAs a result of rising licensing costs, Apple Music is increasing its subscription price beginning today.вҖқ
articles.author: Chance Miller
articles.fingerprint: apple music one prices raises subscriptions
articles.titleFa: Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ Ш§ШҙШӘШұШ§Ъ©вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Apple Music ЩҲ Apple One
articles.summaryFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Ш§Щ…ШұЩҲШІ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ Ш§ШҙШӘШұШ§Ъ©вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Apple Music ШұШ§ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШҜШ§ШҜ. Ш§ШІ Ш§Щ…ШұЩҲШІШҢ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ Ш·ШұШӯ ЩҒШұШҜЫҢ Apple Music ШЁЩҮ ЫұЫұ.Ы№Ы№ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШӣ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҶШіШЁШӘ ШЁЩҮ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЫҢ ЫұЫ°.Ы№Ы№ ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұШҢ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ. ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶШҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ ШЁШұШ®ЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШЁШіШӘЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Apple One ШұШ§ ЩҶЫҢШІ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Apple has raised the price of its Apple Music subscriptions today. The Individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, up from the previous $10.99. In addition, Apple has increased the prices of select Apple One bundles as well.

articles.title: FridayвҖҷs Android app deals and freebies: Final Fantasy VII, Serial Cleaner, Parabellum, more
articles.content: Your Friday afternoon lineup of the best Android game and app deals has arrived, including titles like Foretales, Looking for Aliens, Serial Cleaner, Parabellum: Siege of Legends, FrogBoy, Zombie Night Terror, Final Fantasy VII, and more. Alongside the ongoing and rare price drop on the official Google Pixel Flex Dual Port 67W USB-C Fast Charger, we also have $300 price drops on Google Pixel 10 Pro and Galaxy Buds 4 Pro at up to $120 off , as well as AcerвҖҷs Chromebook Plus 516 laptop with Google AI down at $329 . The apps await below.

articles.title: Do you need to spend $250 on a kettle? I tested the Dualit Classic Kettle to find out
articles.content: The Dualit Classic Kettle is a decent option for anyone looking to buy one of the best electric kettles and who wants a sleek design in their kitchen. ItвҖҷs very quick to boil water and has great heat retention. The shape of the spout means that pouring is always reliable too, which is a real perk if youвҖҷve ever experienced the annoyance of spilt boiling water. However, itвҖҷs a bit pricey, and paying $249 for a kettle when you can pick one up for less than $30 from Walmart is a tall order. I also found the chrome finish picked up all kinds of watermarks and fingerprints, which was annoying, and itвҖҷs heavy to hold when pouring. To find out more about my experience during testing, you can keep reading this full Dualit Classic Kettle review. Dualit Classic Kettle review: Specs Price $249 / ВЈ159 Capacity 1.7L / 1.8LQT Rated power 1.5kW (U.S) / 3kW (U.K.) Colors Chrome, Sandstone, Sage, Matt Black, Brass, Copper, Eucalyptus, Evergreen, Shadow, Damson Dimensions 11.1 x 9.3 x 6.1 inches Weight 3.5 pounds Features Whisper boil, Pure pour, BPA-free Dualit Classic Kettle review: Price & availability The Dualit Classic Kettle is available from Amazon U.S. for $249 and from Amazon U.K. for ВЈ159 . The kettle comes in a variety of colors, including Chrome, Sandstone, Sage, Matt Black, Brass, Copper, Eucalyptus, Evergreen, Shadow and Damson. To check out all of these colors, you can head to the Dualit online store . This kettle is on the pricier side, but it boils water impeccably quickly and has an undeniably sleek look. The Cosori Electric Glass Kettle is nearly ten times cheaper at $27, but it struggles with leaks while pouring, which the Dualit does not. I think itвҖҷs worth spending more on a kettle you know will be reliable to avoid any spillages. If you want more control over your boiling to get the perfect temperature for your tea or coffee brewing, check out the Ninja Precision Temperature for $89. And for more control when pouring for things like pour-over coffee when using a Chemex , the $165 Fellow Stagg EKG gooseneck spout kettle is the way to go. Dualit Classic Kettle review: Design & features The Dualit Classic Kettle is more of a fashion statement than a home appliance. ItвҖҷs incredibly eye-catching because of the intense shine from the polished chrome finish. But you need to love looking at yourself because while watching the water boil, I had a very clear reflection for company. It measures 11.1 x 9.3 x 6.1 inches, so itвҖҷs the size of a traditional kettle, but a little taller than the Cosori Electric Glass Kettle. The Dualit weighs 3.5 pounds, which doesnвҖҷt sound like a lot, but when itвҖҷs filled with water, it gets heavy to hold. (Image credit: Tom's Guide) The hinged lid at the top is very sturdy and takes a bit of a pull to open it. I didnвҖҷt experience any issues with leakage around the lid when pouring. The plastic window on the side is BPA-free and is clearly denoted with measurements in cups on one side and in Liters on the other. The pour spout has a removable, washable limescale filter on the inside to make cleaning a little easier. ThereвҖҷs also a useful downward-facing lip to prevent drips from running down the body and making a mess. (Image credit: Tom's Guide) The base of the kettle has a 3-foot power cord, which is a great length as it doesnвҖҷt get in the way on the counter. The base itself is quite chunky but looks very cohesive with the rest of the kettle. To prevent overheating, the kettle has a boil-dry safety cut-off. Dualit Classic Kettle review: Performance Overall, I was impressed with the performance of the Dualit Classic Kettle вҖ” youвҖҷd like to think so too, given the $249 price tag. The kettle has patented Whisper Boil for quieter operation, but at 63dB, it was around the same volume as the Fellow Stagg EKG and the Ninja Precision Temperature. (Image credit: Tom's Guide) I was impressed by how quickly the kettle boiled water. When compared with the Cosori, it was much quicker. I tested the U.K. version ofвҖҰ

ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶШҢ ШёШ§ЩҮШұ Ъ©ШұЩҲЩ… ШўЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ Ш§Ш«Шұ Ш§ЩҶЪҜШҙШӘ ЩҲ Щ„Ъ©вҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ЩҲ ЩҲШІЩҶ ШўЩҶ ЩҮЩҶЪҜШ§Щ… ШұЫҢШ®ШӘЩҶ ШўШЁ Ъ©Щ…ЫҢ ШіЩҶЪҜЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: What Is Moonshot AI? Why ChinaвҖҷs New Model Is Roiling Markets
articles.content: Chinese startup Moonshot AI released a new artificial-intelligence model on July 17 that drew global attention both for its powerful capabilities and for sending stock markets reeling around the world.
articles.author: Bloomberg News
articles.fingerprint: china markets model moonshot roiling
articles.titleFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Moonshot: ЪҶЪҜЩҲЩҶЩҮ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШЁШ§ШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜШ§ШҜШҹ
articles.summaryFa: Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§Щҫ ЪҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ Moonshot AI ШҜШұ ШӘШ§ШұЫҢШ® ЫІЫ¶ ШӘЫҢШұ ЫұЫҙЫ°ЫІ ЫҢЪ© Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш¬Щ„ШЁ Ъ©ШұШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҶЩҮ ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШҜШұШӘЩ…ЩҶШҜШҙШҢ ШЁЩ„Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш®Ш§Ш·Шұ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШіЩҮШ§Щ… Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЪҜШ°Ш§ШҙШӘШҢ ШҙЩҶШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Chinese startup Moonshot AI launched a new artificial intelligence model on July 17, which garnered global attention for its powerful capabilities. Notably, it also had a significant impact on stock markets worldwide.
insightsFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Moonshot AI ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш¬Щ„ШЁ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШҜШ§ШұШ§ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®Ш§Шө Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШЁШІШұЪҜ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢЫҢ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШҜШұ Ш§ЩӮШӘШөШ§ШҜ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШұШҙШҜ Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§ЩҫвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҙШ§ШЁЩҮ ШҜШұ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ЩҲ ШіШ§ЫҢШұ Ъ©ШҙЩҲШұЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
ШӘШӯЩҲЩ„Ш§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶЩҲШіШ§ЩҶШ§ШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ ШҜШұ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢЫҢ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ШұЩҲШІШ§ЩҒШІЩҲЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ. ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶ ШӯШұЪ©ШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘШ§ШұШӘШ§ЩҫвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Щ…ШіЫҢШұ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: Anthropic is in early talks to lease $10 billion in compute from Meta
articles.content: Anthropic is in very preliminary talks to lease computing power from Meta in a deal worth about $10 billion, the New York Times reported on Friday. CNBC confirmed the early-stage discussions. The talks come weeks after Anthropic announced a $1.25 billion-per-month arrangement with SpaceX to use the Colossus 1 data centreвҖҷs Nvidia GPUs. The deal […] This story continues at The Next Web
articles.author: Ana Maria Constantin
articles.fingerprint: anthropic billion compute early lease meta talks
articles.titleFa: ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұЩҮ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШұЩҮ ЫұЫ° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҙ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШӘШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ
articles.summaryFa: ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШұЩҮ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҙ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШӘШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШұШІШҙЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯШҜЩҲШҜ ЫұЫ° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ ЩҫШі Ш§ШІ Ш§Ш№Щ„Ш§Щ… ЫҢЪ© ЩӮШұШ§ШұШҜШ§ШҜ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіЩҫЫҢШівҖҢШ§ЫҢЪ©Ші ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ GPU ЩҮШ§ЫҢ Nvidia ШҜШұ Щ…ШұЪ©ШІ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ Colossus 1 ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШұШІШҙ Ыұ.ЫІЫө Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ Щ…Ш§ЩҮ ШөЩҲШұШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Anthropic is in early talks to lease computing power from Meta in a deal worth approximately $10 billion. This comes after announcing a $1.25 billion-per-month agreement with SpaceX to utilize Nvidia GPUs from the Colossus 1 data centre.
insightsFa: ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШӘЩӮЩҲЫҢШӘ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҙЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩӮШұШ§ШұШҜШ§ШҜ ШЁШ§ Щ…ШӘШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩӮШ§Ш¶Ш§ЫҢ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҙ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҒЩӮ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіЩҫЫҢШівҖҢШ§ЫҢЪ©Ші ЩҲ Щ…ШӘШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢ ШӘЩҮШ§Ш¬Щ…ЫҢ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Щ„Ш§ЩҶ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮШҢ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШұШ§ ШЁЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮЩҲЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫЫҢШҙШұЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш№Щ„Ш§ЩҲЩҮ ШЁШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶШҢ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШұШ§ШӘЪҳЫҢ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ШҜШ§Щ…ЩҶЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: OpenAI fixes chat access in the ChatGPT app for Mac
articles.content: OpenAI has updated its recently redesigned ChatGPT app for Mac to make chats easier to access once again. Here are the details.
articles.author: Marcus Mendes
articles.fingerprint: access app chat chatgpt fixes mac openai
articles.titleFa: ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶ ChatGPT ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ъ©: ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЪҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§
articles.summaryFa: OpenAI Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶ ChatGPT Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ъ© ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ ШӘШ§ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЪҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜЩҲШЁШ§ШұЩҮ ШўШіШ§ЩҶвҖҢШӘШұ ШҙЩҲШҜ. Ш¬ШІШҰЫҢШ§ШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Щ…ЩҶШӘШҙШұ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: OpenAI has updated its recently redesigned ChatGPT app for Mac to improve access to chats once again. The details of this update have been released.
insightsFa: OpenAI ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ ЩҶШіШ®ЩҮ Щ…Ъ© Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶ ChatGPT Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ ШӘШіЩҮЫҢЩ„ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЪҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШЁШ§ШІШ®ЩҲШұШҜ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШөЩҲШұШӘ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ OpenAI ШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұЫҢ ЩҲ ШұЩҒШ№ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШҜШұ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ШЁЩҮШӘШұ ЩҲ ШіШұЫҢШ№вҖҢШӘШұ ШЁШ§ ШўЩҶ Ъ©Ш§Шұ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: I replaced my space heater and ceiling fan with one Dyson appliance

articles.title: Compute Exchange opens secondary market for used Nvidia H100 and A100 GPUs
articles.content: The Compute Exchange Inc., a procurement marketplace for reserved graphics processing unit capacity, today launched a dedicated marketplace for used and refurbished GPUs, extending the platform into physical artificial intelligence hardware sourcing. The service connects buyers with suppliers of older-generation Nvidia Corp. GPUs, particularly the H100 and A100, as enterprises, cloud providers and AI startups […] The post Compute Exchange opens secondary market for used Nvidia H100 and A100 GPUs appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
articles.author: Duncan Riley
articles.fingerprint: a100 compute exchange gpus h100 market nvidia opens secondary used
articles.titleFa: Compute Exchange ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш«Ш§ЩҶЩҲЫҢЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ GPUЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШіШӘвҖҢШҜЩҲЩ… Nvidia H100 ЩҲ A100 ШұШ§ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜ

articles.title: This Graduate Student Equips NASAвҖҷs Robots With Assembly Skills
articles.content: Like many engineers, Sarah Downs says she knew she wanted to pursue a STEM career from a young age. As a teenager, she discovered robotics through her Tulsa, Okla., middle schoolвҖҷs First Lego League team, and she fell in love with the field, she says. Downs participated in the international robotics program from 2014 to 2016. Watching PBS specials on NASA Mars rovers Spirit and Opportunity , and seeing the live broadcast of the Curiosity rover launch in 2011, inspired the teen to dream of a career working with NASA. Sarah Downs MEMBER GRADE Graduate student member UNIVERSITY Texas A&M University in College Station MAJOR Electric engineering This year the IEEE graduate student member achieved that dream. For her final project as a masterвҖҷs degree candidate in electrical engineering at the University of Tulsa , she worked on an algorithm in collaboration with NASA and the U.S. Air Force . The algorithm she developed enables a robot assembling satellites in space to insert an antenna into the correct spot, addressing roboticsвҖҷs classic peg-in-hole problem of inserting an object into its corresponding hole. Now a Ph.D. student in electrical engineering at Texas A&M University in College Station, Downs is continuing her research on satellite assembly and manipulation вҖңbut on a much larger scale,вҖқ she says. Following a childhood passion Downs grew up in the Tulsa area. Her father, who died from a heart attack in 2015 when she was 13, was a safety advisor in the oil and gas industry. Her mother stayed home to take care of her brother, who has autism. After her father died, her mother went back to college to earn a bachelorвҖҷs degree in business so she could support the family. вҖңWe didnвҖҷt have much income, and my mom was always worried about money,вҖқ Downs says. вҖңThat made me more aware of having a successful career, in a monetary sense.вҖқ From then on, whenever she considered her future career, having a decent salary to support the family was high on her list. By pursuing a career in robotics, she says, she can follow her passion while obtaining financial security. In high school, Downs joined the First robotics club, where she found herself drawn to the electrical components used in the machines she and her classmates built. During her final two years of high school, she participated in an extension program at Tulsa Tech , a training school. She spent half her day in high school classes and the other half taking engineering courses at the vocational school. After graduating in 2020, she accepted scholarships to attend the University of Tulsa. She began her freshman year at UTulsa not knowing whether she wanted to major in electrical or mechanical engineering, she says, adding that her love of working with small systems helped her choose EE. For her senior year capstone project , she and two of her classmates designed a lunar lander exhibit for the Tulsa Air and Space Museum . They created an interactive game that simulates missions on lunar and martian surfaces. Four celestial bodiesвҖ”the moon, Venus, Mars, and TitanвҖ”are listed across three computer monitors. Using a game controller, museum visitors can explore the virtual surface of each one. The exhibit is still on display. Downs earned her bachelorвҖҷs degree in electrical engineering in 2024 and continued her education at the universityвҖҷs EE masterвҖҷs degree program. Both more and less complicated than people think When Downs began her graduate studies, she was supposed to be part of a NASA robotics project for two years. But when a delay in government funding postponed the projectвҖҷs start, she instead spent her first year in the schoolвҖҷs Institute for Robotics and Autonomy , then newly launched. Its main focus is developing robots to assist people who have mobility challenges. Inspired by her grandmother, who was wheelchair-bound due to severe arthritis, Downs developed a robotic arm that helps older people and wheelchair users live independently. articles.title: FBI Arrests Man Accused of Using Steam Games To Drain Victims' Crypto Wallets
articles.content: The FBI arrested a Florida man accused of uploading fake Steam games containing malware that stole passwords, data, and cryptocurrency wallet credentials from victims. Prosecutors say the scheme infected about 8,000 people, compromised roughly 80 crypto wallets, and stole at least $220,000 through games that appeared legitimate but secretly carried malware. TechCrunch reports: On Tuesday, the FBI arrested Zyaire Wilkins, a 21-year-old Florida resident and student. On Wednesday, prosecutors accused him and a number of unnamed co-conspirators of hacking crimes. Over the past two years, Wilkins and his partners allegedly published several malware-laden video games on Steam, including BlockBlasters, Dashverse, Lampy, Lunara, and PirateFi. Using that malware, says the FBI, Wilkins and his accomplices infected around 8,000 victims, and then hacked around 80 cryptocurrency wallets to steal at least $220,000 worth of crypto. Wilkins and the others marketed their malicious video games on Discord, LinkedIn, and Telegram, according to the authorities. [...] After the FBI identified another person involved in the crimes, according to the complaint, federal agents interviewed them. The unnamed person said they worked with other people to raise money to launch and market the malicious games in return for sharing some of the stolen cryptocurrency. The FBI identified a specific crypto account involved in the scheme, and then traced cryptocurrency payments made with that account to buy several gift cards, including for UberEats. After subpoenaing Uber, the feds were able to see that the gift cards were linked to an account that made deliveries to Wilkins, who went by the nickname Sibel.eth online, according to the complaint. The feds then got a search warrant for Wilkins' residence, where they seized his MacBook laptop, cellphones, other devices, and digital wallets. According to the complaint, he refused to speak or answer any questions. Read more of this story at Slashdot.

articles.title: The Corsair Sabre V2 Pro is a mighty impressive mouse with 8,000Hz polling and 33,000 DPI вҖ” hereвҖҷs why itвҖҷs great for FPS and esports
articles.content: The Corsair Sabre V2 Pro is one of the best gaming mice you can buy. Lightweight yet sturdy, the Sabre V2 Pro is built well and is comfortable for long gaming sessions. ItвҖҷs biggest selling point is its 8,000Hz polling rate over wired and wireless connections, and 33,000 DPI вҖ” more than plenty to give you a competitive advantage. Alongside all this, you also get web-based companion software that makes customization a walk in the park. The best part is that the Sabre V2 Pro does all this without breaking the bank, making it a great alternative to expensive flagships from the likes of Razer. But the mouse isnвҖҷt perfect, as battery life isnвҖҷt the strongest, and thereвҖҷs no dedicated DPI button. For the complete breakdown, read my full Corsair Sabre V2 Pro review. Corsair Sabre V2 Pro review: Specs Price $99 / ВЈ89 Max DPI 33,000 Polling rate 8,000Hz Buttons 5 Size 4.69 x 2.46 x 1.5 inches Weight 1.26oz Colors Black, White Body material Plastic Design Right-handed Operating system Windows, macOS Connectivity Wired, 2.4GHz dongle Battery Rechargeable Battery life (rated) 70 hours (1,000Hz) | 16 hours (8,000Hz) Lighting No Corsair Sabre V2 Pro review: Cheat sheet What is it? An ultralight gaming mouse with 33,000 DPI and 8,000Hz polling Who is it for? Competitive gamers and FPS players How much does it cost? The Corsair Sabre V2 Pro is available for $99 / ВЈ89 What do we like? The ultralight yet sturdy design, excellent gaming performance, web-based software, and competitive price What donвҖҷt we like? The poor battery life and absence of a dedicated DPI switch Corsair Sabre V2 Pro review: The ups The Corsair Sabre V2 Pro is ultralight and comfortable, and it boasts powerful gaming performance across a range of genres. Ultralight yet sturdy (Image credit: Tom's Guide) Ultralight mice are all the rage today. They require less physical force to move and stop, which increases agility and results in faster cursor speeds, and even proves less fatiguing during long gaming sessions. As a competitive gaming mouse, the Corsair Sabre V2 Pro is extremely light вҖ” one of the lightest mice IвҖҷve tested, in fact. It weighs just 1.26oz, so itвҖҷs lighter than the Keychron M3 Mini V2 8K ($69, 1.94oz), the Keychron M3 Mini 4K Metal ($99, 2.25oz) and the phenomenal Razer Viper V4 Pro ($159, 1.72oz). What I love about the Sabre V2 Pro is that even though itвҖҷs as light as a feather, itвҖҷs still extremely sturdy, and it feels substantial under my palm. ItвҖҷs made of high-quality plastic that looks and feels premium, and its skates effortlessly glide across most surfaces. Corsair happily includes spare skates in the box just in case the originals wear down. Also included in the box are pieces of anti-slip grip tape, which can be stuck to the mouse for additional grip. Speaking of, the Sabre V2 Pro on its own feels very grippy. I didnвҖҷt feel like my fingers were slipping at any point during testing. The mouse measures 4.69 x 2.46 x 1.5 inches and I found it suitable for my medium-sized hands вҖ” although if youвҖҷve got big hands, you may find the mouse a tad small (unless youвҖҷre a claw gripper). A chunkier mouse like the Fnatic x Lamzu Maya X 8K ($119) or the Keychron M6 8K ($69) may be more suitable, if thatвҖҷs the case. Excellent gaming performance (Image credit: Tom's Guide) Tom's Guide reviews gaming platform Supplied by: MSI | Tom's Guide CPU: AMD Ryzen 7 7700X | Graphics card: MSI RTX 5070 Ti 16GB Vanguard SOC | Motherboard: MSI B850E Gaming Plus WiFi | RAM: Kingston Fury Renegade DDR5 32GB | Cooler: Noctua NH-U12S | PSU: MSI MEG Ai1300P PCIE5 | Case: MSI MPG GUNGNIR 110R The Corsair Sabre V2 Pro is a gaming mouse, and a good one at that. Packing a maximum of 33,000 DPI and 8,000Hz polling over wired and 2.4GHz wireless connections, the Sabre V2 Pro is an excellent choice for esports and FPS gamers. The polling rate can be dropped down to 1,000Hz using the mouseвҖҷs companion software (more on that shortly) if battery life is a concern (more on that later, too). AsвҖҰ

articles.title: I tested the Edifier MR5 вҖ” and theyвҖҷre budget-friendly studio monitors with energy and clarity
articles.content: The Edifier MR5 are some of the best budget-friendly studio monitors IвҖҷve ever tested, and certainly some of the best speakers you can buy these days. Although theyвҖҷre not quite as precise as pricier alternatives вҖ” think the Cambridge L/R S ($459) or Dali Kupid ($600), theyвҖҷre excellent for well under $400. With a veritable feast of connectivity types вҖ” weвҖҷre talking Aux, RCA, TRS, XLR, and Bluetooth вҖ” and a litany of customization options in the Edifier ConneX app, these monitors will be great for any content creator, music producer, or even casual listener. Heavier music fans might be better with the much cheaper Edifier R1280T or the Edifier S880DB MKII, but acoustic, pop, and electronic fans need look no further. So, are the Edifier MR5 worth their salt, or can you find better seasoning elsewhere? Short answer: worth it. But to find out the full story, keep reading this Edifier MR5 review. Edifier MR5 review: Specs Price $349 / ВЈ279 Connectivity Bluetooth, AUX, RCA, TRS, XLR Frequency response rate 46Hz - 40,000 Subwoofer out No Weight 22.9 pounds Dimensions 11 x 6.3 x 10.4 inches Colors Black, white Edifier MR5 review: Price & availability (Image credit: Tom's Guide) The Edifier MR5 are $349 from Amazon U.S. and ВЈ279 from Amazon U.K . This makes them considerably more expensive than the Edifier R1280T ($149), my personal turntable speakers, and the Edifier M60 ($199). If youвҖҷre on a budget, IвҖҷd recommend getting one of those speakers instead вҖ” theyвҖҷre cheaper, but still sound awesome. ThatвҖҷs not to say the MR5 arenвҖҷt worth it вҖ” they are вҖ” but every penny helps, right? IвҖҷve also tested the mid-range Cambridge L/R S ($549) and the premium-on-a-budget Edifier S880DB MKII ($449), both of which are your more punchy, louder speakers rather than the accurate studio-monitor-sound-profile of the MR5. Edifier MR5 review: Design (Image credit: Tom's Guide) The Edifier MR5 look like your standard Edifier speakers. TheyвҖҷre quite large, at 10 inches high, 6 inches wide, and 11 inches deep. I wouldnвҖҷt recommend them for small setups or for people who value a pretty speaker. They donвҖҷt look as striking as the Edifier S880DB MKII, so IвҖҷd recommend those if you want an eye-catching speaker. Alternatively, IвҖҷd recommend the DALI Kupid ($600) (but you need an AVR), or the Kanto UKI ($269). Controls (Image credit: Tom's Guide) The MR5 have just one on-speaker control. YouвҖҷve got the volume dial on the front of the speaker and a headphone output. Of course, you can also control the speakers via EdifierвҖҷs ConneX app, which IвҖҷll discuss in more detail below. YouвҖҷve got a treble and bass (lovingly named вҖңhighвҖқ and вҖңlowвҖқ) on the back of the speaker, but IвҖҷd like to see these knobs on the front in future. ItвҖҷs quite annoying to turn the speakers around just to increase bass. Connectivity (Image credit: Tom's Guide) If you need studio monitors that can do it all, the MR5 might just be it. On top of the standard RCA and aux connectivity ports, the MR5 also have Bluetooth, RCA, and even XLR. This is ideal for studio musicians or home producers who need the highest possible quality. If youвҖҷre using broadcast mics, then youвҖҷll need an XLR connection for the best quality. Unfortunately, the MR5 donвҖҷt have USB-C listening. I found connecting to Bluetooth and Aux easy. ThereвҖҷs really nothing else for me to say! These speakers work as advertised. The array of connectivity types might be a little too much for casual home listeners вҖ” in that case, IвҖҷd recommend the aforementioned R1280T. Edifier MR5 review: App (Image credit: Tom's Guide) As with other Edifier products like the M60 and the S880DB MKII, the MR5 are compatible with the ConneX app. You have to connect via Bluetooth to be able to control the speakers, but the speakers should remember your settings for analog listening sessions. When I first unboxed the speakers, I had to update the firmware. This took 10 minutes, so bear in mind if you wanted to get listening straight away. In terms of the appвҖҰ

articles.summaryEn: The Edifier MR5 is considered one of the best budget-friendly studio monitors, offering good sound quality for under $400. With a variety of connectivity options and the Edifier ConneX app for customization, these speakers are excellent for content creators and casual listeners alike. Despite cheaper alternatives, the MR5 still holds great value for its price.

articles.title: HollowByte DDoS flaw bloats OpenSSL server memory with 11-byte payload
articles.content: A vulnerability dubbed HollowByte allows unauthenticated attackers to trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition on OpenSSL servers with a malicious payload of just 11 bytes. [...]
articles.author: Bill Toulas
articles.fingerprint: bloats byte ddos flaw hollowbyte memory openssl payload server
articles.titleFa: Ш¶Ш№ЩҒ HollowByteШҢ ШӯШ§ЩҒШёЩҮ ШіШұЩҲШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ OpenSSL ШұШ§ ШЁШ§ ШЁШ§ШұЪҜЫҢШұ ЫұЫұ ШЁШ§ЫҢШӘЫҢ ЩҫШұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ЫҢЪ© ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШ§Щ… HollowByte ШЁЩҮ ШӯЩ…Щ„ЩҮвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ ШәЫҢШұЩ…Ш№ШӘШЁШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШұШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ШЁШ§ШұЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Щ…Ш®ШұШЁ ЫұЫұ ШЁШ§ЫҢШӘЫҢШҢ ШҙШұШ§ЫҢШ· Ш§ЩҶЪ©Ш§Шұ ШіШұЩҲЫҢШі (DoS) ШұШ§ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ ШіШұЩҲШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ OpenSSL Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ ШіШұЩҲШұ ЩҲ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ш§Ш®ШӘЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: A vulnerability named HollowByte allows unauthenticated attackers to trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition on OpenSSL servers with a malicious payload of just 11 bytes. This issue can lead to server performance degradation and disruption of services.
insightsFa: ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ HollowByte ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш№ШӘШЁШ§Шұ ШіЩҶШ¬ЫҢ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ЩҶШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
ШӯЩ…Щ„ЩҮ ШЁШ§ ШЁШ§ШұЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ ЫұЫұ ШЁШ§ЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҙЪ©Щ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮЫҢ ШЁШұ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ ШіШұЩҲШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ OpenSSL ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
Щ…Ш¬ШұЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ШіШ§ЫҢШЁШұЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ШіЩҲШЎШ§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
Ш¶ШұЩҲШұШӘ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ OpenSSL ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш¬Щ„ЩҲЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШӯЩ…Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӯШӘЩ…Щ„.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ ЩҫШұЩҲШӘЪ©Щ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш®ШӘЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШҜШұ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ЩҲ ШӯЩ…Щ„Ш§ШӘ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҜЩҮ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ. OpenSSL ЫҢЪ©ЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§Ш¬ШІШ§ЫҢ ШӯЫҢШ§ШӘЫҢ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶШӘШұЩҶШӘЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ ШўШіЫҢШЁ ШЁЩҮ ШўЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш№ЩҲШ§ЩӮШЁ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.

articles.title: I just tested the MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W вҖ” and its effective cooling system and 240Hz refresh rate make it one of the finest gaming laptops today
articles.content: To be considered one of the best gaming laptops , a PC must do a few things: pack a high refresh rate for smooth gameplay; make colors look vibrant; and it shouldnвҖҷt overheat. The MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W does all that and more. Boasting excellent color coverage and accuracy, as well as a 240Hz refresh rate, the Crosshair 16 HX packs performance in spades. The Crosshair 16 HX also features an effective, albeit extremely loud, cooling system that ensures the laptop doesnвҖҷt get too hot during intensive games. All of these goodies are packed into a lovely, premium and practical design вҖ” but it also houses the laptopвҖҷs mediocre battery life, which puts a damper on things. For the complete breakdown, read my full MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W review. MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W review: Specs Specs MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W (starting) MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W (top level configuration and tested) Price TBC TBC CPU Intel Core i7 processor 14650HX Intel Core i7 processor 14650HX GPU NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Display 16-inch (2560 x 1600), Non-Touch, 2.5K, 240Hz 16-inch (2560 x 1600), Non-Touch, 2.5K, 240Hz Memory 16GB 16GB Storage 512GB 1TB Ports 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 (DisplayPort / 100W Power Delivery 3.0), 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 1x HDMI 2.1, 1x RJ45 ethernet, 1x 3.5mm audio jack, 1x power adapter 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 (DisplayPort / 100W Power Delivery 3.0), 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 1x HDMI 2.1, 1x RJ45 ethernet, 1x 3.5mm audio jack, 1x power adapter Connectivity Intel Wi-Fi 6E AX211, Bluetooth 5.3 Intel Wi-Fi 6E AX211, Bluetooth 5.3 Size 14.29 x 10.62 x 1.01 inches 14.29 x 10.62 x 1.01 inches Weight 5.07lbs 5.07lbs MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W review: Cheat sheet What is it? A 16-inch gaming laptop boasting excellent performance across the board Who is it for? Gamers looking for a high-performing Windows laptop How much does it cost? At the time of writing, pricing hasnвҖҷt been confirmed What do we like? The great design, outstanding performance, 240Hz refresh rate for gaming, effective cooling system, and great color coverage and accuracy What donвҖҷt we like? The loud fans and mediocre battery life MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W review: The ups From its premium design and excellent color coverage to its superb gaming performance and effective cooling system, the MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W is a formidable laptop. Gorgeous design with plenty of ports (Image credit: Tom's Guide) If you took one look at the MSI Crosshair 16 HX E14W, youвҖҷd instantly know itвҖҷs a gaming laptop, through and through. MSIвҖҷs dragon shield emblem in teal with a subtle reflective finish graces the laptopвҖҷs matte lid, adding a pop of color to the black notebook. I love the aluminum chassis too, which feels and looks premium. The Crosshair 16 HX E14W is fitted with a 16-inch (2560 x 1600) non-touch screen and the laptop itself measures 14.29 x 10.62 x 1.01 inches вҖ” ever-so slightly bigger than the Dell Alienware 16X Aurora ($2,499) and the Dell XPS 16 ($1,749). It fits easily into most regular-sized laptop backpacks, and IвҖҷve had no issues carrying it to and from work. It weighs 5.07 pounds, so while it isnвҖҷt as light as the MacBook Pro M5 (3.4 pounds, $1,599), it isnвҖҷt as bulky as the 5.66lbs Alienware 16X Aurora. (Image credit: Tom's Guide) IвҖҷve tested many MSI monitors and IвҖҷve come to expect great build quality from the Taiwanese company вҖ” this applies to the Crosshair 16 HX too, from the overall construction to its keyboard and trackpad. The low-profile keyboard is a sheer delight to type on, with each keyвҖҷs total travel distance of 1.7mm making typing fatigue-free. It also features four-zone RGB which can be customized via pre-installed software вҖ” and it further reaffirms that this is a Gaming laptop, with a capital G. I found the trackpad responsive and comfortable in my testing too. (Image credit: Tom's Guide) The Crosshair 16 HX doesnвҖҷt give you much to complain about in the I/O and ports department, either. The laptopвҖҷs leftвҖҰ

articles.title: EY re-envisions RAG around multimodal knowledge graphs to improve accuracy
articles.content: Retrieval-augmented generation is a standard way to ground large language models in enterprise information, but new research from EY, the business name of Ernst & Young LLP, says most implementations overlook a lot of useful data. Conventional RAG systems are built mainly to retrieve text. Enterprise documents, however, often place critical facts in charts, tables, […] The post EY re-envisions RAG around multimodal knowledge graphs to improve accuracy appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
articles.author: Paul Gillin
articles.fingerprint: accuracy around envisions graphs improve knowledge multimodal rag
articles.titleFa: EY ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ: ШӘШ¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ЩҶШёШұ ШҜШұ RAG ШЁШ§ Щ…ШӯЩҲШұЫҢШӘ ЪҜШұШ§ЩҒвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШ§ЩҶШҙ Щ…ЩҲЩ„ШӘЫҢвҖҢЩ…ШҜШ§Щ„ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШҜЩӮШӘ
articles.summaryFa: ШӘШӯЩӮЫҢЩӮШ§ШӘ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ EY ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШіЩҶШӘЫҢ ШӘЩҲЩ„ЫҢШҜ Щ…ШЁШӘЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢШ§ШЁЫҢ (RAG) Щ…Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҒЫҢШҜЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҜШұ ЩҶЩ…ЩҲШҜШ§ШұЩҮШ§ ЩҲ Ш¬ШҜШ§ЩҲЩ„ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ЩҶЩ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ. EY ШЁШ§ ШӘШ¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ЩҶШёШұ ШҜШұ RAG ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ЪҜШұШ§ЩҒвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШ§ЩҶШҙ Щ…ЩҲЩ„ШӘЫҢвҖҢЩ…ШҜШ§Щ„ШҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШҜЩӮШӘ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: New research from EY reveals that traditional Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems often overlook useful data found in charts and tables. EY aims to enhance accuracy and effectiveness by re-envisioning RAG around multimodal knowledge graphs.
insightsFa: ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ RAG ШіЩҶШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Щ…ШӘЩҶ ШӘЩ…ШұЪ©ШІ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ.
ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШҜШұ ЩҶЩ…ЩҲШҜШ§ШұЩҮШ§ ЩҲ Ш¬ШҜШ§ЩҲЩ„ Щ…Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ЩҶШ§ШҜЫҢШҜЩҮ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.
EY Ш§ШІ ЪҜШұШ§ЩҒвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШ§ЩҶШҙ Щ…ЩҲЩ„ШӘЫҢвҖҢЩ…ШҜШ§Щ„ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШҜЩӮШӘ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұЩҲЫҢЪ©ШұШҜ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ Ъ©Ш§ШұШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШІШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
ШӘШӯЩӮЫҢЩӮШ§ШӘ EY Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұ ЩҶШӯЩҲЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ШӘШөЩ…ЫҢЩ…вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶШӘЩҮЫҢ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: Brex built its AI agent policy by watching what agents actually do, not by writing rules first
articles.content: OpenClaw has become one of the most widely adopted agentic frameworks, but it has yet to prove itself at enterprise scale. Agents need real credentials вҖ” API keys, OAuth tokens, service accounts вҖ” to work effectively, and Brex found that traditional guardrails couldn't contain what those agents were doing with them. Brex set out to overcome these limitations by building an internal platform it calls CrabTrap. The open-source HTTP/HTTPS proxy intercepts all network traffic, examines policy rules, and uses a LLM-as-a-judge to decide whether agent requests should be approved or denied. вҖңWhat we noticed was that the network layer was an untapped enforcement point,вҖқ Brex co-founder and CEO Pedro Franceschi told VentureBeat. вҖңEvery request an agent makes is an opportunity to intercept, reason about, and make a policy decision.вҖқ The takeaway Franceschi wants IT leaders to draw: agent governance should shift from SDK-level permissions and model guardrails toward a centralized network control plane that enforces and learns from real in-the-wild agent behavior. How Brex targeted the transport layer The вҖңobvious fixвҖқ (at least initially) to the agent security gap was guardrails, and much of the early work has centered on scoped tools, per-action permissions, and human-in-the-loop approvals. But as agents evolve, each new capability means thereвҖҷs another API to tune or surface to audit, Franceschi noted. вҖңAny agentic system with multiple tools and access to the open internet creates an immediate tension for builders: The more capable you make an agent, the more dangerous it becomes, and the safer you make it, the less useful it is,вҖқ he said. Existing solutions to this tradeoff were вҖңweakвҖқ: Fine-grained API tokens help at the margins but can still be misused and constrain functionality. Semantic guardrails (such as context, skills, or prompt steering) are easily bypassed by prompt injection, especially for agents connected to the internet. Agents can be вҖңdefangedвҖқ when given read-only access or limited toolsets, but then they can't do meaningful work, Franceschi said. On the other hand, granting broad write access and a large tool surface can result in hallucinations and real production consequences. Model context protocol (MCP) gateways enforce policy at the protocol layer вҖ” but only for traffic using MCP. Meanwhile, guardrails from LLM providers are tied to a single model and can be вҖңopaqueвҖқ to customize with enterprise-specific policies. And powerful tools like Nvidia OpenShell offer more of a вҖңper-sandbox egress control.вҖқ вҖңWhen we started, we hadnвҖҷt found a solution to deploying harnesses like OpenClaw safely,вҖқ Franceschi said. вҖңInstead of waiting for the industry to catch up, we decided to own the problem and invent the necessary tools.вҖқ Notably, they needed a platform that sat between every agent and every network request, and could make вҖңnuanced decisions about what to allow,вҖқ he said. This made the transport layer a core architectural component and natural starting point, he said. By operating at this layer, CrabTrap is framework-agnostic, language-agnostic, and API-agnostic. It doesn't require SDK wrappers or per-tool integration. Users set HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY in the agent's environment, and every outbound request routes through the proxy before it reaches a destination. However, Franceschi emphasized, Brex didn't start at the transport layer because it thought it was the only answer; rather, they believe in вҖңsecurity by layers.вҖқ вҖңThe transport layer was simply an underinvested one, and we saw an opportunity to add meaningful enforcement there alongside everything else,вҖқ he said. The LLM-as-a-judge training loop CrabTrap combines deterministic static rules with an LLM-as-a-judge for requests that fall outside known patterns, Franceschi explained. The judge only вҖңfires on the long tail of unfamiliar endpoints or unusual request shapes,вҖқ which for a mature agent is typically fewer than 3% ofвҖҰ
articles.author: taryn.plumb@venturebeat.com (Taryn Plumb)

articles.titleFa: Brex ШіЫҢШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШЁШ§ Щ…ШҙШ§ЩҮШҜЩҮ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ
articles.summaryFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Brex ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜШҢ ШұЩҲЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЪ© ШұШ§ Ш§ШӘШ®Ш§Ш° Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш¬Ш§ЫҢ ЩҶЩҲШҙШӘЩҶ ЩӮЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЩҶ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШЁШӘШҜШ§ШҢ ШЁШұ Ш§ШіШ§Ші ШұЩҒШӘШ§Шұ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. ШўЩҶЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… ШҜШ§Ш®Щ„ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШ§Щ… CrabTrapШҢ ШӘЩ…Ш§Щ… ШӘШұШ§ЩҒЫҢЪ© ШҙШЁЪ©ЩҮ ШұШ§ ШұШөШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШ¬ШІЫҢЩҮ ЩҲ ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІЩҶШҜ ШӘШ§ ШӘШөЩ…ЫҢЩ…Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШұШӘШЁШ· ШЁШ§ ШіЫҢШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ Ш§ШӘШ®Ш§Ш° Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұЩҲШҙ ШЁЩҮ ITЩҮШ§ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ ШӘШ§ ШіЫҢШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӯШ§Ъ©Щ…ЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш·ЩҲШұ Щ…ШӨШ«ШұШӘШұЫҢ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Brex has adopted a new approach to managing its AI agents by designing policies based on the actual behavior of agents rather than writing rules first. They use an internal platform called CrabTrap to monitor all network traffic and analyze agent requests to make policy decisions. This method aids IT leaders in effectively managing agent governance.
insightsFa: Brex ШЁШ§ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ЫҢЪ© ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… ШҜШ§Ш®Щ„ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҶШ§Щ… CrabTrapШҢ ШЁЩҮ ШұШөШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұЩҲЫҢЪ©ШұШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш¬Ш§ЫҢ ЩӮЩҲШ§ЩҶЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮШҢ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ ШұЩҒШӘШ§Шұ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШӘШЈЪ©ЫҢШҜ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ЫҢШ§ШҜЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Щ…Ш§ШҙЫҢЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘШөЩ…ЫҢЩ…вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ ШӘШЈЫҢЫҢШҜ ЫҢШ§ ШұШҜ ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§
ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Щ„Ш§ЫҢЩҮ ШҙШЁЪ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЩҶЩӮШ·ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Щ„ЫҢШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§Ш¬ШұШ§ЫҢ ШіЫҢШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§
ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ§ШІЩҶ Щ…ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬ЩҶШӘ ЩҲ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШўЩҶ

articles.content: A newly renewed, $25 million-per-year contract with a subsidiary of Thomson Reuters further expands the power of ICE under the Trump administration.

articles.titleFa: Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ ICE Ш§ШІ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ШЁЫҢвҖҢШіШұЩҫШұШіШӘ ЩҲ ШӘЩӮЩ„ШЁ
articles.summaryFa: ЩӮШұШ§ШұШҜШ§ШҜ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШұШІШҙ ЫІЫө Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ ШіШ§Щ„ ШЁШ§ ЫҢЪ©ЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШІЫҢШұЩ…Ш¬Щ…ЩҲШ№ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Thomson ReutersШҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ICE ШҜШұ ШҜЩҲШұШ§ЩҶ ШұЫҢШ§ШіШӘвҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұЫҢ ШӘШұШ§Щ…Щҫ Ш§ЩҒШІЩҲШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… ШЁЩҮ ICE Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШІЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ШӘШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬Щ…Ш№вҖҢШўЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜШҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ШәЫҢШұЩҮЩ…ШұШ§ЩҮ ЩҲ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ§ШұШҜ ШӘЩӮЩ„ШЁ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: A newly renewed, $25 million-per-year contract with a subsidiary of Thomson Reuters expands ICE's powers under the Trump administration. This agreement allows ICE to access more data collection tools, aiding in the identification of unaccompanied minors and fraud cases.
insightsFa: ЩӮШұШ§ШұШҜШ§ШҜ ЫІЫө Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұЫҢ Ш§ШұШІШҙ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ICE ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩӮШұШ§ШұШҜШ§ШҜ ШЁЩҮ ЩӮШҜШұШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ICE ШҜШұ Ш¬Щ…Ш№вҖҢШўЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҜ.
ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш§Ш«ШұШ§ШӘ Ш§Ш¬ШӘЩ…Ш§Ш№ЫҢ ЩҲ ШӯЩӮЩҲЩӮ ШЁШҙШұЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҙШ®Шө Ш«Ш§Щ„Ш« ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§ЩҶ ШәЫҢШұЩҮЩ…ШұШ§ЩҮ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШІ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ШұЫҢШ§ШіШӘвҖҢШ¬Щ…ЩҮЩҲШұЫҢ ШӘШұШ§Щ…Щҫ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ICE ШҜШұ Ш¬Щ…Ш№вҖҢШўЩҲШұЫҢ ЩҲ ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ШіШӘШҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШЁШ№Ш§ШӘ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұ ШӯЩӮЩҲЩӮ ШЁШҙШұ ЩҲ ШӯШұЫҢЩ… Ш®ШөЩҲШөЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.

articles.title: How AppleвҖҷs big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAIвҖҷs IPO plans
articles.content: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware officer and claims more than 400 former Apple employees now work at the company. OpenAI’s response so far has been carefully hedged, and the timing couldn’t be worse with the company reportedly eyeing an IPO […]
articles.author: Theresa Loconsolo
articles.fingerprint: apple big could disrupt ipo lawsuit openai plans
articles.titleFa: ШҜШ№ЩҲШ§ЫҢ ШЁШІШұЪҜ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ IPO Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ШұШ§ Щ…Ш®ШӘЩ„ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШұЩҲШІ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ЩҮ ЫҢЪ© ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ ШӯЩӮЩҲЩӮЫҢ ШҜШұШЁШ§ШұЩҮ Ш§ШіШұШ§Шұ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұЫҢ Ш№Щ„ЫҢЩҮ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ШӘЩҶШёЫҢЩ… Ъ©ШұШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ Ш§ШҜШ№Ш§ЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШҜШұШЁШ§ШұЩҮ ШұЩҒШӘШ§Шұ ЩҶШ§ШҜШұШіШӘ Ш§Ш№Ш¶Ш§ЫҢ Ш§ШұШҙШҜ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ Ш§ШҜШ№Ш§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ ЫҙЫ°Ы° Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ…ЩҶШҜ ШіШ§ШЁЩӮ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ Щ…ШҙШәЩҲЩ„ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЪ©Ш§Шұ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢШЁЩҶШҜЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜШ№ЩҲЫҢ ШҜШұ ШўШіШӘШ§ЩҶЩҮ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢШұЫҢШІЫҢ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҲШұЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШЁЩҲШұШі ШЁШіЫҢШ§Шұ ШЁШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, alleging serious misconduct involving senior members of the company. The complaint claims that over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI, and the timing couldn't be worse as the company reportedly plans to go public soon.
insightsFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ Ш№Щ„ЫҢЩҮ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ШӘЩҶШёЫҢЩ… Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ Ш§ШҜШ№Ш§ЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұШЁШ§ШұЩҮ ШұЩҒШӘШ§Шұ ЩҶШ§ШҜШұШіШӘ Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШұШҙШҜ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
ШЁЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ ЫҙЫ°Ы° Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ…ЩҶШҜ ШіШ§ШЁЩӮ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ Щ…ШҙШәЩҲЩ„ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЪ©Ш§ШұЩҶШҜ.
ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢШЁЩҶШҜЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҜШ№Ш§ЩҲЫҢ ШҜШұ ШўШіШӘШ§ЩҶЩҮ IPO Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШўЩҲШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұШ§ШӘ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Ш§ШӯШӘЩ…Ш§Щ„ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Щ…ЩҶЩҒЫҢ ШЁШұ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ IPO Ш§ЩҲЩҫЩҶвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢ ШЁШіЫҢШ§Шұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘШҢ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҲШұЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШЁЩҲШұШі ШЁШ§ ЪҶШ§Щ„ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҙШҜ.

articles.summaryFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЫҢШ§ШӘ ЩҶЩҲЫҢШіЩҶШҜЩҮ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШұШ§ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІЫҢ SQLite Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҜ. ЩҶЩҲЫҢШіЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҶЪ©Ш§ШӘ ЩҲ ЪҶШ§Щ„ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш®ШӘЩ„ЩҒЫҢ ШұШ§ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҒШұШўЫҢЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘШҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШҙШӘШұШ§Ъ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ.

articles.title: Apple's long-running DOJ antitrust case may not make it to trial
articles.content: Apple and the US Justice Department are reportedly in early discussions over the potential for an early settlement of a 2024 iPhone antitrust suit brought about by the Biden administration. Apple hasn't taken the antitrust case lying down. Image source: Apple While discussions are reportedly underway, it's unclear whether an agreement will be reached. However, the Trump administration has been working to settle lawsuits brought by the previous administration for some time. It's clear there is a willingness to do a deal from their side. Apple is also keen. Bloomberg reports that Apple has already made multiple offers to the Justice Department in 2026 alone. The company previously lost a bid to dismiss the antitrust lawsuit in 2025. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums
articles.author: Oliver Haslam and Mike Wuerthele <news@appleinsider.com>
articles.fingerprint: antitrust apple case doj long make may not running trial
articles.titleFa: Щ…Ш¬ШЁЩҲШұ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШіЩҲЫҢЩҮвҖҢ ШӯШіШ§ШЁ ШІЩҲШҜЩҮЩҶЪҜШ§Щ… ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ Ш§ЩҫЩ„
articles.summaryFa: ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШӯШ§Ъ©ЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШўЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҲ ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ЫҢЪ© ШӘШіЩҲЫҢЩҮвҖҢ ШӯШіШ§ШЁ ШІЩҲШҜЩҮЩҶЪҜШ§Щ… ШҜШұ ЩҫШұЩҲЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш¶ШҜ Ш§ЩҶШӯШөШ§Шұ Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· ШЁЩҮ ШўЫҢЩҒЩҲЩҶ ШҜШұ ШіШ§Щ„ ЫІЫ°ЫІЫҙ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ. ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… Ш§ШіШӘШҢ ЩҮЩҶЩҲШІ Щ…ШҙШ®Шө ЩҶЫҢШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШўЫҢШ§ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҒЩӮЫҢ ШӯШ§ШөЩ„ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҙШҜ ЫҢШ§ Ш®ЫҢШұ. Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҫЫҢШҙШӘШұ ЩҫЫҢШҙЩҶЩҮШ§ШҜШ§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШ№ШҜШҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Reports indicate that Apple and the U.S. Department of Justice are in early discussions about a potential early settlement concerning a 2024 antitrust suit related to the iPhone. While negotiations are underway, it's unclear if an agreement will be reached. Apple has already made multiple offers to the Justice Department in 2026 alone.

articles.title: AppleвҖҷs plot to crush OpenAI
articles.content: Apple is suing OpenAI . The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done . So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI? On this episode of The Vergecast , Nilay and David go through the lawsuit, and look at Apple's history of splashy litigation to determine whether Apple is worried about a possible competitor or simply looking to capitalize on a weak moment for OpenAI . All this is happening as Apple ships the public betas of its new software, headlined by the new Siri AI , and we have though вҖҰ Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Mozilla speeds Firefox release schedule to biweekly
articles.content: Firefox releases will soon get even closer together вҖ“ but not ESR ones, which remain annual, with the next one due out soon. The change was announced last week on the dev-platform@mozilla.org mailing list by MozillaвҖҷs director of engineering Sylvestre Ledru: вҖңWe are planning to move Firefox Desktop and Android from a 4-week release cadence to a 2-week release cadence starting in September 2026. вҖңThis will be an experimentвҖҰ This does not mean that all work needs to ship twice as fast. Work that is not ready should not be rushed, and features can still take the time they need to bake. вҖңThe current target is to release Firefox 155 on September 1, 2026, instead of September 15 вҖҰ вҖңWe will closely monitor how this change works in practice and adjust if needed.вҖқ The change is already visible in the upcoming Firefox Release Calendar. At the time of writing, it shows the current four-week cadence for Firefox 153 and 154, with Firefox 155 brought forward to September 1. From then on, it lists releases at roughly two-week intervals. This isnвҖҷt unprecedented. The last time Mozilla did it was over a decade ago. Google announced a very similar change for Chrome back in March, and The Register reported on it going to six-weekly releases in 2010 and then four-weekly ones in 2021. Brace for the impact of the next ESR In the meantime, Firefox 153 is nearly here: at the time of writing, itвҖҷs scheduled for Tuesday, July 21. Right now, the release notes are blank, but the beta release notes are more forthcoming. You can look forward to further improvements to PDF editing. Version 153 will be able to merge multiple PDF documents into one by a simple drag-and-drop in the PDF sidebar, and insert images into PDFs as new pages. It will also improve highlighting text in PDFs. The new version can generate QR codes for sharing web pages offline, such as in printed documents. It can verify and display the EUвҖҷs new Qualified Website Authentication Certificates, or QWACs for short. These are part of the new eIDAS legislation, which is a slightly convoluted acronym for вҖңelectronic IDentification, Authentication and trust ServicesвҖқ. Sounds cumbersome, but if it helps make the Wild West of the Web a bit safer, weвҖҷre all for it. Another security tweak is that extensions will lose local-file access by default. As we use one that relies on it - the excellent Multithreaded Download Manager - that's slightly concerning, although users will be able to grant the permission separately, so we'll see. If a tab is using your location, Firefox 153 will highlight this with a map-pin icon in the address bar. The last release could recognize a few keywords in the address bar to mute all sound: this function will get extended, with typed commands for picking colors from pages, and accessing experimental вҖңLabsвҖқ features. Come back, Ubuntu Unity and your HUD, all is forgiven. ThereвҖҷs also experimental JPEG XL support. Pop-up video player controls will work better, and if you use Windows and have a compatible GPU and drivers, 153 will play HDR video. On macOS, Firefox supports the Fn+F keystroke for switching to full-screen mode вҖ“ which means KeyboardControls.com has to update its description of the function. Firefox 153 will also be a significant release, because it will be the next ESR version: it will get security updates for at least 15 months, meaning until late 2027. (And maybe longer: Firefox 115 is still getting updates, and now will until March 2027.) In turn, that means that Firefox 153 is also the basis of the latest beta of the AI-free Waterfox fork, which recently integrated built-in ad blocking. В®

Щ…ЩҲШ°ЫҢЩ„Ш§ ШЁШ§ ШҜЩӮШӘ Ш§Ш«ШұШ§ШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ ШұШ§ ШІЫҢШұ ЩҶШёШұ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ ШҜШұ ЩҲЫҢШұШ§ЫҢШҙ PDF ШҜШұ ЩҶШіШ®ЩҮ ЫұЫөЫі ЩҒШ§ЫҢШұЩҒШ§Ъ©Ші Ш§ЩҒШІЩҲШҜЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҙШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩ„Ш§Шҙ Щ…ЩҲШ°ЫҢЩ„Ш§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұЫҢ ЩҒШ§ЫҢШұЩҒШ§Ъ©Ші ШҜШұ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ШіШ§ЫҢШұ Щ…ШұЩҲШұЪҜШұЩҮШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШұ ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұЫҢ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Щ…Ш«ШЁШӘ ШЁЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШҜ. ШЁШ§ Ш§ЩҒШІЩҲШҜЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШіШұЫҢШ№вҖҢШӘШұШҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШІШ§ЫҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮШұЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҶШҜ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: The Pentagon froze 155 wind projects in 24 states, claiming drones can hide in wind farms
articles.content: The Pentagon has frozen the permitting process for at least 155 new wind projects in 24 states for nearly a year, citing concerns that drones can hide among wind turbines and evade radar detection. The affected projects have a combined capacity of 44 gigawatts, four times the generation capacity of the offshore wind projects the […] This story continues at The Next Web

articles.summaryFa: ЩҫЩҶШӘШ§ЪҜЩҲЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶЪ©ЩҮ ЩҫЩҮЩҫШ§ШҜЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШҜШұ Щ…ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ШӘЩҲШұШЁЫҢЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШҜЫҢ ЩҫЩҶЩҮШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Ш§ШІ ШӘШҙШ®ЫҢШө ШұШ§ШҜШ§ШұЫҢ ЩҒШұШ§Шұ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜШҢ ШұЩҲЩҶШҜ ШөШҜЩҲШұ Щ…Ш¬ЩҲШІ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӯШҜШ§ЩӮЩ„ ЫұЫөЫө ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШЁШ§ШҜЫҢ ШҜШұ ЫІЫҙ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„ШӘ ШұШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘЩӮШұЫҢШЁШ§ЩӢ ЫҢЪ© ШіШ§Щ„ Щ…ШӘЩҲЩӮЩҒ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. ШёШұЩҒЫҢШӘ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЪ© Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЫҙЫҙ ЪҜЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҲШ§ШӘ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…Ш№Ш§ШҜЩ„ ЪҶЩҮШ§Шұ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ШёШұЩҒЫҢШӘ ШӘЩҲЩ„ЫҢШҜ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШҜЫҢ ЩҒШұШ§ШіШ§ШӯЩ„ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҙЩ…Ш§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢШҜ.

ШӯШҜШ§ЩӮЩ„ ЫұЫөЫө ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ ШҜШұ ЫІЫҙ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„ШӘ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШ§Ш«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
ШёШұЩҒЫҢШӘ Ъ©Щ„ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ШЁШ§ ЫҙЫҙ ЪҜЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҲШ§ШӘ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Meta In Talks To Lease Computing Power To Anthropic In Potential $10 Billion Deal
articles.content: Anthropic is reportedly in very early talks to lease computing power from Meta in a potential deal worth around $10 billion. The discussions follow Anthropic's recent compute deal with SpaceX and come as Meta explores selling excess AI capacity as part of a broader push to turn its massive infrastructure spending into a cloud business. CNBC reports: Access to enough AI chips remains a challenge for firms like Anthropic, which places usage limits on its most advanced models like Fable. [...] Meta could spend as much as $145 billion on capital expenditures, including for AI infrastructure, in 2026. Last October, Zuckerberg said that companies are regularly "asking if we have compute that they could buy from us at some premium to what we've bought it at." Read more of this story at Slashdot.
articles.author: BeauHD
articles.fingerprint: anthropic billion computing deal lease meta potential power talks

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЪҜЩҒШӘЪҜЩҲЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ ЩҫЫҢ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҒЩӮ Ш§Ш®ЫҢШұ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіЩҫЫҢШівҖҢШ§ЫҢЪ©Ші Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ШӘШ§ ШҜШұ ШӘЩ„Ш§Шҙ Ш§ШіШӘ ШӘШ§ ШёШұЩҒЫҢШӘ Ш§Ш¶Ш§ЩҒЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШұЩҲШҙ ШЁШұШіШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘШ§ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш№ШёЫҢЩ… Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЫҢЪ© Ъ©ШіШЁвҖҢЩҲЪ©Ш§Шұ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ШӘШЁШҜЫҢЩ„ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Anthropic is reportedly in early talks to lease computing power from Meta in a potential $10 billion deal. These discussions follow Anthropic's recent compute contract with SpaceX, as Meta aims to monetize its excess AI capacity and turn its infrastructure investments into a cloud business.
insightsFa: ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШұЩҮ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁШ§ШӘЫҢ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШӘШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ШұШІШҙ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҒЩӮ Ш§ШӯШӘЩ…Ш§Щ„ЫҢ ЫұЫ° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШӘШ®Щ…ЫҢЩҶ ШІШҜЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Щ…ШӘШ§ ЩӮШөШҜ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ШёШұЩҒЫҢШӘ Ш§Ш¶Ш§ЩҒЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШұЩҲШҙ ШЁШұШіШ§ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҒЩӮ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіЩҫЫҢШівҖҢШ§ЫҢЪ©Ші ШөЩҲШұШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұШҜ.
ШёШұЩҒЫҢШӘ ЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҙЫҢ Щ…ШӯШҜЩҲШҜ ЫҢЪ©ЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЪҶШ§Щ„ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШөЩ„ЫҢ ШўЩҶШӘШұЩҲЩҫЫҢЪ© Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Icertis CEO is departing; contract management company names CFO and board member interim leaders
articles.content: Anand Subbaraman. (LinkedIn Photo) Anand Subbaraman is departing as CEO of Icertis, the Bellevue, Wash.-based contract management software company said Friday. Chief Financial Officer Rajat Bahri and longtime executive and Icertis board member Jim Moffatt will serve as interim co-CEOs as Icertis searches for its next CEO. Subbaraman took the helm in August 2025 when Icertis co-founder Samir Bodas stepped down . Bodas had been the company’s only CEO since launching the business in 2009. Bodas shared at the time that he was resigning due to a health concern, and passed away in January after a battle with cancer. Subbaraman, who joined Icertis in 2024 as chief operating officer, will serve as an advisor during the leadership transition. No reason was provided for his exit. We’ve asked the company for further details. “We are grateful for Anand’s service and his work to expand the company’s AI capabilities and scale operations,” Moffatt said in a statement. “As our Board conducts its search for the next CEO, Rajat and I will ensure we do not miss a beat during this important time for our company.вҖқ Icertis Chief Financial Officer Rajat Bahri, left, and board member Jim Moffatt will serve as interim co-CEOs as the company searches for a new chief executive. (Icertis Photos) Founded in 2009, Icertis has raised more than $500 million and was valued at $5 billion four years ago. Its investors include SoftBank’s Vision Fund, SAP and PSP Partners, the firm chaired by lead independent director Penny Pritzker. Bloomberg reported in February that Icertis was working with Goldman Sachs to explore a potential sale that could value the company at as much as $5 billion, citing people familiar with the matter. Buyout firms had shown preliminary interest, and no final decision had been reached, according to the report. The company said Friday that Bahri will hold dual roles as interim co-CEO and CFO. He joined Icertis in 2022 and previously served as CFO at several companies, including ID.me; Wish, where he helped lead the company’s IPO; and Jasper Technologies. Moffatt has served on the Icertis board since 2022, after previously serving on its advisory board, and is a member of the board’s audit and compensation committees. He has also been appointed chair of the board, in addition to his interim leadership role. Moffatt spent more than 35 years at Deloitte, leaving the company as vice chairman and global CEO of Deloitte Consulting. He is now president of JSM Advisors.
articles.author: Lisa Stiffler

articles.title: Following the questions where they lead
articles.content: Ever since she was a child playing on her familyвҖҷs farmland in Wisconsin, Bailey Flanigan was guided by her own selective, yet wide-ranging, curiosity. Describing her young self as spirited and a bit unruly, she directed her energies to everything from building booby traps to doing experimental construction projects to exploring an intense interest in medicine to writing fiction and music to planning nonprofit organizations to help lessen social inequality. By high school, Flanigan was intensely drawn to particular subjects. вҖңI found myself unmotivated to take all the AP [advanced placement] classes for the sake of it. My interest was captured by classes where I could be creative вҖ” where I could use math to solve real-world problems, creatively write, make music, connect distant ideas, or deeply explore the humanities вҖ” and I worked on such classes obsessively, as an opportunity to explore my intuitions and interests,вҖқ she says. вҖңInstead of joining clubs, I ended up spending a lot of time thinking and creating on my own, and trying to understand what I enjoyed.вҖқ Today Flanigan is a shared faculty member between the MIT Schwarzman College of Computing and the MIT departments of Political Science and Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS), and a principal investigator in the MIT Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems. She has been involved in research at the University of Wisconsin, the National Institutes of Health, Google, and Carnegie Mellon, Drexel, Harvard, Princeton, and Stanford universities. Her current work focuses on using computational and mathematical tools to create new avenues for meaningful democratic participation. Perhaps not surprisingly, her path has crossed huge expanses of subject matter and specialties вҖ” from medicine and bioengineering to public health, and from economics to her joint appointment at MIT in computer science and political science, which began in fall 2025. вҖңMy trajectory across disciplines was just a result of me chasing down the problems I felt were most pressing or inspiring at the time. Along the way, I wound up in a lot of situations where I was less well-trained or qualified in the standard ways. While this was sometimes precarious, it was also incredibly fun, and it cultivated my ability to learn the languages of new disciplines more easily вҖ” a skill pretty much essential to my current research and job.вҖқ In college at the University of Wisconsin at Madison, Flanigan worked in a wet lab on therapeutic targets in cancer and computationally on tumor genetics. She says she found the research intellectually interesting, but eventually began to wonder about whether it would have the kind of impact she wanted. вҖңAt the time, I started to worry that the science I was developing might only, in the best case, be used by a small, relatively wealthy fraction of the world, when there were people suffering from much more-preventable diseases in much larger numbers,вҖқ she says. So Flanigan moved toward public health, where she researched microfluidic devices for HIV detection that could be used in low-resource settings. Still bothered by the circumstances driving these settingsвҖҷ limited resources to begin with, she then started to dabble in economics. Around the same time, FlaniganвҖҷs academic advisors were chipping away at preconceptions she held about her own abilities. Steven Wright, a professor of law and creative writing at UW-Madison, served as FlaniganвҖҷs informal mentor throughout college, and they worked together on a case at the Wisconsin Innocence Project. вҖңHe guided me through my evolving interests in science, social inequality, and economics,вҖқ she says. вҖңHe was one of the people most responsible for convincing me that I could aim higher in my career, and that I could actually go to places like MIT or Harvard.вҖқ Also while she was in college, the two heads of the UW-Madison scholarship office, Debbie Berger and Julie Stubbs, sent Flanigan repeated emails, encouraging herвҖҰ
articles.author: Michaela Jarvis | MIT Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems
