Ш§ЩҲ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШӯШ§Ш¶Шұ ШӘЩ…ШұЪ©ШІ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁШ§ШӘЫҢ ЩҲ ШұЫҢШ§Ш¶ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Щ…ШіЫҢШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШҜШұ Щ…ШҙШ§ШұЪ©ШӘ ШҜЩ…ЩҲЪ©ШұШ§ШӘЫҢЪ© Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ШҜШ§Шұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: PalantirвҖҷs CEO predicts AI will make him 20x richer while middle-class workers get modest raises
articles.content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp estimated that AI could make him вҖң20x wealthier,вҖң implying a fortune approaching $300 billion, up from roughly $15 billion today. Middle-class workers, he said, might simply see their salaries double over the next decade. Karp called the disparity вҖңa complete decoupling of unimaginable wealth and normal wealthвҖқ and said it is […] This story continues at The Next Web
articles.author: Alina Maria Stan
articles.fingerprint: 20x ceo class get him make middle modest palantir predicts raises richer while workers
articles.titleFa: Щ…ШҜЫҢШұШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ ЩҫШ§Щ„Ш§ЩҶШӘЫҢШұ ЩҫЫҢШҙвҖҢШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ЩҲ ШұШ§ ЫІЫ° ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘЩ…ЩҶШҜШӘШұ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Ш§Щ„Щ… Ш§Щ„Ъ©ШӘШ§ШЁ ЩҫШ§Щ„Ш§ЩҶШӘЫҢШұШҢ Ш§Щ„Ъ©Ші Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҫШҢ ШӘШ®Щ…ЫҢЩҶ ШІШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш§ЩҲ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ Ш·ШұШІ ЪҶШҙЩ…ЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘЩ…ЩҶШҜШӘШұ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ШіШұЩ…Ш§ЫҢЩҮ Ш§ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ ШӯШҜЩҲШҜ ЫіЫ°Ы° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШЁШұШіШҜ. ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ…ЩҶШҜШ§ЩҶ Ш·ШЁЩӮЩҮ Щ…ШӘЩҲШіШ· Ш§ШӯШӘЩ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢ ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШҜЩҲ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯЩӮЩҲЩӮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮЩҶШҜ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘШҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҫ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҶШ§ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұЫҢ ШұШ§ В«ЩҒШөЩ„ Ъ©Ш§Щ…Щ„Ш§ЩӢ Ш¬ШҜШ§ЪҜШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘ ШәЫҢШұЩ…Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ„ ЩҲ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘ Ш№Ш§ШҜЫҢВ» ШӘЩҲШөЫҢЩҒ Ъ©ШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Palantir CEO Alex Karp estimated that AI could make him significantly wealthier, potentially amassing a fortune of around $300 billion. In contrast, he noted that middle-class workers might only see their salaries double, describing this inequality as a "complete decoupling of unimaginable wealth and normal wealth."
insightsFa: Ш§Щ„Ъ©Ші Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҫ ЩҫЫҢШҙвҖҢШЁЫҢЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘ Ш§ЩҲ ШЁЩҮ ЫіЫ°Ы° Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢШ§ШұШҜ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ШЁШұШіШҜ.
Ъ©Ш§ШұЩ…ЩҶШҜШ§ЩҶ Ш·ШЁЩӮЩҮ Щ…ШӘЩҲШіШ· Ш§ШӯШӘЩ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢ ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ ЫІ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ ШҙШҜЩҶ ШӯЩӮЩҲЩӮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮЩҶШҜ Ъ©ШұШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҶШ§ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘ ШЁЩҮ В«ЩҒШөЩ„ Ъ©Ш§Щ…Щ„Ш§ЩӢ Ш¬ШҜШ§ЪҜШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢВ» Ш§ШҙШ§ШұЩҮ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
ЩҫЫҢШҙШұЩҒШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұ ШӘЩҲШІЫҢШ№ Ш«ШұЩҲШӘ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘ.
ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Ъ©Ш§ШұЩҫ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ШЁЩҶШ§ЫҢ ШЁШӯШ«вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ ШҜШұ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ Ш№ШҜШ§Щ„ШӘ Ш§ЩӮШӘШөШ§ШҜЫҢ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.

articles.title: Apple in talks to settle DOJ antitrust lawsuit, per report
articles.content: Back in 2024, the US Department of Justice filed a lawsuit against Apple, accusing the company of a вҖңsmartphone monopoly.вҖқ Apple has voiced its opposition to the case many times over the last two years . Now, a Bloomberg report says the DOJ and Apple are in talks to settle the lawsuit.
articles.author: Chance Miller
articles.fingerprint: antitrust apple doj lawsuit per report settle talks
articles.titleFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӯЩ„ ЩҲ ЩҒШөЩ„ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ Ш¶ШҜШӘШұШ§ШіШӘ ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ ШўЩ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ШіШӘ
articles.summaryFa: ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮ ШҜШұ ШіШ§Щ„ ЫІЫ°ЫІЫҙ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш№Щ„ЫҢЩҮ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ "Щ…ЩҲЩҶЩҲЩҫЩҲЩ„ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЪҜЩҲШҙЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙЩ…ЩҶШҜ" ШӘЩҶШёЫҢЩ… Ъ©ШұШҜ. Ш§Ъ©ЩҶЩҲЩҶ ШЁЩҮ ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙ ШЁЩ„ЩҲЩ…ШЁШұЪҜШҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ЩҲ ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӯЩ„ ЩҲ ЩҒШөЩ„ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: In 2024, the US Department of Justice filed a lawsuit against Apple for a "smartphone monopoly." Now, a Bloomberg report indicates that Apple and the DOJ are in talks to settle the lawsuit.
insightsFa: ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ ЩҲШІШ§ШұШӘ ШҜШ§ШҜЪҜШіШӘШұЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҲЩҶЩҲЩҫЩҲЩ„ЫҢ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЪҜЩҲШҙЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙЩ…ЩҶШҜ Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШЁЩҮ Ш·ЩҲШұ Щ…ШҜШ§ЩҲЩ… ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙЪ©Ш§ЫҢШӘ Ш§Ш№ШӘШұШ§Ш¶ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш°Ш§Ъ©ШұШ§ШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШіШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЩҶШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШ§ЩҶЩҲЩҶЫҢ ЩҲ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ ШҜШұ ШұШ§ЩҮШЁШұШҜ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: Windows 11 24H2 Support Ends Soon: What Users Need to Do Before October
articles.content: Windows 11 24H2 Home and Pro support ends Oct. 13, 2026. See who is affected, available upgrade paths, and what IT teams should do now. The post Windows 11 24H2 Support Ends Soon: What Users Need to Do Before October appeared first on TechRepublic .
articles.author: Aminu Abdullahi
articles.fingerprint: 24h2 before ends need october soon support users windows
articles.titleFa: ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЩҲЫҢЩҶШҜЩҲШІ ЫұЫұ ЩҶШіШ®ЩҮ ЫІЫҙHЫІ ШЁЩҮ ШІЩҲШҜЫҢ ЩҫШ§ЫҢШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұШіШҜ: Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ЪҶЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ЫҢШҜ ЩҫЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ Ш§Ъ©ШӘШЁШұ Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… ШҜЩҮЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШІ ЩҶШіШ®ЩҮ ЫІЫҙHЫІ ЩҲЫҢЩҶШҜЩҲШІ ЫұЫұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Ш®Ш§ЩҶЪҜЫҢ ЩҲ ШӯШұЩҒЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ§ ШӘШ§ШұЫҢШ® ЫұЫі Ш§Ъ©ШӘШЁШұ ЫІЫ°ЫІЫ¶ Ш§ШҜШ§Щ…ЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘ. ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮШҢ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ Ъ©ШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘШӯШӘ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЩҶШҜШҢ Щ…ШіЫҢШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШұШӘЩӮШ§ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ЩҲ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ…Ш§ШӘЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘЫҢЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШЁШ§ЫҢШҜ ШӘШ§ Ъ©ЩҶЩҲЩҶ Ш§ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ… ШҜЩҮЩҶШҜШҢ ЩҫШұШҜШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

ШӘЫҢЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШЁШ§ЫҢШҜ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢШұЫҢШІЫҢ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЪҜШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШұШӘЩӮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: Introducing Mobile Layout for Amazon Quick dashboards
articles.content: Teams that rely on dashboards for daily decisions often must pinch and zoom to interact with controls originally designed for larger displays. Checking revenue during a morning standup, reviewing pipeline metrics between meetings, or monitoring operations while traveling all require extra effort when the dashboard was built for a desktop screen. Mobile Layout for Amazon Quick Free Form dashboards solves this by automatically rendering dashboards as a single-column, touch-optimized view that fills the device screen. Readers get immediate access to their data without resizing or scrolling horizontally. Mobile Layout for Free Form dashboards is now available in each supported AWS Region. Mobile Layout transforms the desktop canvas into a single-column, continuous scroll experience sized for the device. Visuals fill the screen width, maintain their aspect ratio, and remain interactive. Authors do not need to make changes for this to take effect. Dashboard readers get immediate, frictionless access to their data on any phone or tablet. In this post, we show you how Mobile Layout works and what you can do to optimize your dashboards. The following animation shows a Free Form dashboard opening on a phone in Mobile Layout, with the reader scrolling through key performance indicators (KPIs) and charts in a continuous vertical feed. Mobile layout explained Mobile Layout is a rendering mode for Free Form dashboards. When a reader opens a Free Form dashboard on a device with a small viewport, Amazon Quick detects the screen size and renders the mobile view. The reader gets a single-column, vertically scrollable feed of visuals, each sized to fill the screen width. Detection is based on the device viewport. When the viewport falls within phone or tablet dimensions, Mobile Layout activates. On larger screens, the standard desktop rendering is used. Capabilities Mobile Layout includes the following capabilities for Free Form dashboards. Continuous scroll. Visuals stack in a single column. Each visual fills the full viewport width. Aspect ratios are preserved: portrait-shaped visuals stay tall, landscape-shaped visuals stay wide. Group-aware rendering. Free Form dashboards often use overlapping visuals (for example, KPI values layered on a colored background). Mobile Layout respects group boundaries. Grouped visuals render together as a single unit, preserving the layered design. Reader controls. In the Quick mobile app, readers can use a built-in view switcher to toggle between Mobile view (continuous scroll) and Desktop view (the original rendering). On web (mobile browser), portrait orientation renders the mobile experience and landscape orientation renders the desktop experience. Performance. Mobile Layout uses an optimized rendering path that reduces resource consumption on mobile devices. Dashboards that previously had difficulty rendering on phones now load and scroll smoothly. The following image shows the same dashboard in Desktop view (left) and Mobile continuous scroll view (right). Required actions For dashboard readers: Nothing. Mobile Layout works for existing Free Form dashboards. There is no setting to turn on, no migration to perform, and nothing to update. Open a dashboard on a phone or tablet and access the mobile view immediately. For authors and administrators: Nothing is required. Authors of Free Form dashboards benefit automatically because their published dashboards already render in Mobile Layout on smaller screens. Authors who use Tiled or Classic layouts are not affected. Those layout types retain their existing responsive behavior. Administrators do not need to configure anything. Mobile Layout is active by default for Free Form dashboards across the account. Getting the best mobile experience with groups Authors who use overlapping visuals can take one optional step to verify that their design translates well to mobile: organize those visuals into Groups. In Free Form layout, authors commonly layer visuals on topвҖҰ

articles.titleFa: Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШЁЩҲШұШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Amazon Quick
articles.summaryFa: ШҜШ§ШҙШЁЩҲШұШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Amazon Quick ШЁШ§ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ШӘШ§ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШІЩҲЩ… Ъ©ШұШҜЩҶ ЫҢШ§ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Ш§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІЩҮШҢ ШЁЩҮ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұЩҲЫҢ ШӘЩ„ЩҒЩҶ ЫҢШ§ ШӘШЁЩ„ШӘ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШөЩҲШұШӘ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§Шұ ШҜШ§ШҙШЁЩҲШұШҜЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЫҢЪ© ЩҶЩ…Ш§ЫҢ Щ„Щ…ШіЫҢ ЩҲ ЫҢЪ© ШіШӘЩҲЩҶЫҢ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ Ш§ЩҶЫҢЩ…ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫЫҢЩҲШіШӘЩҮ ЩҲ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Amazon Quick dashboards now feature a mobile layout that allows users to access critical information effortlessly on their phones or tablets without the need for pinching or zooming. This automatic design transforms dashboards into a single-column, touch-optimized view that enhances interactivity and accessibility.
insightsFa: ШҜШ§ШҙШЁЩҲШұШҜЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШөЩҲШұШӘ Ш§ШӘЩҲЩ…Ш§ШӘЫҢЪ© ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҶЩ…Ш§ЫҢШҙ ШҜШұ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ ШЁЩҮЫҢЩҶЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.
Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ ЩҶЩ…Ш§ЫҢ Щ„Щ…ШіЫҢ ЩҲ ЫҢЪ© ШіШӘЩҲЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ ШұШ§ ШўШіШ§ЩҶвҖҢШӘШұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.
Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩҶШёЫҢЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§Ш¶Ш§ЩҒЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ЩҫЫҢШҜШ§ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘ Ш§ШіЪ©ШұЩҲЩ„ ЩҫЫҢЩҲШіШӘЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұЫҢ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ъ©ШұШҜ Ъ©ШіШЁвҖҢЩҲЪ©Ш§ШұЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШҜЩҮШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ШЁЩҮЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢШіШ§ШІЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШЁЩҲШұШҜЩҮШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҜШіШӘЪҜШ§ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШЁШ§ЫҢЩ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШҜШіШӘШұШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш§Ш·Щ„Ш§Ш№Ш§ШӘ Ъ©Щ„ЫҢШҜЫҢ ШұШ§ ШіШұЫҢШ№вҖҢШӘШұ ЩҲ ШұШ§ШӯШӘвҖҢШӘШұ Ъ©ЩҶШҜШҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ШЁ ШӘШіЩҮЫҢЩ„ ШӘШөЩ…ЫҢЩ…вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӯШіШ§Ші Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
articles.content: A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys. A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with ComfyUI, Ollama, n8n, Open WebUI, Langflow, and Gradio: the image generators, local model runners, and workflow builders that teams stand up fast and firewall late. The intel feed behind that counter

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҶШӘ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ComfyUI ЩҲ OllamaШҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш¬ШіШӘШ¬ЩҲ ШҜШұ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ЩҲ Щ…ШӯЩ„ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҫШұШҜШ§ШІШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШіШұШ№ШӘ ШұШ§ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІЫҢ ШҙШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Щ…Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ЩҫШі Ш§ШІ ШўЩҶ Ш§ШІ ЩҒШ§ЫҢШұЩҲШ§Щ„ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: A Go-based botnet named NadMesh emerged in early July, targeting exposed AI services and claiming to have discovered 3,811 unique AWS keys. The botnet utilizes tools like ComfyUI and Ollama to scan for cloud and local services that are quickly deployed and often secured with firewalls afterward.
insightsFa: NadMesh ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҶШӘЫҢ Щ…ШЁШӘЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұ Go Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұШЁШ§ШӘвҖҢЩҶШӘ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШўШіЫҢШЁвҖҢЩҫШ°ЫҢШұ ШӯЩ…Щ„ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ШҜШ№Ш§ЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Ыі,ЫёЫұЫұ Ъ©Щ„ЫҢШҜ AWS Щ…ЩҶШӯШөШұ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШұШҜ ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ComfyUI ЩҲ Ollama ШҜШұ Ш§ШіЪ©ЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҶЩҲШ№ ШӯЩ…Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШӘЩҮШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ Ш§ШЁШұЫҢ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩҲЫҢЪҳЩҮ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ ШІЫҢШұШ§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШӯЩ…Щ„Ш§ШӘ ШіШ§ЫҢШЁШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЩҲ Ш§ЩҶЩҒШ¬Ш§Шұ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШҜЩ…Ш§ШӘ ШҜШұ ШЁЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ. ШЁШ§ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШҙЫҢЩҲШ№ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢШҢ ШӯЩҒШё Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШіШЈЩ„ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШӯШ§ШҰШІ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШӘШЁШҜЫҢЩ„ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.content: For all the hysteria over вҖңwokeвҖқ casting and ahistorical choices, Christopher NolanвҖҷs epic is on track to make $200 million globally during its opening weekend.

ШЁШ§ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӯШ§Щ„ШҢ ЩҒЫҢЩ„Щ… ШҜШұ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ ЩҒШұЩҲШҙ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҲЩҒЩӮЫҢШӘ ЪҶШҙЩ…ЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШҜШіШӘ ЩҫЫҢШҜШ§ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Troubling new details emerge on diabetes ouster controversy
articles.content: Last month, we reported on a troubling incident at the annual meeting of the American Diabetes Association (ADA) in New Orleans. On June 5, five leading scientists were ousted for handing out copies of an editorial, published in the journal Diabetes Care (an ADA journal) in April, sharply criticizing the Trump administrationвҖҷs ongoing attacks on scientific research. There was a public outcry and (eventually) a personal apology from the ADA's CEO for the heavy-handed response, but it seems the organization has not yet learned its lesson. The deputy editors of Diabetes Care have posted an editorial and seven accompanying opinion articles to a preprint serverвҖ”handily contained in a single PDF fileвҖ”that they say the ADA has refused to publish. Several troubling new details are included in the articles, including an accusation that ADA leadership knew in advance that members would be handing out copies of the editorial and deliberately set up an ambush by venue security and local police. That decision, in turn, might be due to a simmering tensions connected to a session organized the year before. ADA leadership was provided with the articles in advance of publication with an invitation to simultaneously publish their response. Read full article Comments
articles.author: Jennifer Ouellette
articles.fingerprint: controversy details diabetes emerge ouster troubling

ШҜЩҲЫҢШұ ЩҲЫҢШұШ§ЫҢШҙЪҜШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҶШҙШұЫҢЩҮ Diabetes Care Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЫҢЪ© ШіШұЩҲШұ ЩҫЫҢШҙвҖҢЩҶШҙШұ Ш§ШұШіШ§Щ„ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШӘЩҲШіШ· ADA ЩҶШ§ШҜЫҢШҜЩҮ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ ЩҲ ШӯШ§ЩҲЫҢ Ш¬ШІШҰЫҢШ§ШӘ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШұЩҲЫҢШҜШ§ШҜ Ш¬ЩҶШ¬Ш§Щ„ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҙШ§Щ…Щ„ Ш§ШҜШ№Ш§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…ШЁЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұ Ш§ЫҢЩҶЪ©ЩҮ ШұЩҮШЁШұЫҢ ADA Ш§ШІ ЩӮШЁЩ„ Ш§ШІ ШӘЩҲШІЫҢШ№ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮ ШўЪҜШ§ЩҮЫҢ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ.

Щ…ЩӮШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ ШЁЩҮ ADA ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЩҶШӘШҙШ§Шұ ЩҫШ§ШіШ® Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ ШҙШҜ.
ЩҲШ§Ъ©ЩҶШҙ ШӘЩҶШҜ ADA ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҶШӘЩӮШ§ШҜЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ Ш§ЩҶШӘЩӮШ§ШҜ Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ…ЫҢ ЩӮШұШ§Шұ ЪҜШұЩҒШӘ.

articles.title: Google is making no progress on switching iPhone users to Android
articles.content: Existing iPhone owners continue to account for nearly nine in 10 surveyed U.S. purchases, showing how Apple's enormous customer base isn't dwindling under pressure from Google and other Android smartphone manufacturers. iPhone 17 Consumer Intelligence Research Partners estimates that 87% of U.S. iPhone buyers in the March 2026 quarter came from another iPhone. The share reached its highest level in the three years covered by the report, up from 84% in 2025 and 85% in 2024. Another 12% switched from an Android smartphone, down from 14% in 2025 and slightly below the 13% measured in 2024. The remaining 1% came from a basic phone, bought a first smartphone, or fell into another category. The numbers point to a smartphone market in which most buyers settled on a platform years ago. Apple can still attract Android owners, but the larger opportunity comes from convincing hundreds of millions of existing customers to replace an older iPhone with a newer model. Continue Reading on AppleInsider | Discuss on our Forums
articles.author: Andrew Orr <andreworr@appleinsider.com>
articles.fingerprint: android google iphone making progress switching users

articles.summaryFa: ШЁШұШ§ШіШ§Ші ЪҜШІШ§ШұШҙЫҢШҢ ШӘЩӮШұЫҢШЁШ§ЩӢ Ы№Ы° ШҜШұШөШҜ Ш§ШІ Ш®ШұЫҢШҜШ§ШұШ§ЩҶ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ iPhone ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЫҢ iPhone ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ ЫұЫІ ШҜШұШөШҜ Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Android ШЁЩҮ iPhone Щ…ЩҶШӘЩӮЩ„ ШҙШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҫШ§ЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҮ ШЁШІШұЪҜ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ Apple ШЁШ§ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҙШҜЫҢШҜ Ш§ШІ ШіЩҲЫҢ Google ЩҲ ШіШ§ЫҢШұ ШӘЩҲЩ„ЫҢШҜЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ Ш§ШіЩ…Ш§ШұШӘвҖҢЩҒЩҲЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Android ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШұШҙШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: According to a report, nearly 90% of new iPhone buyers in the U.S. come from existing iPhone users, with only 12% switching from Android. This indicates that Apple's large customer base continues to thrive despite competition from Google and other Android smartphone manufacturers.
insightsFa: Ы№Ы° ШҜШұШөШҜ Ш®ШұЫҢШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ iPhone Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЫҢ iPhone Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӘЩҶЩҮШ§ ЫұЫІ ШҜШұШөШҜ Ш§ШІ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Android ШЁЩҮ iPhone Щ…ЩҶШӘЩӮЩ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.
ШіЩҮЩ… Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ Android ШҜШұ Ш®ШұЫҢШҜ iPhone Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШЁШІШұЪҜвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ЩҒШұШөШӘ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Apple ШҜШұ ЩҒШұЩҲШҙ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШҜЫҢЩ…ЫҢ iPhone Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩҫШ§ЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ Apple ШЁЩҮвҖҢШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ЩҲЩҒШ§ШҜШ§ШұЫҢ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶШ§ЩҶ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЫҢШ§ШЁШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ Apple ШЁШ§ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙЩ…Ш§ШұШҢ ЩҮЩ…ЪҶЩҶШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШҜШұ ШЁЩҮ ШӯЩҒШё ЩҲ Ш¬Ш°ШЁ Щ…ШҙШӘШұЫҢШ§ЩҶ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш§ШіЩ…Ш§ШұШӘвҖҢЩҒЩҲЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§Щ…Шұ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘЩ…ШұШ§Шұ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ЩҲ ЩҶЩҒЩҲШ° ШЁШұЩҶШҜ Apple ШҜШұ ШҜЩҶЫҢШ§ЫҢ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ШҙШ§ШұЩҮ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.

articles.title: HackerNoon Projects of the Week: Bayan Flow, GAG Token, Shugert
articles.content: Welcome back to Projects of the Week , our weekly look at some of the most interesting products coming out of the Proof of Usefulness Hackathon . The bar is pretty simple: build something people actually find useful. Every project featured here tackles a real problem, whether that's through better infrastructure, smarter automation, or a fresh take on an old workflow. This week, three more projects made the cut. From interactive data visualization and AI-powered consumer research to ecommerce optimization, each tackles a problem that people and businesses run into every dayвҖ”and offers a practical way to solve it. Want to see your own project spotlighted here? Join the Proof of Usefulness Hackathon to get on our radar. Meet the Projects of the Week Bayan Flow https://hackernoon.com/bayan-flow-earns-a-34-proof-of-usefulness-score-by-building-interactive-real-time-visualizations?embedable=true Dashboards are everywhere, but most still leave users squinting at charts and exporting CSVs. Bayan Flow takes a different approach, building interactive, real-time visualizations that invite users to explore their data instead of simply viewing it. The project caught our attention for treating visualization as a decision-making tool rather than just another reporting layer See Bayan FlowвҖҷs full Proof of Usefulness report . GAG Token https://hackernoon.com/gag-token-earns-a-335-proof-of-usefulness-score-by-building-an-ai-driven-market-research-and-consumer-insights-platform?embedable=true Market research is full of expensive panels, slow turnaround times, and survey responses that don't always reflect what people actually think. GAG Token is trying to change that with an AI-powered consumer insights platform that uses token incentives to encourage participation while helping businesses gather and analyze feedback at scale. It's an ambitious blend of AI, Web3, and market research, but what stood out most was its focus on making consumer intelligence faster, more transparent, and easier to act on. Read GAG TokenвҖҷs full Proof of Usefulness Report . Shugert https://hackernoon.com/shugert-earns-a-392-proof-of-usefulness-score-by-building-expert-e-commerce-solutions?embedable=true Building an online store has never been easier. Building one that actually converts is another story. Shugert helps ecommerce brands bridge that gap with Shopify development, custom themes, technical SEO, and conversion optimization backed by years of hands-on experience. Rather than promising overnight growth, the team focuses on fixing the technical bottlenecks that quietly cost retailers salesвҖ”an approach that's already earned the trust of hundreds of merchants and a standout Proof of Usefulness score. See ShugertвҖҷs full Proof of Usefulness report. This is Your Sign to Stop Building in the Dark The internet has no shortage of polished demos, inflated metrics, and products that sound impressive until someone tries to use them. Proof of Usefulness was created to reward something simpler and much harder to fake: products that genuinely work for real people. Why submit? Instant Validation: Get your Proof of Usefulness score (from -100 to +1000) the moment you submit. The Prize Pool: Compete for $20K in cash and $130K+ in software credits from Bright Data , Neo4j , Storyblok , Algolia , and HackerNoon . Built-in Distribution: Your submission becomes a HackerNoon story, putting your build in front of millions of monthly readers. Rewards for All: Every qualifying participant unlocks a suite of software credits just for entering. How to Enter the Proof of Usefulness Hackathon https://hackernoon.com/how-to-enter-the-proof-of-usefulness-pou-hackathon?embedable=true 1. Get Your Score: Head to www.proofofusefulness.com and submit your project details to generate your PoU Report Card. 2. Generate Your Draft: Click the button on your report page to convert your submission into a HackerNoon blog post draft. 3. Refine & Publish: Edit your draft to add your technical "secret sauce,"вҖҰ

Ш§ШІ ШӘШ¬ШІЫҢЩҮ ЩҲ ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ЫҢ ЩҲ ШӘШӯЩӮЫҢЩӮ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Щ…ШЁШӘЩҶЫҢ ШЁШұ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШӘШ§ ШЁЩҮЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢШіШ§ШІЫҢ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұШӘ Ш§Щ„Ъ©ШӘШұЩҲЩҶЫҢЪ©ШҢ ЩҮШұ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ ШұШ§ЩҮвҖҢШӯЩ„ЫҢ Ш№Щ…Щ„ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЪҶШ§Щ„ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұЩҲШІЩ…ШұЩҮ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ Ъ©ШіШЁвҖҢЩҲЪ©Ш§ШұЩҮШ§ Ш§ШұШ§ШҰЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ.

ШӘШ¬ШІЫҢЩҮ ЩҲ ШӘШӯЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШҜШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШөЩҲШұШӘ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ…Щ„ЫҢ ЩҲ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШ§ШіШ® ШіШұЫҢШ№вҖҢШӘШұ Ш§ШІ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШұШІ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ШіШӘ.

Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢШіШ§ШІЫҢ ШӘШӯЩӮЫҢЩӮ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ЩҲ ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұШӘ Ш§Щ„Ъ©ШӘШұЩҲЩҶЫҢЪ© Ш§ШІ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ ШЁШұШ¬ШіШӘЪҜЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: ValveвҖҷs 14-Year Journey to Make the Steam Machine
articles.content: The company first began laying the groundwork for this yearвҖҷs hardware way back in 2012
articles.author: Jason Schreier
articles.fingerprint: journey machine make steam valve year
articles.titleFa: ШіЩҒШұ ЫұЫҙ ШіШ§Щ„ЩҮ Valve ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШіШ§Ш®ШӘ Steam Machine
articles.summaryFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Valve Ш§ШІ ШіШ§Щ„ ЫІЫ°ЫұЫІ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ШіШ®ШӘвҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ Steam Machine ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӘЩ„Ш§ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш·ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩҶЫҢвҖҢЩ…ШҜШӘ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҲШұЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ШұШөЩҮ ШіШ®ШӘвҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ ЪҜЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩҶЪҜ ЩҲ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁШ§ Ъ©ЩҶШіЩҲЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Valve has been working on the development of Steam Machine hardware since 2012. This project reflects the company's long-term efforts to enter the gaming hardware arena and compete with consoles and other platforms.
insightsFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ Valve Ш§ШІ ЫІЫ°ЫұЫІ Ъ©Ш§Шұ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Steam Machine ШұШ§ ШўШәШ§ШІ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШіШ®ШӘвҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁШ§ Ъ©ЩҶШіЩҲЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ЪҶШ§Щ„ШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲШұЩҲШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШіШ®ШӘвҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ ЪҜЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩҶЪҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Microsoft cuts OneDrive support for older Windows 10 versions next month
articles.content: Microsoft will end OneDrive synchronization support next month for older versions of Windows 10, leaving users without client updates, fixes, or technical assistance. According to a post on the company's Message Center, OneDrive sync app updates will continue on Windows 10 22H2 until October 10, 2028, but will stop for earlier versions from August 15, 2026. No support means no more updates, fixes, or security patches, although the service itself won't suddenly stop working. However, users running into problems cannot expect Microsoft to fix them. The move should not come as a surprise. Support for Windows 10 21H2, the last official release before the final 22H2, ended on June 13, 2023. The message mentions Windows 10 22H1, although this was never officially released by Microsoft вҖ“ the company switched to an annual release cadence after 21H2. According to Microsoft, "this change aligns OneDrive support with the Windows lifecycle policy and helps Microsoft focus ongoing investments on supported operating systems." Microsoft 365 file synchronization services will also be affected, although, again, nothing will necessarily stop working immediately. Microsoft stated: "Existing installations may continue to function, but future functionality is not guaranteed." Users still clinging to Windows 10 versions before 22H2 will have to use the web interface to access OneDrive, which is a good deal less convenient than desktop synchronization. Alternatively, there's always the upgrade to Windows 11 that Microsoft would dearly like users to make, although OneDrive synchronization on Windows 10 22H2 should be fine until 2028. There are plenty of alternatives for cloud synchronization. For organizations looking for something a little more sovereign, there are always options like Nextcloud, which told The Register that its synchronization software would work on Windows 10 1809 or later. As with Microsoft, Windows 11 is the recommendation. However, unlike Microsoft, Linux is also an option. В®

articles.title: Thanks HN for 15 years of support and helping me find my life's work
articles.content: Tomorrow is the 15th anniversary of the first day of the Recurse Center ( https://www.recurse.com/ ) My cofounders and I did YC all the way back in the Summer of 2010, with the initial idea of building "OkCupid for jobs." That idea quickly fizzled, and we spent the better part of a year pivoting between other ideas that also failed. Finally, we made something that we wanted ourselves: a self-directed programming retreat, where people built fun projects, contributed to open source, and helped each other become better programmers. After running two small batches, we launched on HN[1] and got an incredible reception. That post on HN helped us reach beyond our personal networks and meet programmers from around the world, many of whom have since become friends. HN brought us the majority of people who came to our next few batches, and in the years since, HN has remained our #2 source of applicants (after word of mouth). Alas, pg's comment[2] on HN when we launched turned out to be prescient: Running free programming retreats isn't a billion-dollar business, but it's still a worthwhile thing to do, and has positively impacted over 3,000 people so far. And 15 years on I still wake up every day excited to keep working on it. So, thanks HN, for helping make the Recurse Center possible, and for helping me find my life's work. [1] https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=3435183 [2] "This sounds like a crazy plan for a startup, I realize, but this is the right sort of crazy. In fact, the way the Hackruiters think about Hacker School is a lot like the way we initially thought about YC: if it doesn't make money, it will at least have been a benevolent thing to do." articles.summaryFa: ЩҒШұШҜШ§ШҢ ЩҫШ§ЩҶШІШҜЩҮЩ…ЫҢЩҶ ШіШ§Щ„ЪҜШұШҜ ШӘШЈШіЫҢШі Щ…ШұЪ©ШІ Recurse Ш§ШіШӘ. ШЁЩҶЫҢШ§ЩҶвҖҢЪҜШ°Ш§ШұШ§ЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШұЪ©ШІ ШҜШұ ШўШәШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ЫҢЪ© ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ… ШҙШәЩ„ЫҢ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҶШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁШ§ ЩҶШ§Ъ©Ш§Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘШ№ШҜШҜЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ШҜШұ ЩҶЩҮШ§ЫҢШӘШҢ ШЁШ§ ШӘШ§ШіЫҢШі ЫҢЪ© ШҜЩҲШұЩҮ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢЩҶЩҲЫҢШіЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪҜШұШҜШ§ЩҶШҢ Щ…ЩҲЩҒЩӮ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұЪҜШ°Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁШұ ШІЩҶШҜЪҜЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ ЫіЫ°Ы°Ы° ЩҶЩҒШұ ШҙШҜЩҶШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Tomorrow marks the 15th anniversary of the Recurse Center's founding. The co-founders initially aimed to create a job platform but faced many failures before establishing a self-directed programming retreat, positively impacting over 3,000 people so far.
insightsFa: Щ…ШұЪ©ШІ Recurse ЫұЫө ШіШ§Щ„ ЩҫЫҢШҙ ШӘШЈШіЫҢШі ШҙШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢШҜЩҮ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ЩҫЩ„ШӘЩҒШұЩ…ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҙШәЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ ШҙЪ©ШіШӘ Щ…ЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮ ШҙШҜ.
ШӘШ¬ШұШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҲЩҒЩӮЫҢШӘ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШҜЩҲШұЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШұЩҶШ§Щ…ЩҮвҖҢЩҶЩҲЫҢШіЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪҜШұШҜШ§ЩҶ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШұЪ©ШІ ШӘШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШЁШұ ШІЩҶШҜЪҜЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ ЫіЫ°Ы°Ы° ЩҶЩҒШұ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұЪҜШ°Ш§Шұ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШӯЩ…Ш§ЫҢШӘ HN ШЁЩҮ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ШҙШЁЪ©ЩҮ Ш§ШұШӘШЁШ§Ш·ЫҢ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЪҜШ§ЩҶ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ШӯШ§Щ…ЫҢШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ Ш¬ЩҲШ§Щ…Ш№ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Щ…Ш«ШЁШӘ Ш§ШіШӘ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ШҙЩҲЩӮЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ ШӘШ§ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШҜЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ъ©Ш§Шұ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: Car Keys in Apple Wallet reportedly coming to major Chinese automaker
articles.content: As Apple continues expanding Car Keys support across the auto industry, the feature now appears to be headed to select vehicles from a major Chinese automaker. Here are the details.
articles.author: Marcus Mendes
articles.fingerprint: apple automaker car chinese coming keys major reportedly wallet
articles.titleFa: ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ъ©ШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲ ШҜШұ Ъ©ЫҢЩҒ ЩҫЩҲЩ„ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШЁЩҮ ЫҢЪ© Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲШіШ§ШІ ШЁШІШұЪҜ ЪҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұШіШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШЁЩҮ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ЩҫШҙШӘЫҢШЁШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ъ©ШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲ ШҜШұ ШөЩҶШ№ШӘ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲШіШ§ШІЫҢ Ш§ШҜШ§Щ…ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ ЩҲ ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩҶШёШұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұШіШҜ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШӘШ®ШЁ ЫҢЪ© Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲШіШ§ШІ ШЁШІШұЪҜ ЪҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ Ш§Ш¶Ш§ЩҒЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ШҙШҜ. Ш¬ШІШҰЫҢШ§ШӘ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШұ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… ШҜШұ ШҜШіШӘШұШі Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Apple is continuing to expand Car Keys support across the automotive industry, and this feature appears to be coming to select vehicles from a major Chinese automaker. More details are available on this development.
insightsFa: Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘ Ъ©ШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲ ШҜШұ Ъ©ЫҢЩҒ ЩҫЩҲЩ„ ШҜЫҢШ¬ЫҢШӘШ§Щ„ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЪҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ ЩҶЫҢШІ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұШіШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШўШіЫҢШ§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЩҶШӘШёШ§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұЩҲШҜ ШЁШ§ Ш§Ш¶Ш§ЩҒЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҶ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ЫҢШӘШҢ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҮЩ… ШҙЩҲШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ЪҜШіШӘШұШҙ ЩҶЩҒЩҲШ° Ш§ЩҫЩ„ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲШіШ§ШІЫҢ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ЩҲ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШҜЫҢШ¬ЫҢШӘШ§Щ„ ШҜШұ Ш®ЩҲШҜШұЩҲЩҮШ§ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Moonshot AI Launches WorldвҖҷs Largest Open-Source Model
articles.content: Moonshot AIвҖҷs 2.8-trillion-parameter Kimi K3 raises ChinaвҖҷs open-model ambitions while posing major infrastructure and cost questio The post Moonshot AI Launches WorldвҖҷs Largest Open-Source Model appeared first on TechRepublic .
articles.author: Kezia Jungco
articles.fingerprint: largest launches model moonshot open source world
articles.titleFa: Moonshot AI Щ…ШҜЩ„ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢШЁШ§ШІ ШЁШІШұЪҜвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶ ШұШ§ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜ
articles.summaryFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ Kimi K3 Ш§ШІ Moonshot AI ШЁШ§ ЫІ.Ыё ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ЩҫШ§ШұШ§Щ…ШӘШұШҢ ЩҮШҜЩҒ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШұШ§ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢШЁШ§ШІ Ш§ШұШӘЩӮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШіЩҲШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ ШЁШІШұЪҜЫҢ ШҜШұ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШўЩҲШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 model, boasting 2.8 trillion parameters, elevates China's ambitions for open models while raising significant questions about infrastructure and costs.
insightsFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ Kimi K3 ШЁШІШұЪҜвҖҢШӘШұ Ш§ШІ ЩҮШұ Щ…ШҜЩ„ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢШЁШ§ШІ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁШ§ ЫІ.Ыё ШӘШұЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ЩҫШ§ШұШ§Щ…ШӘШұШҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ Ш§ШұШӘЩӮШ§ШЎ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШіЩҲШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ ШІЫҢШ§ШҜЫҢ ШҜШұШЁШ§ШұЩҮ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ШІЫҢШұШіШ§Ш®ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШұШҜ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ Щ…Ш·ШұШӯ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Moonshot AI ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ЩҫЫҢШҙЫҢвҖҢЪҜШұЩҒШӘЩҶ Ш§ШІ ШұЩӮШЁШ§ ШҜШұ Ш№ШұШөЩҮ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢШЁШ§ШІ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ЩҶЩҲЫҢШҜШЁШ®Шҙ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Ш№Щ…ШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШҜШұ ШөЩҶШ№ШӘ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ ЫҢЪ© Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҒЩҲЩӮвҖҢШ§Щ„Ш№Ш§ШҜЩҮ ШЁШІШұЪҜ ЩҲ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұЪҜШ°Ш§Шұ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ ЩҲ ШЁШұ ШұЩҲЫҢ ШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҫЪҳЩҲЩҮШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШӘШЈШ«ЫҢШұ Ш®ЩҲШ§ЩҮШҜ ЪҜШ°Ш§ШҙШӘ.

articles.title: China's Moonshot AI claims Kimi K3 can rival OpenAI and Anthropic
articles.content: The company has unveiled a massive new artificial intelligence model it says can take on top American firms.
articles.fingerprint: anthropic can china claims kimi moonshot openai rival
articles.titleFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ЪҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ Kimi K3 Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ OpenAI ЩҲ Anthropic ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ
articles.summaryFa: ШҙШұЪ©ШӘ ЪҶЫҢЩҶЫҢ Moonshot AI Ш§ШІ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Kimi K3 ШұЩҲЩҶЩ…Ш§ЫҢЫҢ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШҜШ№Ш§ЫҢ ШўЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШЁШ§ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁШұШ¬ШіШӘЩҮ ШўЩ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ЫҢЫҢ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЩҫЫҢШҙШұЩҒШӘЩҮвҖҢШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШӘЩ„Ш§Шҙ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШӯЩҲШІЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: Chinese company Moonshot AI has unveiled a new artificial intelligence model, Kimi K3, which they claim can rival leading American firms. This model is touted as China's most advanced effort in the field of artificial intelligence.
insightsFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„ Kimi K3 ШЁЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШұЩӮЫҢШЁЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ OpenAI ЩҲ Anthropic Щ…Ш№ШұЩҒЫҢ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶвҖҢШҜЩҮЩҶШҜЩҮ ЩҫЫҢШҙШұЩҒШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§Ш®ЫҢШұ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Moonshot AI ЩӮШөШҜ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ Ш¬Ш§ЫҢЪҜШ§ЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШұШ§ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ШӯЪ©Щ… Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ШІЫҢШӘ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ Ш№ШұШөЩҮ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ.
ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘ ШЁЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЫҢШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘ Щ…ШӘШӯШҜЩҮ ЩҲ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҙШҜШӘ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Departing AWS exec Dave Brown is reportedly joining Meta, as Facebook parent mulls its own cloud
articles.content: Dave Brown, the departing AWS senior vice president, has been a member of its senior leadership team. (Amazon Photo) One of Amazon’s top cloud leaders will be joining Meta as the Facebook parent company considers turning its massive AI buildout into a cloud business of its own. That’s the report from the Wall Street Journal overnight, quoting anonymous sources saying that Dave Brown, the senior Amazon executive who led AWS compute and AI services, will join Meta in the coming weeks to work on its data center build-out. Meta hasn’t committed to becoming a cloud provider, but CEO Mark Zuckerberg has said the idea is on the table. He told shareholders in May that companies were regularly approaching Meta to pay for access to its AI models or spare computing capacity вҖ” a business that would put Meta in competition with cloud providers it now relies on, including AWS. At Meta, Brown will report to infrastructure chief Santosh Janardhan, according to the WSJ report. Janardhan co-leads Meta Compute, an initiative Zuckerberg launched in January to plan the company’s data center buildout. Meta has said it expects to spend $125 billion to $145 billion on capital expenditures this year, much of it tied to AI data centers. Amazon isn’t commenting on the report. We’ve contacted Meta for confirmation and details. Brown’s departure from AWS was announced on Wednesday , with a warmly worded message from AWS CEO Matt Garman giving no indication that Amazon would try to challenge or restrict his new role on competitive grounds. AWS has gone to court before to enforce noncompete agreements against departing executives, suing two AWS leaders who left for Google Cloud in 2019 and 2020, respectively. But such agreements have grown harder to enforce. California bars them almost entirely, and Washington вҖ” Amazon’s home state вҖ” enacted a near-total ban this year, though it doesn’t take effect until mid-2027. Garman’s message said Brown had decided to take “a new role outside of the company” but did not say where he was going. He’s remaining at AWS through the end of July to help with the transition. At AWS, Brown will be succeeded by Dave Treadwell, a longtime Amazon executive who has run the technology behind the company’s retail operations and spent 27 years at Microsoft before joining Amazon in 2016. He takes over AWS Compute and ML Services on Aug. 1.
articles.author: Todd Bishop
articles.fingerprint: aws brown cloud dave departing exec facebook joining meta mulls own parent reportedly

articles.title: AsusвҖҷ top-end 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor is $400 off
articles.content: The discounted Asus QD-OLED in all its glory. | Image: The Verge The Asus ROG Swift 32-inch 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor has almost everything I want in a high-end gaming monitor, most notably a QD-OLED panel for inky black levels and vivid colors that go beyond whatвҖҷs possible on an LCD screen. Amazon , Best Buy , and B&H Photo have this model on sale for $899.99 (usually $1,299.99). ItвҖҷs particularly well-suited for fast-paced games with its 240Hz refresh rate, and G-Sync variable refresh rate to prevent screen tearing. This monitor has been available for this price for most of July. WeвҖҷre not sure how long the deal will stick around for, but Asus is likely trying to move units at a discount so it can put more focus on its newer $1,299.99 model that has a Tandem QD-OLED display and slightly better specs. Asus ROG Swift OLED PG32UCDMR Where to Buy: $1299.99 $899.99 at Amazon $1299.99 $899.99 at Best Buy $1299.99 $899.99 at B&H Photo The port selection is good, comprised of a DisplayPort 2.1 input, a pair of HDMI 2.1 ports, and USB-C for video via DisplayPort alt mode (with 90W of charging power), plus a USB 3.2 Gen 1 hub with three Type-A ports. A unique feature is that this and several other Asus OLED monitors have a proximity sensor that let it turn off when you walk away to reduce the risk of burn-in. It has a three-year warranty that covers burn-in with normal use. Other deals to consider Newegg has Marathon for Steam on sale for $21.99 when you apply the coupon code EPF5365 at checkout. The notoriously difficult extraction shooter from Bungie is gorgeous and more inviting to new players in its second season thatвҖҷs currently available. This is the cheapest price weвҖҷve seen if youвҖҷre looking to start your career as a runner, and great timing ahead of the experimental PvE mode thatвҖҷs launching on July 21st. A perennial favorite in our deal coverage, the Anker Nano Smart Display Charger Block is on sale for $25.99 at Best Buy and Amazon . The compact 45W USB-C power adapter with foldable prongs comes in four fun colors, and has a display on the front with charging progress and speed and a cute, smiley face to accompany you as your device charges. The Sennheiser Accentum Open Wireless Earbuds are $59.99 (usually $129.99) at Best Buy . They donвҖҷt have the same truly open design as other clip-on style earbuds like the Asus ROG Cetra Open Wireless , and instead fit in your ear without tips, similar to the standard Apple AirPods . They have an IPX4 rating, multipoint pairing, and up to 28 hours of runtime on a single charge, while still allowing outside noise to pass through. TheyвҖҷre a good, budget-friendly option if youвҖҷre looking for something to stay aware while working out or doing yardwork.
articles.author: Brad Bourque

articles.summaryFa: Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШӘЩҲШұ ЪҜЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩҶЪҜ ЫіЫІ Ш§ЫҢЩҶЪҶЫҢ Asus ROG Swift QD-OLED ШЁШ§ ШӘШ®ЩҒЫҢЩҒ ЫҙЫ°Ы° ШҜЩ„Ш§ШұЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘ ЫёЫ№Ы№.Ы№Ы№ ШҜЩ„Ш§Шұ ЩҒШұЩҲШ®ШӘЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШӘЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ Ъ©ЫҢЩҒЫҢШӘ ШӘШөЩҲЫҢШұ ЩҒЩҲЩӮвҖҢШ§Щ„Ш№Ш§ШҜЩҮ ЩҲ ЩҶШұШ® ЩҶЩҲШіШ§ШІЫҢ ЫІЫҙЫ° ЩҮШұШӘШІШҢ ЪҜШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШіШЁ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШіШұЫҢШ№ ШЁЩҮ ШҙЩ…Ш§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШұЩҲШҜ. Ш§ЫҢШіЩҲШі ШЁЩҮвҖҢЩ…ЩҶШёЩҲШұ ШӘЩ…ШұЪ©ШІ ШЁШұ Щ…ШҜЩ„ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜШӘШұ Ш®ЩҲШҜ ШіШ№ЫҢ ШҜШұ ЩҒШұЩҲШҙ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ШҜЩ„ ШЁШ§ ШӘШ®ЩҒЫҢЩҒ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: The 32-inch Asus ROG Swift QD-OLED 4K gaming monitor is currently on sale for $899.99, a $400 discount. With its impressive image quality and a 240Hz refresh rate, it is well-suited for fast-paced gaming. Asus is likely discounting this model to shift inventory and focus on its newer model.
insightsFa: Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШӘЩҲШұ ШЁШ§ ЩҶШұШ® ЩҶЩҲШіШ§ШІЫҢ ЫІЫҙЫ° ЩҮШұШӘШІ ЩҲ Ъ©ЫҢЩҒЫҢШӘ ЫҙK Щ…ЩҶШ§ШіШЁ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШіШұЫҢШ№ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШӯШіЪҜШұ ЩҶШІШҜЫҢЪ©ЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ШұЫҢШіЪ© ШіЩҲШ®ШӘЪҜЫҢ ШӘШөЩҲЫҢШұ Ш§ШІ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЪҜЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШӯШөШұ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШұШҜ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШӘЩҲШұ Ш§ШіШӘ.

ЩҫЩҲШұШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӘЩҶЩҲШ№ Ш§ШІ Ш¬Щ…Щ„ЩҮ DisplayPort 2.1 ЩҲ HDMI 2.1 ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШӘШөШ§Щ„ ШұШ§ШӯШӘвҖҢШӘШұ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ ШҜШ§ШұШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЫҢШӘЩҲШұ ШҜШ§ШұШ§ЫҢ ЪҜШ§ШұШ§ЩҶШӘЫҢ ШіЩҮ ШіШ§Щ„ЩҮ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш¬Щ„ЩҲЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШіЩҲШ®ШӘЪҜЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: The cost of saying yes has changed
articles.content: The most expensive part of a small feature request used to be writing the code. Now it’s usually the meeting about whether or not to write the code. That’s a real shift, and it quietly breaks a lot of engineering instincts. Engineers learn early that most “small asks” aren’t small: they need tests, a rollout plan, someone to think through the edge cases and own the behavior after it ships. A two-hour change can become a two-week distraction if it touches the wrong part of the system. So we push back. Is this really needed? Does it belong in this release? Does it change a contract we already agreed to? I’m not giving that instinct up. But it rests on an assumption that’s quietly breaking, which is that writing the first version of the code is the expensive step. For a specific class of change, it no longer is. If you can tell those changes apart from the rest, you can replace “is this in scope?” with a question you can answer in thirty minutes instead of a two-day debate. The debate often costs more than the patch Here’s a pattern I keep seeing. Someone asks for a small change such as surfacing a last_active_at timestamp that already exists in the backend on a settings page. The team spends forty minutes in a thread. One person says it sounds risky. Someone remembers a related migration from two years ago. Someone mentions the deadline. Eventually we land on “probably a day or two, could be more,” with low confidence, primarily because nobody has actually tried it. That process made sense when trying was the expensive part. You had to stop what you were doing, load the context into your head, make the change by hand, write the tests, then discover the second- and third-order consequences. When the first attempt is cheap, defending the boundary can cost more than crossing it. An agent can produce that first patch in the time the thread takes to warm up. It’s not free and definitely not automatically correct. But it is cheap enough that the smart move is often to stop guessing and look at a real diff. The first patch is a price check, not the product The mistake is to treat the generated patch as the deliverable. It isn’t. It’s a probe. It turns an abstract scope argument into a concrete artifact you can interrogate: Does it touch the files you expected, or does it sprawl across five packages? Are the tests obvious, or does the change resist being tested? Does it preserve the existing abstractions? Does it quietly require a new product decision? Would you be comfortable owning this behavior six months from now? Those are better questions than “does this feel like scope creep?” because now you’re arguing from evidence instead of vibes. If the last_active_at field comes back as a four-line diff with a passing test, ship it. The debate was the expensive part. However, if that same request comes back touching the auth middleware, you’ve learned the request was never small. Not only that, you learned this in thirty minutes instead of two days. This is not letting the AI decide. It’s using the AI to make human judgment cheaper and better-informed. Cheap to write is not the same as cheap to own Here’s the trap, and it’s the most important distinction of the AI era. A change is not cheap just because the code was cheap to generate. It’s cheap only if a human can confidently review and own the result. A thousand-line diff that technically passes but nobody wants to own is not a cheap change. It’s a deferred cost. So the dividing line in that case isn’t “can an agent write this?” It’s “can a person validate it?” Adding a display field that already exists in the backend is usually cheap. Changing authorization behavior is not cheap, no matter how clean the diff. Refactoring a well-tested helper is usually cheap. Changing data-retention semantics is not cheap.вҖҰ
articles.author: Dalia Abuadas

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ ШҜШұ ЩҒШұШўЫҢЩҶШҜ Ъ©Ш§ШұЫҢ Щ…ЩҮЩҶШҜШіШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҶШӘЫҢШ¬ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Ш§ШІ ШұЩҲЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЫҢ ШәЫҢШұЩ…ЩҶШ§ШіШЁ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶЪ© Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ШӘЩ„Ш§ЩҒ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ШҙЩҲШҜ ЩҲ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ЩҶШіШЁШӘ ШЁЩҮ Ш®ЩҲШҜ Ъ©ШҜЩҶЩҲЫҢШіЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩҶШЁШ§Щ„ ШҜШ§ШҙШӘЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: Currently, the most significant cost associated with small feature requests is often the meetings and discussions that decide on those changes rather than the actual code writing. This shift in working processes for engineers reveals a new and potentially inefficient approach to small requests, which can result in wasted time and higher costs than the coding itself.
insightsFa: ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶЪ© Щ…Ш№Щ…ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШЁШӯШ« ЩҲ Щ…ШҙШ§Ш¬ШұЩҮ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ.
Ъ©Ш§ШұШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ ЩҶЩҲШҙШӘЩҶ Ъ©ШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢШЁШұ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҶ Ш¬Щ„ШіШ§ШӘ ШӘШөЩ…ЫҢЩ…вҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ЩҒШұШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШҜЫҢЩ…ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜЪ©Ш§Шұ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ШӘЩ„Ш§ЩҒ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШЁШ№ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.
ШЁШ§ШІЩҶЪҜШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ЩҶШӯЩҲЩҮ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ ШҜШұШ®ЩҲШ§ШіШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҲЪҶЪ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШЁЩҮ ШөШұЩҒЩҮвҖҢШ¬ЩҲЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШЁШІШ§ШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ ШҜШұ ШӘШіШұЫҢШ№ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұШ§ШӘ Ъ©Щ…Ъ©вҖҢ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§ШҙШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұ ШЁЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҜЩҮШҜ Ъ©ЩҮ ЪҶЪҜЩҲЩҶЩҮ ЩҒШұШўЫҢЩҶШҜЩҮШ§ ЩҲ ШұЩҲЫҢЪ©ШұШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҶЩҲЫҢЩҶ ШҜШұ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҶШұЩ…вҖҢШ§ЩҒШІШ§Шұ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ШЁЩҮШұЩҮвҖҢЩҲШұЫҢ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ЩҮШҙ ЩҮШІЫҢЩҶЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ. ШҜШұ ШҜЩҶЫҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ…ШұЩҲШІШҢ Щ…ШҜЫҢШұЫҢШӘ Ъ©Ш§ШұШўЩ…ШҜ ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШЁШ№ Ш§ШІ Ш§ЩҮЩ…ЫҢШӘ ЩҲЫҢЪҳЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁШұШ®ЩҲШұШҜШ§Шұ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: The Real AI Threat Is Blind Trust
articles.content: AI models left to both interpret and execute commands eliminate critical cybersecurity oversight.
articles.author: R. Justin Martin
articles.fingerprint: blind real threat trust
articles.titleFa: ШӘЩҮШҜЫҢШҜ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШ№ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢШҢ Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҲШұ Ш§ШіШӘ
articles.summaryFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш§Ш¬Ш§ШІЩҮ ШҜШ§ШұЩҶШҜ ШҜШіШӘЩҲШұШ§ШӘ ШұШ§ ШӘЩҒШіЫҢШұ ЩҲ Ш§Ш¬ШұШ§ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜШҢ ЩҶШёШ§ШұШӘ ШӯЫҢШ§ШӘЫҢ ШЁШұ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШіШ§ЫҢШЁШұЫҢ ШұШ§ Ш§ШІ ШЁЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШЁШұЩҶШҜ. Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ЫҢШ¬Ш§ШҜ ШӘЩҮШҜЫҢШҜШ§ШӘ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘ ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҲ Ъ©Ш§ШұШЁШұШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.
articles.summaryEn: AI models that are allowed to interpret and execute commands compromise critical cybersecurity oversight. This poses serious threats to organizational and user security.
insightsFa: Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…Щ…Ъ©ЩҶ Ш§ШіШӘ ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ ЩҶШёШ§ШұШӘ Ш§ЩҶШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ЩӮШҜШ§Щ… Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
Ш§Ш№ШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜ Ъ©ЩҲШұ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШЁЩҮ Ш®Ш·ШұШ§ШӘ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢ ШҙЩҲШҜ.
ШЁЩҮШЁЩҲШҜ ЩҶШёШ§ШұШӘ ШЁШұ ЩҒШ№Ш§Щ„ЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш¶ШұЩҲШұЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
ШіШ§ШІЩ…Ш§ЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁШ§ЫҢШҜ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШӯШ§ЩҒШёШӘЫҢ ШЁШұ Ш§ШіШ§Ші ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШұШ§ ШЁШ§ ШҜЩӮШӘ ШЁШұШұШіЫҢ Ъ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ.
ШўЪҜШ§ЩҮЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШӘЩҮШҜЫҢШҜШ§ШӘ ЩҶШ§ШҙЫҢ Ш§ШІ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ ЩҶШ§ШҜШұШіШӘ Ш§ШІ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ ШЁШіЫҢШ§Шұ Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘ.

ШЁЩҶШ§ШЁШұШ§ЫҢЩҶШҢ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШҜШ§ШЁЫҢШұ ЩҶШёШ§ШұШӘЫҢ ЩӮЩҲЫҢвҖҢШӘШұ Ш§ШӯШіШ§Ші Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.

articles.title: Will Russia's answer to the Falcon 9 rocket ever take flight?
articles.content: Everyone seems to be launching and landing rockets these days. Last week, China joined the club of countries that have launched an orbital mission and brought its booster safely back to Earth, which is just the beginning of public and private ventures in that country aggressively pushing into rocket reuse. Also in Asia, Japan's space agency has been conducting hop tests , and Honda recently performed vertical reuse tests . In the United States, of course, SpaceX launches and lands reusable rockets every few days. Blue Origin, although its New Glenn booster is temporarily grounded, has also demonstrated the ability to both land and re-launch a large orbital booster. Other US companies, including Stoke Space, Rocket Lab, and Relativity Space, are all making credible progress toward partially or fully reusable rockets. Read full article Comments
articles.author: Eric Berger
articles.fingerprint: answer ever falcon flight rocket russia take
articles.titleFa: ШўЫҢШ§ ЩҫШ§ШіШ® ШұЩҲШіЫҢЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘ ЩҒШ§Щ„Ъ©ЩҲЩҶ 9 ШЁЩҮ ЩҫШұЩҲШ§ШІ ШҜШұЩ…ЫҢвҖҢШўЫҢШҜШҹ
articles.summaryFa: ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҮ Ъ©ШҙЩҲШұЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ЩҲ ЪҳШ§ЩҫЩҶ ШЁШ§ ЩҫШұЩҲЪҳЩҮвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШұШӘШ§ШЁ ЩҲ ЩҒШұЩҲШҜ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜШҢ ЩҲШ§ШұШҜ ШұЩӮШ§ШЁШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҙШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШҢ ШЁШіЫҢШ§ШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ Ш§ШұШІЫҢШ§ШЁЫҢ ЪҶШҙЩ…вҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШІ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШұЩҲШіЫҢЩҮ ЩҮШіШӘЩҶШҜ. ШўЩ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ ЩҶЫҢШІ ШЁШ§ ЩҫЫҢШҙШұЩҒШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ШіШӘЩ…Шұ SpaceX ЩҲ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш¬ШҜШҜШҢ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШӯЩҲЩ„Ш§ШӘЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮЫҢ ШҜШіШӘ ЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
articles.summaryEn: As countries like China and Japan enter the space race with their launch and landing systems, many are evaluating the potential of new Russian rockets. The U.S. is also experiencing significant advancements in reusable rockets through continuous achievements from SpaceX and other companies.
insightsFa: ЪҶЫҢЩҶ ЩҲ ЪҳШ§ЩҫЩҶ ШЁЩҮ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ Ъ©ШҙЩҲШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШұШӘШ§ШЁвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЩҮ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘ ЩҫЫҢЩҲШіШӘЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
SpaceX ЩҲ ШҜЫҢЪҜШұ ШҙШұЪ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШўЩ…ШұЫҢЪ©Ш§ЫҢЫҢ ШҜШұ ШІЩ…ЫҢЩҶЩҮ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш¬ШҜШҜ Щ…ЩҲЩҒЩӮЫҢШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӘШ§ШІЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШҜШіШӘ ШўЩҲШұШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ ШұШ§Ъ©ШӘвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢШ§ЩҒШӘ ШҜШұ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШұШҙШҜ ЩҲ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ШҜШұ ШіШ·Шӯ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ЩҶЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Meta in Talks to Sell Computing Power to Anthropic
articles.content: Meta Platforms Inc. is in early discussions to lease computing power from its data centers to Anthropic PBC, according to people familiar with the matter, an opportunity for the social media giant to build a new business around its heavy investment in AI infrastructure.

articles.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
articles.content: Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine. Expel, which shared technical details of the event, described the threat actor as a sub-group of GoldenEyeDog (aka APT-Q-27, Dragon Breath, and Miuuti Group), a Chinese cybercrime group known for its targeting of the gambling and gaming sectors using
articles.author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЪҜШұЩҲЩҮ Ъ©ЩҮ ШІЫҢШұЩ…Ш¬Щ…ЩҲШ№ЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШІ GoldenEyeDog Ш§ШіШӘШҢ ШЁЩҮ Ш®Ш§Ш·Шұ ЩҮШҜЩҒвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШөЩҶШ§ЫҢШ№ ЩӮЩ…Ш§Шұ ЩҲ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢЩҲЫҢЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§Ш®ШӘЩҮ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ЪҜШұЩҲЩҮ ШЁЩҮ ЩҮШҜЩҒвҖҢЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ ШөЩҶШ§ЫҢШ№ ЩӮЩ…Ш§Шұ ЩҲ ШЁШ§ШІЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҲЫҢШҜЫҢЩҲЫҢЫҢ Щ…Ш№ШұЩҲЩҒ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Billing software error sends billion-dollar AWS estimates
articles.content: Your AWS billing estimate might look just a little inflated right now. If you woke up to find an email from Amazon Web Services this morning telling you that youвҖҷd gone over your billing threshold by a few hundred million dollars, donвҖҷt panic: SomethingвҖҷs gone wrong in the AWS Billing Console, the company admitted. An open issue on the AWS Health Dashboard (archived copy at the time of writing) popped up at 1:33 am Pacific time on Friday informing users that Cost Explorer was вҖңreflecting inaccurate estimated billing data.вҖқ As of writing, the issue is still unresolved despite AWS trying several different things to get it fixed. The company apparently identified the root cause within an hour and a half of beginning its investigation, only describing it as вҖңan issue with unit pricing within the estimated billing computation subsystem.вҖқ AWS followed up by pausing estimated bill updates, saying customers would continue to see the inflated figures already displayed, but that those estimates would not increase further. вҖңThe displayed billing estimates do not reflect actual usage and charges,вҖқ AWS explained, noting that customers donвҖҷt need to take any action, like, we imagine, flooding the help portal with tickets telling them what they already know, for instance. вҖңOnce the issue has been mitigated, we expect full resolution to take multiple hours as we work through recomputing the estimated billing data,вҖқ AWS added. After we first published this article, Amazon updated the issue page to indicate that it had identified the root cause and mitigated the underlying issue. The company says that it's begun backfilling data in the Cost Management Console to correct billing numbers, and that all customers should see corrected amounts by Saturday, July 18 at noon pacific time. We owe HOW much? Users took to Reddit and Hacker News this morning to report theyвҖҷd received overage emails for massive amounts - we werenвҖҷt exaggerating with that hundreds of millions opening line. If anything, it was an understatement. Screenshots posted in the Reddit thread showed one user whose AWS charges totaled just $0.19 last month receiving an estimated bill of nearly $2.5 billion. Others in the thread claimed to have received estimated monthly charges ranging from $126,000 to as much as $2.5 trillion. Hacker News users similarly reported estimates in the billions. Amazon said the figures shown in customers' accounts were inaccurate estimates rather than actual charges. As for when users might see their billing portal reflect an accurate number, that could take a while. AWS declined to explain the issue aside from pointing us to the dashboard page linked above. We'll be keeping an eye on this developing story and update it as we learn more. В® Updated at 1903 to show that Amazon has updated its issue page with a resolution.

ШўЩ…Ш§ШІЩҲЩҶ ЩҲШЁ ШіШұЩҲЫҢШіШІ ШЁЩҮ Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШ№ Ш§Ш°Ш№Ш§ЩҶ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ ЩҲ Ш§Ш№Щ„Ш§Щ… Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ Ъ©ЩҮ Ш®Ш·Ш§ЫҢ Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШ· ШЁЩҮ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁШ§ШӘ ЩӮЫҢЩ…ШӘЫҢ ШҜШұ ШіШ§Щ…Ш§ЩҶЩҮ Щ…ШӯШ§ШіШЁЩҮ ШөЩҲШұШӘШӯШіШ§ШЁ ШӘШ®Щ…ЫҢЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҲШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.

articles.title: Dell XPS 16 review: A sleek, high-end laptop perfect for creatives and professionals

Щ…ЩҶШ§ШіШЁ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш®Щ„Ш§ЩӮШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШӯШұЩҒЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§.
ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘШұЫҢЩҶ ШіШ·Шӯ Ъ©ЫҢЩҒЫҢШӘ ШҜШұ Щ„ЩҫвҖҢШӘШ§ЩҫвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…ЩҲШ¬ЩҲШҜ.
articles.whyItMattersFa: Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Щ„ЩҫвҖҢШӘШ§Щҫ ШЁЩҮ ШҜЩ„ЫҢЩ„ Ш·ШұШ§ШӯЫҢ ЩҲ ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩҲШұЫҢ ШЁШ§Щ„Ш§ЫҢ Ш®ЩҲШҜШҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШҜ Ш§ШіШӘШ§ЩҶШҜШ§ШұШҜЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜЫҢ ШұШ§ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Щ„ЩҫвҖҢШӘШ§ЩҫвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҫШұЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩҲЩ… ШӘШ№ЫҢЫҢЩҶ Ъ©ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Ш§ЩҶШӘШ®Ш§ШЁвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ Ш®Щ„Ш§ЩӮШ§ЩҶ ЩҲ ШӯШұЩҒЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҮЩ… ШўЩҲШұШҜ.

articles.title: How Smartsheet built a remote MCP server on AWS
articles.content: Smartsheet is an enterprise work management platform that hundreds of thousands of organizations rely on. As enterprise teams adopt AI agents, those agents need structured access to the data inside systems like Smartsheet, but most systems arenвҖҷt built for that. To bridge this gap, Smartsheet built a remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server on AWS that gives AI clients direct access to its data and capabilities. AI assistants like Amazon Quick and Claude Desktop help users interact with SmartsheetвҖҷs capabilities through natural language, analyzing project data, updating tasks, creating sheets, managing workspaces, and more. Enterprises are also building custom AI agents for workloads that run without human prompting. These AI agents can work autonomously in their roles, coordinating through Smartsheet using MCP. A few examples are capturing requirements, picking up tasks, attaching test results, drafting documentation. These happen in the same sheets their human counterparts use, compressing workflows that took weeks into days or hours. The MCP server connects to SmartsheetвҖҷs existing APIs and central intelligence layer. It also adds an AI-optimized interface on top, designed to minimize token cost, help prevent hallucination, and help large language models (LLMs) work reliably with enterprise data. Since launch, Smartsheet saved over 3 billion tokens, based on internal telemetry, through these optimizations. In this post, we cover a high-level view of the Smartsheet remote MCP architecture, with a focus on the AWS infrastructure behind it. This includes security, governance, scaling and deployment, and the AI-specific optimizations Smartsheet built on AWS. Architecture One MCP layer serves both internal and external agents. SmartsheetвҖҷs own Smart Assist (the in-product AI experience) and externally connected AI clients like Amazon Quick run on the same infrastructure, with the same tools, optimizations, and intelligence stack. This parity is a deliberate architectural choice: Smartsheet builds once and every agentic client benefits immediately. The architecturally critical AWS services in the data path are: AWS Fargate for Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) for stateless server containers. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink for change-event ingestion into Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3) . Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Neptune for LLM inference and knowledge graph powering cross-project insights. The detailed architecture flow is as follows: AI clients to API gateway layer to MCP Server : Requests pass through an API gateway layer ( AWS WAF , AWS Shield , AWS Application Load Balancer, and OAuth validation) before reaching the MCP server on AWS Fargate. MCP Server to Domain Services : The MCP server calls SmartsheetвҖҷs domain services through their APIs for transactional operations. MCP Server to Intelligence Layer : The MCP server queries the Intelligence Layer built on Amazon Neptune and Databricks for cross-project agentic insights. Domain Services to Intelligence Layer : Change events stream through Amazon Kinesis and Apache Flink into the Amazon S3-backed intelligence layer. The Intelligence Layer follows the medallion architecture. Figure 1: Smartsheet MCP Server architecture on AWS. Supporting services such as edge protection, container registry, observability, secrets appear in the relevant sections in the following sections. Deployment and scaling On the scaling side, AI traffic differs from conventional request patterns. Agents autonomously orchestrate sequences of tool calls, firing several requests in a second as they work through a task, then going quiet while the model reasons. This bursty pattern demands a scaling strategy that responds to both sudden spikes and sustained throughput. To handle and validate this pattern, Smartsheet built the MCP server to run on AWS Fargate for Amazon ECS. ECS Auto Scaling uses target-tracking policies, combining trafficвҖҰ

articles.title: Upgrade your WFH setup: 6 ProtoArc ergonomic keyboard deals to save your wrists and your wallet
articles.content: If you work at a computer all day long then you'll know just how much strain can be put on your wrists. One good way to beat that is by using an ergonomic keyboard. These are designed to align with your body to reduce the risk of RSI and muscle strain. One of the brands leading the way in keyboard design right now is ProtoArc, which has built a strong reputation by designing keyboards, mice, and other peripherals focused on both ergonomics and portability. The six keyboards we've picked out here combine functionality with great design and good value. They're also all on sale at Amazon right now, so you can make some great savings. articles.title: Fubo hikes prices by $15 after restoring some NBCU channels lost in November
articles.content: Fubo prices are going up by $15 per month because it will have some NBCUniversal channels again. For years, Fubo, a sports-centric vMVPD (virtual multichannel video programming distributor, which lets subscribers watch traditional TV channels live over the Internet), offered NBCUniversal channels. That stopped in November 2025 due to a contract dispute. With the loss of local NBC affiliates, Telemundo, nine regional sports channels, and 32 national channels, Fubo made the sensible but rare decision to lower subscription prices in December . The Essential plan went from $85 per month to $74 per month. The Pro plan dropped from $85 to $75 per month, and the Elite plan dropped from $95 to $84 per month. Read full article Comments

articles.title: MuskвҖҷs SpaceX Starship launch suddenly aborted in what would have been its 13th flight
articles.content: SpaceX’s mega Starship rocket came within a second or so from blasting off on a test flight Thursday, but some of the engines failed to ignite, triggering a launch abort amid billowing clouds of smoke and vapor. Elon Musk , the company’s founder and CEO, said two engines will be replaced “to be confident of a good flight” before sending Starship from Texas on a space-skimming journey halfway around the world. It will be the 13th flight for Starship, which at 407 feet (124 meters) tall with 33 main engines is the world’s biggest and most powerful rocket. SpaceX’s launch webcast showed the start of engine ignition three seconds before the planned liftoff, viewed from a drone high above the pad. Although the company did not elaborate, onscreen data showed four engines not firing, with the remaining 29 engines immediately shutting down and keeping the rocket anchored to the pad. It was the first time a full-scale Starship experienced a last-second abort like this. The launch team immediately began draining the fuel from the rocket. “Most probable launch timing is early next week,” Musk said via X. Everything was going SpaceX’s way, even the weather, until the partial engine ignition. In the end, the rocket’s automatic launch system worked as planned by halting everything. Too few operating engines could have doomed the launch. Some earlier Starship flights ended in explosive fireballs. Elon Musk’s company had newest, most advanced Starlinks aboard Twenty of SpaceX’s newest and most advanced Starlinks were on board Starship for release during the planned hourlong flight from Starbase, the company’s hub near the Texas-Mexico border. The internet satellites were going to try communicating with Starlinks already in orbit while taking photos of Starship’s heat shield. Neither the first-stage booster nor spacecraft were meant to be recovered, with both ending up in the sea. The rocket’s automatic launch system worked as planned by halting everything. Too few operating engines could have resulted in a failed launch. Some earlier Starship flights, for example, ended in explosive fireballs. World’s biggest rocket is key to putting astronauts back on the moon NASA is counting on Starship to land its astronauts on the moon in the next few years. The space agency has hired SpaceX and Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin to build and fly the lunar landers that will return humanity to the surface of the moon after an absence of more than half a century. Both companies need to have their landers вҖ” Starship and Blue Moon вҖ” ready to fly by next year so that the newly named Artemis III crew can practice docking their capsule with them in orbit around Earth. The mission after that вҖ” Artemis IV planned for no earlier than 2028 вҖ” would use one of those landers to take two astronauts to the moon’s south polar region. The Associated Press Health and Science Department receives support from the Howard Hughes Medical Institute’s Department of Science Education and the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation. The AP is solely responsible for all content. вҖ”Marcia Dunn, AP Aerospace Writer

articles.title: AI spam filters are getting suckered by old-school text salting
articles.content: Notice more spam getting through that corporate email filter lately? Attackers are using a technique known as "text salting," which hides benign-looking words intended to confuse some AI-powered email filters, says cybersecurity firm Barracuda. The email security outfit said on Thursday that it had detected more than one million retail-themed phishing attacks using text salting since April. ItвҖҷs not a new technique by any stretch and has been used to fool traditional secure email gateways for years, but Barracuda says it can also confuse machine-learning and LLM-based security tools. Text salting involves peppering (sorry) a malicious email with random, harmless-seeming words in order to fool an email scanning system into thinking thereвҖҷs nothing off about the flavor of a message (sorry again), tricking the system into passing it to its recipient for consumption (IвҖҷll stop with the food jokes here). Pour a pile of salty text on top of an email and a human reader would probably get suspicious, however, so attackers typically use one or more of three flavor variations (okay, I'm done вҖ“ promise) to hide the additives from human readers, but not automated scanners, per Barracuda. Typical techniques include CSS cropping, which sets the visible window small enough that a human won't see the hidden filler text; text manipulation to move the salty copy outside the visible screen; and zero font techniques which insert misleading words between suspicious phishing copy thatвҖҷs visible to a machine but not a human. The end result of each of those techniques is a message that reads less malicious, more gibberish to a machine, leading it to assume the email is fine, and which looks exactly as the attacker intended when viewed by a human. Modern email security systems have largely adapted to these techniques, with newer tools able to remove hidden text to see what a reader is supposed to see, sounding alarms when a lot of hidden stuff is inserted in an email, and the like. AI, however, hasnвҖҷt managed to follow suit, says Barracuda. вҖңText salting and related techniques can be used to confuse AI-driven content analysis engines by flooding the email with random terms that encourage the AI system into making an incorrect classification decision,вҖқ the company wrote in its report - just like those early 2000s SEGs. What a technological leap weвҖҷve made! LLMs, Barracuda explained, are typically designed to process email text and source code plainly, with no understanding of whether text is visible or hidden from a user. They can be trained to do so, but that just means most tools probably arenвҖҷt doing that by default. So, what can enterprises do to stop the flow of salty spam to their employees? Barracuda recommends a layered approach to email security rather than relying solely on keyword detection, including checking sender reputation, authentication results, embedded URLs, HTML-rendering techniques, and differences between user-visible and hidden content. Ditching that AI spam filter might not be a bad idea, either. В®

articles.summaryEn: Email spam is increasingly bypassing corporate filters, with attackers utilizing a technique known as 'text salting' to confuse AI-powered email filters. This approach involves embedding benign-looking words in malicious emails to trick scanning systems into thinking the messages are safe, thus increasing the success rate of phishing attacks.
insightsFa: ШӘШ№ШҜШ§ШҜ ШЁЫҢШҙ Ш§ШІ ЫҢЪ© Щ…ЫҢЩ„ЫҢЩҲЩҶ ШӯЩ…Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЫҢШҙЫҢЩҶЪҜ ШЁШ§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢЪҶШұШ®Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҙШҜЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢЪҶШұШ®Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ЩҒЫҢЩ„ШӘШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§ЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩ„ ЩҲ ШіЫҢШіШӘЩ…вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЫҢШ§ШҜЪҜЫҢШұЫҢ Щ…Ш§ШҙЫҢЩҶ ШўШіЫҢШЁ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШІЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ШіШӘЩҒШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§ШІ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ©вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢЫҢ ЪҶЩҲЩҶ ШЁШұШҙ CSS ЩҲ ЩҒЩҲЩҶШӘ ШөЩҒШұ ШЁШұШ§ЫҢ ЩҫЩҶЩҮШ§ЩҶвҖҢШіШ§ШІЫҢ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш®ШұШЁ.
ЩҒЫҢЩ„ШӘШұЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш§Щ…ЩҶЫҢШӘЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶШ§ЫҢЫҢ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Щ…Ш®ЩҒЫҢ ШұШ§ ЩҫЫҢШҜШ§ Ъ©ШұШҜЩҮвҖҢШ§ЩҶШҜ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ© Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ ШЁШ§Ш№Ш« Ш§ЩҒШІШ§ЫҢШҙ ЩҶЪҜШұШ§ЩҶвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜЩҮ ШҜШұ ШӘШ№ШҜШ§ШҜ Ш§ЫҢЩ…ЫҢЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШәЫҢШұЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШҙЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЫҢЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШҙЩҲШҜ.

ШӘЪ©ЩҶЫҢЪ©вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш¬ШҜЫҢШҜ Щ…Ш§ЩҶЩҶШҜ Щ…ШӘЩҶвҖҢЪҶШұШ®Ш§ЩҶЫҢ ШЁЩҮ ШӘЩҮШҜЫҢШҜШ§ШӘ ШіШ§ЫҢШЁШұЫҢ Ъ©Щ…Ъ© Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶШҜ ЩҲ ЩҶЫҢШ§ШІ ШЁЩҮ ШЁЩҮвҖҢШұЩҲШІШұШіШ§ЩҶЫҢ Щ…ШіШӘЩ…Шұ ШұЩҲШҙвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШӯЩҒШ§ШёШӘЫҢ ШұШ§ Ш¶ШұЩҲШұЫҢ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШіШ§ШІШҜ.

articles.title: San Francisco orders Apple, Google to remove nudify apps from app stores
articles.content: This week, San FranciscoвҖҷs attorney general, David Chiu, sent cease-and-desist letters, demanding that Apple and Google remove 13 so-called nudification apps from their app stores, Wired reported. Nudification apps can make it trivially easy to transform ordinary photos of real people into explicit images. The harmful AI tools allow bad actors to remove clothing, change a personвҖҷs features, place them in sexualized positions, and swap victimsвҖҷ faces onto other peopleвҖҷs naked bodies. Chiu's letter warned that app stores were violating "CaliforniaвҖҷs laws that prohibit supporting services that create deepfake pornography," Wired reported. Read full article Comments
articles.author: Ashley Belanger

Ш§ЫҢЩҶ Ш§ЩҫЩ„ЫҢЪ©ЫҢШҙЩҶвҖҢЩҮШ§ ШЁЩҮ ШұШ§ШӯШӘЫҢ Ш§Щ…Ъ©Ш§ЩҶ ШӘШәЫҢЫҢШұ Ш№Ъ©ШівҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш№Ш§ШҜЫҢ Ш§ЩҒШұШ§ШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШӘШөШ§ЩҲЫҢШұ ШәЫҢШұЩ…Ш¬Ш§ШІ ШұШ§ ЩҒШұШ§ЩҮЩ… Щ…ЫҢвҖҢЪ©ЩҶЩҶШҜ ЩҲ Щ…ЫҢвҖҢШӘЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҶШҜ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШіШӘвҖҢЪ©Ш§ШұЫҢвҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ Ш®Ш·ШұЩҶШ§Ъ©ЫҢ Щ…ЩҶШ¬Шұ ШҙЩҲЩҶШҜ.

articles.title: Kimi K3 tops ArenaвҖҷs coding leaderboard вҖ” and itвҖҷs open-weight
articles.content: Developers building with AI have largely relied on proprietary models like Anthropic’s Fable and OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 Sol for their most demanding coding tasks. Moonshot AI’s latest release suggests open-weight models may be catching up faster than expected. The Chinese startup unveiled Kimi K3 this week, and within hours of its debut on Thursday, the open-weight model climbed to the top of Arena’s frontend coding leaderboard, outperforming leading closed-source systems in blind evaluations. If K3 lives up to its early benchmarks, developers may soon have another high performer they can run themselves rather than be tied to a proprietary API. If K3 lives up to its early benchmarks, developers may soon have another high performer they can run themselves rather than be tied to a proprietary API. Open-weight models gain ground Most AI coding tools can already connect to multiple models, but the toughest coding jobs still tend to end up on OpenAI or Anthropic models. If Kimi K3 can match that level of performance, it gives engineering teams another option to run in their own environment instead of sending every request to a third-party API. Open-weight models are becoming part of the conversation, and developers will expect their IDEs to support them alongside proprietary models. Open-weight models are becoming part of the conversation, and developers will expect their IDEs to support them alongside proprietary models. Arena results need verification Moonshot AI released Kimi K3 on Thursday, and developers quickly zeroed in on one result: it landed at the top of Arena’s frontend coding leaderboard. In Arena’s blind evaluations, K3 ranked ahead of Anthropic’s Opus 4.8 and OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 Sol on frontend coding tasks. It also performed well on Arena’s general text leaderboard, finishing above Opus 4.8 and roughly even with Sol. That’s an impressive debut, but it’s still just one benchmark. Like any new model release, the real test will come when developers start throwing production code workflows at it. Arena’s results are a strong first signal, but developers have only had a few hours to kick the tires, and the biggest test is still ahead. Moonshot hasn’t released Kimi K3’s weights yet, so no one can run it locally or benchmark it against their own repositories. That will change on July 27, when the company plans to publish the weights. Pricing defies expectations Kimi K3 is a 2.8 trillion-parameter mixture-of-experts model — activating 16 of 896 experts for compute efficiency — with a one-million-token context window and multimodal support, making it one of the largest open-weight models released so far. Maintaining that benchmark performance across massive repository scans and long-horizon agentic workloads could make it a serious option for teams building or deploying AI coding tools. K3 also breaks with the pricing strategy many developers have come to expect from Chinese models because Moonshot is charging closer to frontier-model prices. Rather than aggressively undercutting Western competitors, Moonshot has priced K3 at $3 per million input tokens and $15 per million output tokens (with cached inputs dropping to $0.30), a blended average of roughly $12 per million tokens that places it closer to premium frontier offerings than to earlier low-cost Chinese releases. That puts the focus squarely on performance. Once the weights are released, teams can run K3 in their own environment, connect it to their internal tooling, and see how it handles their code. IDEs must earn developers The release of Kimi K3 points to a broader shift in how engineering teams use AI. Developers want the freedom to swap systems depending on the job — using one model for frontend coding and a completely different one for full repository reviews. If IDE vendors can no longer rely on exclusive access to proprietary models to lock in users, they have toвҖҰ

ЩҶШӘШ§ЫҢШ¬ Ш§ЩҲЩ„ЫҢЩҮ K3 ШҜШұ ШЁШұШ§ШЁШұ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ШұЩӮШЁШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШөЩ„ЫҢ ЩӮШ§ШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘ.
Ш§ЫҢЩҶ ШӘЩҲШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҶШҙШ§ЩҶЩҮвҖҢШ§ЫҢ Ш§ШІ ШўШұШ§Щ…Шҙ ШЁЫҢШҙШӘШұЫҢ ШҜШұ ШЁШ§ШІШ§Шұ Щ…ШҜЩ„вҖҢЩҮШ§ЫҢ ЩҮЩҲШҙ Щ…ШөЩҶЩҲШ№ЫҢ Ш§ШіШӘ.
